jobs.title: Simulator Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Simulator Software Engineer - Sr Simulator Software Engineer to be filled at Clinton Nuclear Power Station in Clinton, IL. This is a multi-level opening that can be filled at the Simulator Software Engineer level or the Sr Software Engineer level. Please see the minimum qualifications for each level below. Who We Are As the nation's largest producer of clean, carbon-free energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: accelerating the transition to a carbon-free future. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things and help fight climate change. Join us to lead the clean energy future. Total Rewards Constellation offers a wide range of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, we offer a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including a robust wellness program; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Simulator Software Engineer - Expected salary range of $105,300 to $117,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Sr Simulator Software Engineer - Expected salary range of $125,100 to $139,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for creating and modifying simulation software logic and dynamic models used at a nuclear power plant in the Main Control Room Simulator, including simulator software maintenance, model minor plant modifications, and enhancements with minimal support. Functions independently and advises Constellation's Training Department on simulator technology. Coordinate simulator tasks and collaborate across teams as needed. Works closely with the training department, project managers, and Clean Energy Center (CEC) engineers to ensure compliance with project needs. The role is centered on the development and improvement of new and existing features. Additionally, the role involves reviewing peers' code, advocating for standard development methodologies, and participating in an inclusive, energetic, and highly cross-functional team. Expected to work efficiently, independently, and with minimal supervision. Primary Duties and Accountabilities CEC Main Control Room Simulator Projects: Participate in the planning, designing, building, or managing of simulator solutions. Participate in planning activities for assigned CEC. Create, modify, and document simulation software logic and dynamic models to meet business needs. Software configuration management. Participate in finding technology solutions by weighing advantages of technology trends, market availability of products, risks, and benefits of technology to meet business needs. Participate in identifying risks and issues that affect the projects. Provide second tier service to another simulator, as required, to backup that CEC Simulator Software Engineer with little or no assistance. Participates in projects for other CECs in the fleet as required. Engage with business partners to gather information, analyze requirements and deliver practical, efficient and cost-effective solutions that satisfy business needs. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Simulator Software Engineer - Job Code XSSD Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical or nuclear) or related science with 2 years of related experience (e.g. programming technologies) OR Associate's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical or nuclear) or related science with 4 years of related experience (e.g. programming technologies) Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Sr Simulator Software Engineer - Job Code XSSE Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical or nuclear) or related science with 5 years of related experience (e.g. programming technologies) OR Associate's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical or nuclear) or related science with 7 years of related experience (e.g. programming technologies) Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Simulator Software Engineer - Job Code XSSD Preferred Qualifications Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or nuclear engineering with 3+ years of engineering experience Proficient in FORTRAN and C programming languages Knowledge of graphic design software Experience with Visual Studio Experience with engineering print reading Experience leading small projects or sub-teams Experience with GIT configuration management Experience with installing, configuring, and maintaining Windows Operating Systems Familiarity with network topology Familiarity with creating software interface protocols Teamwork and self-motivation skills to achieve success in a rapidly evolving environment Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) for conveying complex findings to non-technical stakeholders Understanding of advanced programming concepts, data structures, and algorithms Sr Simulator Software Engineer - Job Code XSSE Preferred Qualifications Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or nuclear engineering with 5+ years of engineering experience Proficient in FORTRAN and C programming languages Knowledge of graphic design software Experience with Visual Studio Experience with engineering print reading Experience leading small projects or sub-teams Experience with GIT configuration management Experience with installing, configuring, and maintaining Windows Operating Systems Familiarity with network topology Familiarity with creating software interface protocols Teamwork and self-motivation skills to achieve success in a rapidly evolving environment Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) for conveying complex findings to non-technical stakeholders Understanding of advanced programming concepts, data structures, and algorithms

Functions independently and advises Constellation's Training Department on simulator technology. Coordinate simulator tasks and collaborate across teams as needed. structured_description.about: Constellation is seeking a Simulator Software Engineer to create and maintain simulation software logic and dynamic models for the Clinton Nuclear Power Station Main Control Room Simulator. This role offers the opportunity to work independently on simulator enhancements, support the training department, and collaborate across engineering teams. The position is available at both the Simulator Software Engineer and Sr. Simulator Software Engineer levels.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Participate in planning, designing, building, and managing simulator solutions for CEC Main Control Room projects
• Create, modify, and document simulation software logic and dynamic models to meet business needs
• Perform software configuration management and participate in technology solution assessments
• Review peers' code and advocate for standard development methodologies
• Identify risks and issues affecting projects and provide recommendations
• Provide backup support to other simulator software engineers with minimal assistance
• Engage with business partners to gather requirements and deliver cost-effective solutions
• Participate in projects across the fleet as required and support outage activities
• Perform emergency response and departmental coverage duties as directed
structured_description.qualifications: Simulator Software Engineer: Bachelor's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical, or nuclear) or related science with 2 years of related programming experience; OR Associate's degree in engineering or related science with 4 years of related programming experience. Sr. Simulator Software Engineer: Bachelor's degree in engineering (chemical, civil/structural, electrical, industrial, mechanical, or nuclear) or related science with 5 years of related programming experience; OR Associate's degree in engineering or related science with 7 years of related programming experience. All candidates must maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements as applicable and pass favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties.
structured_description.desired:
• Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or nuclear engineering
• Proficiency in FORTRAN and C programming languages
• Knowledge of graphic design software
• Experience with Visual Studio
• Experience with engineering print reading
• Experience leading small projects or sub-teams
• Experience with GIT configuration management
• Experience installing, configuring, and maintaining Windows Operating Systems
structured_description.location_details: Clinton, IL; onsite at Clinton Nuclear Power Station; includes support for outage activities and emergency response duties

jobs.title: Engineer, Sr Staff Fuels /  Project Manager
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $151,200 to $168,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Performs advanced fuels related problem solving in support of Clean Energy Center (CEC) operations. Responsible for technical decisions. Possesses superior knowledge in functional discipline and its practical application and regulations. Functions with recognized industry-level of expertise in a technical area. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Perform advanced nuclear engineering and technical tasks in support of CECs Perform independent research, reviews, studies and analyses in support of regulatory/technical projects and programs. Provides in-depth technical expertise to develop, manage, and implement nuclear engineering activities related to CEC safety, reliability, maintainability, and availability ensuring compliance to customer's needs, regulatory requirements, and corporate policies. Accountable for the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work ensuring proper configuration management and assuring that standard design criteria, practices and codes are used in preparation of plans and specifications. Provide technical expertise and consultation through direct involvement to identify and resolve equipment and system problems. Provides initial or complete task management of engineering issues. Recommend new concepts and techniques to improve performance, simplify operations, reduce costs or comply with changes in codes or regulations. Supports CEC design modifications, testing and problem solving, as necessary. Assure all engineering products prepared or overseen are in accordance with applicable safety analyses, industry codes, and all regulatory requirements. Develop, implement and coordinate functional supervisory activities including, but not limited to, personnel, budgetary accountabilities and planning goals with respect to Nuclear Fuels personnel. Possesses superior knowledge in functional discipline and its practical application and regulations. Functions with recognized industry-level of expertise in a technical area. Accountable for the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work ensuring proper configuration management and assuring that standard design criteria, practices and codes are used in preparation of plans and specifications. Recommend new concepts and techniques to improve performance, simplify operations, reduce costs or comply with changes in codes or regulations. Supports CEC design modifications, testing and problem solving, as necessary. Assure all engineering products prepared or overseen are in accordance with applicable safety analyses, industry codes, and all regulatory requirements. Develop, implement and coordinate functional supervisory activities including, but not limited to, personnel, budgetary accountabilities and planning goals with respect to Nuclear Fuels personnel. jobs.review_notes: Expanded vague 'about' section with specific context on fuels engineering focus and dual IC/supervisory scope. Consolidated 11 bullet responsibilities into 8 clearer statements focusing on nuclear-specific tasks (safety analyses, codes/standards compliance, regulatory work). All fields substantive and appropriate for senior nuclear fuels engineering role.

structured_description.about: Senior Staff Engineer and Project Manager at Constellation's Clean Energy Center, providing advanced nuclear fuels technical expertise and overseeing nuclear fuels engineering activities. This role combines individual contributor responsibilities for complex nuclear engineering problems with supervisory and project management duties for the Nuclear Fuels department. You will drive technical decisions on safety, reliability, and regulatory compliance while managing personnel and budgets.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Perform advanced nuclear engineering analyses and independent technical reviews for regulatory and operational projects
- Develop and manage nuclear engineering activities supporting safety, reliability, maintainability, and system availability
- Identify and resolve equipment/system problems through technical expertise; manage engineering issues from identification through resolution
- Verify all engineering products comply with safety analyses, industry codes (ASME, IEEE), and NRC regulatory requirements
- Support design modifications, testing activities, and outage problem-solving for CEC operations
- Recommend improvements to performance, cost reduction, and regulatory compliance strategies
- Supervise Nuclear Fuels personnel; manage budgets, planning goals, and personnel development
- Perform emergency response duties and outage support as assigned
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Chemical, Civil/Structural, Electrical, Industrial, Mechanical, or Nuclear Engineering
- 10+ years of nuclear industry or related engineering experience
- Demonstrated technical leadership (e.g., duty team lead, inspection lead roles)
- Ability to obtain/maintain unescorted access and pass favorable medical examination
structured_description.desired:
- Project Management Professional (PMP) certification
- Design Engineering Qualifications (DG01, DG03)
- Qualified Nuclear Engineer (QNE) qualification
- Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) certification
structured_description.location_details: Constellation Clean Energy Center

Salary starts at $24.39/hour This class is slated to start September 1st, 2026 Primary Purpose of Position Protect the health and welfare of the public by defending the station against any act of radiological sabotage, attack, or theft of special nuclear material. structured_description.about: Armed Security Officer Trainee for Constellation's nuclear facilities. Protect nuclear power stations against radiological threats, sabotage, and theft of special nuclear material. Conduct patrols, control access to security areas, monitor surveillance systems, and respond to contingency events as a member of the Armed Response Force. This is a federally regulated armed security position requiring extensive training and qualification to maintain nuclear unescorted access.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Conduct armed stationary, foot, and vehicle patrols (interior and exterior)
- Control access and egress of personnel, materials, and vehicles to security areas
- Perform search activities and inspections per Station Security Plan and post orders
- Monitor CCTV, plant surveillance equipment, and alarm systems
- Respond to threats and contingency events as member of Armed Response Force
- Perform equipment testing and maintenance
- Deter criminal activity, misconduct, and safety violations
- Maintain accurate documentation and record-keeping per federal and corporate guidelines
- Participate in initial and annual formal training to maintain armed Nuclear Security Officer qualifications
- Maintain thorough knowledge of plant systems to support site defensive strategy
- Perform emergency response duties, department coverage, and outage support as assigned
structured_description.qualifications:
- At least 21 years of age
- High School diploma or GED
- Ability to obtain and maintain nuclear unescorted access
- Meet minimum state requirements to carry a firearm
- Valid driver's license
- REAL ID or REAL ID-compliant ID per REAL ID ACT
- Pass state and federal required training for firearms licensing
- Pass drug and alcohol test with negative results (including THC/marijuana)
- Pass extensive background check including criminal history, references, employment/educational verification, and credit check
- Pass psychological assessment and physical exams (vision and hearing)
- Successfully complete physical fitness test, firearms qualification, and nuclear site-specific training
- Ability to operate radio, telephone equipment, console monitors, and surveillance equipment
- Ability to interact cordially and communicate with the public
- Ability to transit work location and conduct routine rounds (walk, climb stairs/ladders)
- Ability to access and egress Bullet Resistant Enclosure (BRE) towers
- Ability to manually carry, lift, or handle gear up to 25 lbs
- Body positioning ability (stand, sit, kneel, bend, squat, reach over shoulder height)
- Ability to work 12-hour shifts including nights, weekends, and forced overtime
structured_description.location_details: Onsite at Constellation nuclear power facility; 12-hour shifts including nights, weekends, and forced overtime; duties include access to elevated BRE towers and routine site patrols

We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Sr Environmental Specialist Expected salary range of $113,400 to $126,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for providing independent technical, regulatory, program support, and expertise on Constellation Nuclear Environmental compliance issues and projects in all relevant environmental areas. Serve as the program owner and administrator working with functional areas for assigned programs that directly affect the compliance status of the facility (e.g., Air Permits, DOT shipment of hazardous wastes, National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permits, hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste regulations, storage tanks, drinking water, Spill Prevention Control Countermeasure, Superfund Amendments Reauthorization Act (SARA)/Emergency Plan Community Right to Know Act (EPRA), International Standardization Organization/Environmental Management System, Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) chemical safety, Hazmat). Attends and presents at leadership meetings. Responsible for working with industry trade groups and regulatory agencies to increase knowledge base and influence programs, projects and regulations. Develops relationships with stakeholders and is recognized by peers in at least one program area as a subject matter expert. Works to develop expertise in additional program areas, as appropriate. Develops and provides environmental training to site personnel as needed. With the assistance of others, interacts with regulatory officials providing tours and obtains approvals, permits, certifications, and licenses relating to Environmental projects, pollution control systems, or waste management activities. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Responsible for providing technical guidance in assigned programs. Provides regulatory support for all environmental program areas as necessary. Maintains environmental contracts as assigned. Responsible to represent the site during environmental audits conducted by both internal and external organizations. React to and assist the Site and fleet in responding to emergent environmental issues. Manage environmental projects including development of scope, solicitation of proposals, proposal evaluation, soliciting funding, project timeline, budget and deliverables. Serve as technical expert to support and participate in representing Constellation Nuclear in meetings with regulatory agencies, engineering consultants, Legal Counsel (internal and external) and serve on technical committees as assigned. Reviews, evaluates and interprets new and existing environmental regulations and legislation pertaining to Constellation Nuclear operations and activities. Updates procedures to reflect regulatory requirements, as appropriate. Support and perform the evaluation of new technology, processes, and programs and recommend specific actions for Constellation Nuclear operations. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Sr Environmental Specialist Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Environmental or Chemical Engineering, Biology; Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geology or related program/equivalent with 6 years of experience working with environmental programs (i.e., Clean Air Act; Clean Water Act; Safe Drinking Water Act; SARA; Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA); and/or other environmental program areas) Minimum of 2 years of experience presenting issues and advocating positions Demonstrated knowledge of multi-media Federal and State Environmental statutes and regulations Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications Sr Environmental Specialist Experience in the nuclear utility industry highly preferred Experience with Environmental Regulations, Compliance, Plans and reports highly preferred. Master's degree or graduate work in engineering, sciences, law or business is preferred Professional certification or other recognition

jobs.title: Auxiliary Operator
jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. TOTAL REWARDS Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Starting pay rate $48.83/hr. (non-negotiable) This class will start in August 2026 PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION Regularly and customarily is responsible for the monitoring, control and operation of nuclear station equipment for the control room. This includes the following, together with other related and incidental work. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES Starts, stops, controls and monitors nuclear reactor and auxiliary reactor systems, as required for plant operations, including taking manual control of equipment as conditions warrant. Starts, stops, controls and monitors steam turbine, main generator and auxiliary turbine generating equipment as required to maintain maximum station capability, including taking manual control of equipment as conditions warrant. As directed, starts, stops, adjusts, tests and operates pumps, valves, switchgear, controls and other components of systems. Monitors recorders and indicators within the control room for the nuclear and turbine generator systems for operation within prescribed limits. Obtains overview data and performs various calculations as required by procedure. Responds to plant indicators and alarms in accordance with plant procedures including identifying those parameters that are indicative of an emergency condition and initiating designated actions. Performs operations in accordance with administrative procedures and NRC license including shift turnovers, log maintenance, etc. Responds to and takes action as required in mitigating consequences of plant transient. Required to be thoroughly familiar with entry conditions and cautions for the emergency operating procedure and the basis of each step in the procedures. Required to be familiar with the abnormal operating event procedures and system diagnostic and restoration procedures. Participates as a member of station fire brigade and will be required to function as fire brigade leader. Assists shift supervision in the performance of any and all of the duties involved in directing and coordinating the actions of the other personnel assigned to assist in operation of the station. Responsible to identify and correct deficient conditions through the use of the station Corrective Action program, initiating action requests for material condition issues and submitting Procedure change requests. Initiates Emergency Plan in accordance with implementing procedure. Implements tagging and system out-of-service requests, including directing others to realign valves, rack out breakers and hang tags. During refueling of the reactor, manipulates controls, drives, etc. and observes nuclear and radiation monitoring instruments in accordance with procedural controls. May be required to assist in refueling. Operates station communications equipment as required. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS High School diploma or GED Must pass the Plant Operator Selection System (POSS) and Basic Math and Science Test (BMST) testing Must be able to receive unescorted Nuclear access as a member of a critical group AND at least one of the following requirements below: 1 year of technical experience, which includes any of the following: work performed in power production, petrochemical or other industrial process plants during operational, maintenance, design, or training activities. Alternative relevant experience examples include operation and maintenance of equipment, such as HVAC, electrical and maintenance technicians. OR 2 years of experience in operations or maintenance of a non-generating nuclear power facility (e.g. Navy Nuclear operations or maintenance training/experience or operations or maintenance experience at a DOE facility) OR A Bachelor of Science degree in engineering, engineering technology, or related science program OR An Associates of Science degree in engineering, engineering technology or related science program OR 43 credit hours of post-secondary education of which 75% (at least 8 courses or 32 hours) is advanced math, physics, chemistry and/or engineering/engineering technology. Trainees must successfully complete a License Medical Screening and Fire Brigade Screening prior to introduction into the Non-Licensed Operator Training Program.

jobs.review_notes: Genuine nuclear reactor operations role. Enhanced 'about' field with career pathway context. Cleaned up formatting and corrected 'Able to receive' to 'Ability to receive'. All 16 responsibilities are substantive and NRC-compliant.

structured_description.about: Entry-level Auxiliary Operator role at a Constellation nuclear power station. You will monitor, control, and operate nuclear reactor and auxiliary systems from the control room, supporting licensed operators in maintaining safe, reliable plant operations. This is a shift-based position ideal for candidates with technical backgrounds seeking to launch a career in nuclear energy operations and potentially advance to Licensed Operator certification.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Start, stop, control and monitor nuclear reactor and auxiliary reactor systems as required for plant operations, including manual control of equipment as conditions warrant
- Start, stop, control and monitor steam turbine, main generator and auxiliary turbine generating equipment to maintain maximum station capability
- Start, stop, adjust, test and operate pumps, valves, switchgear, controls and other system components as directed
- Monitor recorders and indicators within the control room for nuclear and turbine generator systems to ensure operation within prescribed limits
- Obtain overview data and perform calculations as required by procedure
- Respond to plant indicators and alarms in accordance with plant procedures and identify emergency conditions
- Perform operations in accordance with administrative procedures and NRC license requirements, including shift turnovers and log maintenance
- Respond to and mitigate consequences of plant transients
- Maintain thorough familiarity with emergency operating procedures and abnormal operating event procedures
- Participate as member of station fire brigade and function as fire brigade leader as required
- Assist shift supervision in directing and coordinating personnel assigned to station operations
- Identify and correct deficient conditions through the station Corrective Action program
- Initiate Emergency Plan in accordance with implementing procedures
- Implement tagging and system out-of-service requests, including directing others to realign valves and rack out breakers
- Manipulate controls and observe nuclear and radiation monitoring instruments during reactor refueling operations
- Operate station communications equipment as required
structured_description.qualifications:
- High School diploma or GED
- Pass the Plant Operator Selection System (POSS) and Basic Math and Science Test (BMST)
- Ability to receive unescorted nuclear access as a member of a critical group
- At least 1 year of technical experience in power production, petrochemical or industrial process plants (operational, maintenance, design, or training activities), OR operation and maintenance of equipment such as HVAC, electrical and maintenance work
- OR 2 years of experience in operations or maintenance of a non-generating nuclear power facility (Navy Nuclear operations/maintenance or DOE facility experience)
- OR Bachelor of Science degree in engineering, engineering technology, or related science program
- OR Associates of Science degree in engineering, engineering technology or related science program
- OR 43 credit hours of post-secondary education with at least 75% (8 courses or 32 hours) in advanced math, physics, chemistry and/or engineering/engineering technology
- Successfully complete License Medical Screening and Fire Brigade Screening prior to entry into Non-Licensed Operator Training Program
structured_description.location_details: Full-time shift work, plant-based operations. Start date: August 2026. Starting pay rate: $48.83/hr.

structured_description.about: Supervisor of chemistry operations at a nuclear power plant under Constellation Energy. Direct oversight of field sampling, laboratory analysis, and radioanalytical programs supporting reactor operations. Responsible for supervising chemistry technicians, managing chemistry projects, ensuring regulatory and NRC compliance, and maintaining quality control of analytical measurements critical to plant safety and performance.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Supervise chemistry technicians in sampling and laboratory analysis; provide coaching on chemistry fundamentals, human performance, and procedure compliance
- Lead chemistry projects and troubleshooting activities impacting plant operations
- Schedule and execute all chemistry samples and analyses, including NRC-required regulatory samples
- Oversee lab instrument calibration, operation, and quality assurance while maintaining chemical hygiene standards
- Review Clean Energy Center work schedules to coordinate chemistry technician activities and prevent operational conflicts
- Manage technician scheduling, policies, and budgets
- Participate in Emergency Response Organization (ERO) rotation and Chemistry on-Call rotation; subject to emergency callout
- Provide ongoing coaching and mentoring to chemistry staff
structured_description.qualifications:
- High School diploma/GED with 9 years experience (3 years chemistry-related, 1 year at nuclear power plant, 0.25 years on-site); OR
- Associate's degree with 7 years experience (3 years chemistry-related, 1 year at nuclear power plant, 0.25 years on-site); OR
- Bachelor's degree with 5 years experience (3 years chemistry-related, 1 year at nuclear power plant, 0.25 years on-site)
- Meet ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 Standard (4.4.4) selection and qualification requirements for nuclear power plant personnel
- Maintain unescorted or escorted access badge requirements per NRC and facility policy
- Pass medical examination and any required testing per position duties (may include DOT physical)
structured_description.desired:
- Bachelor's degree in chemistry or chemical engineering with 5 years chemistry operational experience at an operating nuclear power plant
- Prior supervisory or lead technician experience
- Navy Enlisted Electrical Training (ELT) or nuclear power plant chemistry technician background
- ANSI Radiochemistry Supervisor qualifications (radiochemistry training certification plus 2 years gamma spectroscopy operations experience)
structured_description.location_details: On-site at nuclear power plant facility; subject to NRC emergency preparedness callout rotation and on-call requirements for chemistry coverage

structured_description.about: Instruments and Controls Electrical Engineer at Constellation's Byron nuclear plant in Illinois. This role supports reactor operations and safety systems through electrical engineering design, analysis, and technical support. Position levels available: Entry (BS Engineering), Mid (2+ yrs nuclear experience), Senior (5+ yrs nuclear experience). All work must comply with safety analyses, NRC regulations, and industry codes per ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Design, analyze, and maintain electrical systems supporting reactor instrumentation and control (I&C) systems in accordance with applicable safety analyses and NRC regulatory requirements
- Perform engineering calculations, drawings, and technical assessments for I&C electrical equipment and installations
- Support safety-related and non-safety electrical systems testing, commissioning, and troubleshooting
- Participate in outage planning and execution for I&C electrical maintenance and upgrades
- Support emergency preparedness activities and on-call response duties as assigned
- Ensure compliance with nuclear quality assurance (QA) requirements and regulatory oversight
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Nuclear Engineering, or related discipline
- For Mid-level: minimum 2 years nuclear industry or related engineering experience
- For Senior-level: minimum 5 years nuclear industry or related engineering experience
- Meet ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 qualification standards for nuclear power plant personnel
- Pass security clearance review and maintain unescorted access eligibility
- Pass medical examination appropriate to assigned duties (radiation exposure potential)
structured_description.desired:
- Engineer in Training (EIT) or Professional Engineer (PE) certification
- Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering (preferred over other engineering disciplines)
- Demonstrated experience designing and testing electrical systems in regulated environments
- Prior experience with instrumentation and controls systems, particularly in power generation
structured_description.location_details: Byron, IL (onsite position); includes emergency response and on-call duty rotation

Primary Purpose of Position Implements and supports the plans for the Constellation Nuclear Training technology strategy. Clean Energy Center (CEC) specialist in curricula development activities involving the implementation of Constellation's Systematic Approach to Training (SAT) model and procedural requirements for the analysis, design, development, implementation, evaluation that provides guidance to CEC instructors. Provides expertise in analyzing training problems, developing and recommending solutions relative to Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), Institute of Nuclear Power Operations (INPO), and the training department requirements to include the following areas: training program effectiveness and training staff development. Provides oversight of the self-assessment program for the training department. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Implements and supports the training technology strategy and provides oversight of the implementation of Constellation training efficiency improvements related to e-learning (computer and web-based training) and software/hardware technology upgrades. Supports In-Processing, including teaching courses as needed Provides expertise to support/mentor instructors in developing training programs in compliance with Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), Institute of Nuclear Power Operations (INPO), corporate, and department requirements. Conducts instructor evaluations and recommends developmental activities to enhance instructor performance to the instructor and the instructor's manager. Provides oversight and support of fleet qualification tracking processes. Supports project management for implementation of CEC training technology improvements. Develops and conducts CEC specific continuing instructional skills and performance improvement training for instructional staff and Subject Matter Experts. Supports instructor and manager selection and makes recommendations for purchase of instructional support materials and equipment. Oversees the training self-assessment program to ensure quality evaluations are performed at a frequency to preclude CEC issues in training. Participates in NRC, INPO and management audits/assessments for areas relevant to the SAT. Evaluates the results and prepares responses to audit findings. Supports benchmarking initiatives and periodically helps to assess the effectiveness of technology implementation strategies. Support CEC and fleet activities in the areas of business plan initiatives, project management, re-fuel outage support, emergency preparedness duties and special projects. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in a technical discipline, education, or training with 3 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator license with 3 years of related experience OR Associate's degree in a technical discipline, education, or training with 5 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 7 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties. Preferred Qualifications Experience in the implementation of information technology Experience in instructional technology Previous instructional experience Knowledge in Asset Suite 9, Learning Management System (LMS), web-based training, Vision, Nantel, Business Process Management (BPM), and other Information Technology (IT)/training applications Knowledge of and experience with the Systematic Approach to Training (SAT)

Primary Purpose of Position Directly responsible for the supervision, assignment, and coordination of Outage Planning and Services (OP&S) operations and maintenance activities to support safe, reliable, and efficient outage activities consistent with Constellation Nuclear and clean energy center goals. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Effectively supervise craft personnel to assure all assigned maintenance activities are performed safely and effectively, by qualified workers, and in accordance with procedures, Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) regulations and the clean energy center(s) schedule. Participate in various aspects of work control coordination, scheduling, and engineering meetings involving online and refuel outage activities, to ensure that assigned work is properly assigned, planned and ready to work. Effectively communicate and address plant and personnel issues to the appropriate levels of OP&S and station management. Foster open communications and collaboration between all organizations internal and external to OP&S, including site management, Institute of Nuclear Power Operations (INPO), and regulatory agencies. structured_description.about: Risk Assessment Engineer supporting nuclear plant operations at Constellation Energy, the nation's largest clean energy producer. This role performs probabilistic risk assessment (PRA) and engineering analysis to support safety compliance, regulatory requirements, and operational decision-making across nuclear facilities. Available at Entry, Mid-level (E02), or Senior (E03) engineer levels.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Perform probabilistic risk assessment (PRA) engineering and technical analysis applying industry-standard methodologies and nuclear engineering principles
- Ensure all engineering products comply with applicable safety analyses, industry codes (ASME, IEEE), engineering specifications, and NRC regulatory requirements
- Develop and implement PRA models, risk-informed applications, and process improvements to support operational and maintenance decisions
- Recommend format and methodology improvements to risk assessment standard processes and procedures
- Participate in emergency response, outage support, and departmental on-call coverage as directed by management
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Chemical, Civil/Structural, Electrical, Industrial, Mechanical, or Nuclear)
- 2+ years of nuclear or related engineering experience (Mid-level E02) or 5+ years (Senior E03)
- Maintain NRC unescorted access or meet site access requirements
- Favorable medical examination and clearance in accordance with position duties
structured_description.desired:
- 2+ years of PRA (Probabilistic Risk Assessment) experience including model development and risk-informed applications
- NRC or industry PRA certification or equivalent demonstrated expertise
structured_description.location_details: Constellation Energy nuclear facilities (multiple sites available)

jobs.title: Armed Security Officer
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Salary starts at $25.59/hour This class starts August 2026 Primary Purpose of Position Protect the health and welfare of the public by defending the station against any act of radiological sabotage, attack, or theft of special nuclear material. As specified by procedure and the site security plan, maintain a secure perimeter for site access and egress and employee, contractor and visitor entry. Perform search, patrol, and inspections in accordance with the Station Security Plan, procedures, and post orders. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Maintain qualifications for position Conduct patrols and inspections Monitor access to security areas Perform search activities Respond to threats and contingency events Perform equipment testing Acts as a member of an Armed Response Force to provide physical protection of Constellation nuclear clean energy centers and evaluate adversarial actions to prevent intrusion and acts of sabotage. Conduct armed stationary, foot and/or vehicle patrol (interior and/or exterior). Control access and/or egress of personnel, materials and vehicles. Monitor Closed Circuit Television (CCTV), plant surveillance equipment and alarm systems. Deter criminal activity, misconduct, and safety violations. Maintains and implements extensive NRC requirements and Federal/State Laws for site protection and protected area access control. Participate in initial and annual formal training program to complete class and field qualifications necessary to be an armed Nuclear Security Officer Responsible for accurate documentation and record keeping, as required by federal and corporate guidelines. Maintains thorough knowledge of plant to adequately initiate and maintain site defensive strategy All other job assignments and/or duties pursuant to company policy or as directed by management to include but not limited to: (Emergency Response duties and/or coverage, Department duty coverage and/or call out, and positions outside of department in support of outage activities etc.) Minimum Qualifications Must be at least 21 years of age Must have a High School diploma or GED Must be able to obtain and maintain nuclear unescorted access Must meet minimum state requirements to carry a firearm Must have and maintain a valid driver's license Must have and maintain a REAL ID and/or an ID that is REAL ID compliant in accordance with the REAL ID ACT Must pass any State and Federal required training or other qualifications for licensing Must be able to pass a drug and alcohol test with negative results. Constellation follows federal NRC legal requirements for marijuana and medical marijuana, regardless of whether it is legalized in the state of application. A positive test, including for THC or marijuana, will impact the ability to obtain unescorted access to the nuclear power plant which is a required condition of employment Must be able to pass extensive background check, including criminal history, personal references, employment and educational verifications, and credit check Must be able to operate a radio or telephone equipment and/or console monitors and surveillance equipment Must be able to successfully complete all training required for the position, including but not limited to a physical fitness test, firearms qualification, and nuclear site-specific training and testing providing general knowledge of nuclear power generation science and principles Must be able to successfully pass the following in accordance with code of federal regulations and site training and qualification plan for armed nuclear security assignment: Psychological assessment, as appropriate, and physical exams, including vision and hearing Demonstrate an ability to interact cordially and communicate with the public Ability to transit to work location and/or posts (Walk, Climb Stairs, Climb Ladder, Conduct Routine Rounds, etc.) Ability to access and egress Bullet Resistant Enclosure (BRE) Towers Ability to manually carry, lift, or handle gear <= 25 lbs. (i.e., rifle, BRE access hatch, etc.) Body positioning (Stand, Sit, Kneel, Bend, Squat, Reach Over Shoulder Height) Must be able to work 12-hour shifts, including nights, weekends, and forced overtime Must meet and comply with all other requirements under NRC, 10 CFR Part 26 & Appendix B to Part 73 General Criteria for Security Personnel Preferred Qualifications Prior military experience Prior law enforcement experience or Criminal Justice degree Prior commercial security/loss prevention experience

Salary starts at $25.59/hour This class starts August 2026 Primary Purpose of Position Protect the health and welfare of the public by defending the station against any act of radiological sabotage, attack, or theft of special nuclear material. As specified by procedure and the site security plan, maintain a secure perimeter for site access and egress and employee, contractor and visitor entry. Perform search, patrol, and inspections in accordance with the Station Security Plan, procedures, and post orders. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Maintain qualifications for position Conduct patrols and inspections Monitor access to security areas Perform search activities Respond to threats and contingency events Perform equipment testing Acts as a member of an Armed Response Force to provide physical protection of Constellation nuclear clean energy centers and evaluate adversarial actions to prevent intrusion and acts of sabotage. Conduct armed stationary, foot and/or vehicle patrol (interior and/or exterior). Control access and/or egress of personnel, materials and vehicles. Monitor Closed Circuit Television (CCTV), plant surveillance equipment and alarm systems. Deter criminal activity, misconduct, and safety violations. Maintains and implements extensive NRC requirements and Federal/State Laws for site protection and protected area access control. Participate in initial and annual formal training program to complete class and field qualifications necessary to be an armed Nuclear Security Officer Responsible for accurate documentation and record keeping, as required by federal and corporate guidelines. Maintains thorough knowledge of plant to adequately initiate and maintain site defensive strategy All other job assignments and/or duties pursuant
jobs.review_notes: Legitimate armed security role at nuclear facility. Cleaned up formatting and consolidated location details. All content is substantive and relevant to nuclear security operations.

structured_description.about: Armed Security Officer protecting Constellation's nuclear power plants against radiological sabotage, attack, and theft of special nuclear material. You'll conduct armed patrols (stationary, foot, and vehicle), control access to personnel and materials, monitor surveillance systems, and serve as part of an Armed Response Force. This role requires NRC certification and federal firearms licensing.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Conduct armed stationary, foot, and vehicle patrols (interior and exterior)
- Control access and egress of personnel, materials, and vehicles at security checkpoints
- Monitor CCTV, plant surveillance equipment, and alarm systems
- Perform search activities and inspections per Station Security Plan and post orders
- Respond to threats and contingency events as part of Armed Response Force
- Deter criminal activity, misconduct, and safety violations
- Perform equipment testing and maintenance on security systems
- Maintain and implement NRC requirements and Federal/State Laws for site protection
- Maintain thorough knowledge of plant layout to support site defensive strategy
- Conduct accurate record keeping and documentation per federal and corporate guidelines
- Participate in initial and annual formal training to maintain armed Nuclear Security Officer qualifications
- Support emergency response and outage operations as directed
structured_description.qualifications:
- At least 21 years of age
- High School diploma or GED
- Ability to obtain and maintain nuclear unescorted access
- Meet minimum state requirements to carry a firearm
- Valid driver's license and REAL ID or REAL ID-compliant identification
- Pass State and Federal required training and licensing qualifications
- Pass drug and alcohol test with negative results (including THC/marijuana)
- Pass extensive background check (criminal history, personal references, employment and educational verifications, credit check)
- Ability to operate radio, telephone equipment, console monitors, and surveillance equipment
- Successfully complete physical fitness test, firearms qualification, and nuclear site-specific training
- Pass psychological assessment and physical exams (vision and hearing)
- Ability to interact cordially and communicate with the public
- Ability to transit work locations (walk, climb stairs, climb ladders, conduct routine rounds)
- Ability to access and egress Bullet Resistant Enclosure (BRE) Towers
- Ability to manually carry, lift, or handle gear up to 25 lbs
- Physical capability to stand, sit, kneel, bend, squat, and reach over shoulder height
- Comply with all NRC requirements under 10 CFR Part 26
structured_description.location_details: 12-hour shifts including nights, weekends, and forced overtime; interior and exterior patrol duties at nuclear power plant facility; onsite station

jobs.title: Senior Reactor Operator
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. During initial license training, Total compensation earnings eligibility ranging from $159,000.00 to $182,000.00 per year (depending on experience and past license status) including a 15% annual incentive bonus, an initial sign-on bonus, $10,000 bonus spread through training milestones, a comprehensive benefits package, and a 401k with employer matching on contributions. After obtaining SRO license, Total compensation earnings eligibility from $191,000.00 per year, including a target 15% annual bonus, License Premium, as well as extended hours pay at 1.5 times the base salary (expressed on hourly basis) involving mandated operation shift coverage. Relocation Assistance: Relocation assistance may be offered to those who meet the conditions in the Company’s policy. Constellation is seeking Initial License Trainees (ILT) to become a licensed Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) supporting LaSalle Nuclear Power Sation in Marseilles, IL. These Initial License Trainees (ILT) are for the LaSalle Nuclear Station in Marseilles, IL – ILT class beginning March 2027. Primary Purpose of Position The primary purpose of this position is to train and prepare to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor who holds a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO)License. Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. They will also be trained to directly supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators generally. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Attend Initial License Training (ILT) which includes classroom training and examination, along with on-the-job training and evaluation. Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an SRO license in order to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma/GED Meet or exceed one of the following: 1 year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility or 1.5 years at a noncomparable facility. Comparable is PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR and noncomparable is PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR. 1.5 years or more in a position equivalent to Reactor Operator (or Supervisor) at a military reactor (i.e., Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Reactor Operator, Engineering Officer of the Watch, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor). Must have 1.5 years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5 years as a qualified non-licensed operator or Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. Managerial, staff engineer, or supervisor experience at a noncomparable plant may be credited on a 1.5:1.0 basis (i.e., over 2.25 years required for noncomparable facility), and 3 years required for a nontraditional degree. Current or previous SRO Instructor certification and 4 years experience at a comparable facility or 6 years experience at a non-comparable facility. Maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties

Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. Must have 1.5 years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5 years as a qualified non-licensed operator or Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. jobs.review_notes: Cleaned up bullet formatting and concatenated text; clarified NRC licensing pathway and shift requirements. High-quality nuclear operations role with clear training progression and well-defined qualifications.

structured_description.about: Constellation is recruiting Initial License Trainees to become licensed Senior Reactor Operators (SRO) at LaSalle Nuclear Station in Illinois. You will complete comprehensive NRC-mandated training including classroom instruction, examinations, and on-the-job training to obtain your SRO license, then supervise reactor operations and licensed staff in the control room.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Attend Initial License Training (ILT) including classroom training, examinations, and on-the-job training and evaluation
• Successfully complete all phases of training and pass NRC examination to obtain SRO license
• Perform duties as Operations Shift Supervisor, supervising licensed reactor operators in the control room
• Supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators during shift operations
• Respond to emergency situations and support refueling outage activities as assigned
• Execute other job assignments and duties as directed by management or company policy
structured_description.qualifications:
• High school diploma or GED
• One of the following: (1) 1 year Reactor Operator license at comparable facility (PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR); (2) 1.5 years at non-comparable facility (PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR); (3) 1.5+ years in equivalent Reactor Operator or Supervisor position at military reactor; (4) 4-year engineering/engineering technology/physical sciences degree plus 1.5+ years as qualified non-licensed operator or staff member at comparable facility; (5) Current or previous SRO Instructor certification with 4 years comparable or 6 years non-comparable facility experience
• Maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements per NRC regulations
• Pass favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties
structured_description.desired:
• Professional engineer (PE) license
• Experience in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, or chemistry
structured_description.location_details: LaSalle Nuclear Station, Marseilles, Illinois; on-site position; Initial License Training class begins March 2027; extended shift coverage with mandated operational shift requirements

jobs.title: Senior Reactor Operator
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. During initial license training, Total compensation earnings eligibility ranging from $159,000.00 to $182,000.00 per year (depending on experience and past license status) including a 15% annual incentive bonus, an initial sign-on bonus, $10,000 bonus spread through training milestones, a comprehensive benefits package, and a 401k with employer matching on contributions. After obtaining SRO license, Total compensation earnings eligibility from $191,000.00 per year, including a target 15% annual bonus, License Premium, as well as, extended hours pay at 1.5 times the base salary (expressed on hourly basis) involving mandated operation shift coverage. Relocation Assistance: Relocation assistance may be offered to those who meet the conditions in the Company’s policy. Constellation is seeking Initial License Trainees (ILT) to become a licensed Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) supporting Dresden Nuclear Station in Morris, IL. These Initial License Trainees (ILT) are for the Dresden Nuclear Station in Morris, IL – ILT class beginning August,2027. Primary Purpose of Position The primary purpose of this position is to train and prepare to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor who holds a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO)License. Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. They will also be trained to directly supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators generally. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Attend Initial License Training (ILT) which includes classroom training and examination, along with on-the-job training and evaluation. Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an SRO license in order to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma/GED Meet or exceed one of the following: 1 year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility or 1.5-years at a noncomparable facility. Comparable is PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR and noncomparable is PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR. 1.5-years or more in a position equivalent to Reactor Operator (or Supervisor) at military reactor (i.e., Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Reactor Operator, Engineering Officer of the Watch, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor). Must have 1.5-years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5-years as a qualified non-licensed operator or a Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. Managerial, staff engineer, or supervisor experience at noncomparable plant may be credited on a 1.5:1.0 basis (i.e., over 2.25-years required for noncomparable facility), and 3-years required for a nontraditional degree. Current or previous SRO Instructor certification and 4 years experience at a comparable facility OR 6 years experience at a non-comparable facility Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties

structured_description.about: Constellation is recruiting Initial License Trainees to become NRC-licensed Senior Reactor Operators (SRO) at Dresden Nuclear Station in Morris, IL. You'll complete comprehensive classroom and on-the-job training to qualify as an Operations Shift Supervisor responsible for supervising licensed reactor operators in the control room of a BWR facility.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Attend Initial License Training (ILT) including classroom training, NRC examinations, and on-the-job training and evaluation
• Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an NRC SRO license
• Perform duties as Operations Shift Supervisor, directly supervising licensed reactor operators and equipment operators
• Execute emergency response duties, departmental coverage, on-call responsibilities, and support outage activities as directed by management
structured_description.qualifications:
• High school diploma/GED
• One year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility (PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR) OR 1.5 years at a noncomparable facility (PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR); OR 1.5+ years in military reactor position equivalent to Reactor Operator or Supervisor (Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor, etc.); OR 4-year degree in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences with 1.5+ years as qualified non-licensed operator or staff/supervisor in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at comparable facility; OR current/previous SRO Instructor certification with 4 years experience at comparable facility or 6 years at noncomparable facility
• Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements per 10 CFR 73.56
• Pass favorable medical examination and testing per position duties
structured_description.location_details: Dresden Nuclear Station, Morris, IL. Shift-based operations role with extended hours pay at 1.5x base salary for mandated operational shift coverage. Initial License Training class begins August 2027.

structured_description.about: Constellation, the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, seeks an Electrical Analytical Engineer to perform engineering and technical analysis in support of nuclear plant operations. This role supports electrical system design, protection, and transient analysis for nuclear facilities. Position available at Entry, Mid-level, or Senior level depending on experience.
structured_description.responsibilities:

- Ensure all engineering products comply with applicable safety analyses, industry codes (IEEE, NERC), engineering specifications, and NRC regulatory requirements
- Develop and implement analytical processes and techniques for electrical systems (protection, transient analysis, power quality) in compliance with established policies and procedures
- Recommend format and methodology improvements to standard engineering processes and procedures
- Support emergency response, departmental coverage, on-call duties, and outage activities as directed by management

- Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Chemical, Civil/Structural, Electrical, Industrial, or Nuclear)
- Maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements as applicable to nuclear facility
- Favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties
- Entry-level: bachelor's degree required
- Mid-level: 2+ years of nuclear or related engineering experience
- Senior-level: 5+ years of nuclear or related engineering experience

- Transmission or generation protection experience
- Electromagnetic Transients Program (EMTP) modeling experience
- Electrical Transient Analyzer Program (ETAP) experience
- Generator and plant modifications experience

jobs.title: Manager Engineering
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $162,000 to $180,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Over the past decade, Constellation has invested more than $7.1 billion in capital across its nuclear fleet to support continued safe, reliable operations and long-term license extensions. We are planning multi-billion dollar investments through 2030 to include possible extended uprates and overall modernization of our nuclear facilities where appropriate. We are seeking Engineers and other skilled technical experts to help Constellation, the largest private sector power producer in the world, continue to design, build and operate its nuclear fleet at world-class standards. Primary Purpose of Position Performs advanced technical/engineering problem solving in support of nuclear plant operations while acting as a resource and technical expert to engineers. Responsible for technical decisions. Possesses excellent knowledge in functional discipline and its practical application and has detailed knowledge of applicable industry codes and regulations. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Provide in-depth technical expertise to develop, manage and implement engineering analysis, activities and programs. Provide technical expertise and consultation through direct involvement and as a subject matter expert when consulted to identify and resolve equipment and system problems Directly fulfill engineering and technical leadership accountability regarding short-term and long-term programs that impact site operations Perform engineering and technical tasks as assigned by supervision applying general engineering principles Accountable for the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work ensuring proper configuration management and assuring that standard design criteria, practices, procedures and codes are used in preparation of plans and specifications. Perform independent research, reviews, studies and analyses in support of technical projects and programs. Recommend equipment, new concepts and techniques to improve performance, simplify construction, reduce costs, correct design or material flaws, or comply with changes in codes or regulations.
