structured_description.about: Lead engineering management of a multi-disciplined nuclear power plant extended power uprate program. This role requires technical expertise in nuclear power plant systems combined with direct management accountability for engineering modifications, specifications, and field support to enhance plant performance and reliability.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Manage and approve nuclear power plant modifications, engineering analysis, activities, and programs with in-depth technical expertise
- Oversee multi-disciplined design engineering team issuing specifications, modification change packages, upgrade selections, and field engineering support
- Maintain accountability for engineering modifications across power train pumping systems, cooling systems, auxiliary electrical systems, plant control systems, and reactor auxiliary systems
- Identify, communicate, and resolve equipment procurement, equipment reliability, and system problems
- Ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work while maintaining proper configuration management and adherence to design criteria, practices, procedures, and NRC codes
- Perform independent research, reviews, studies, and analyses in support of technical projects and programs
- Recommend equipment, new concepts, and techniques to improve performance, reduce costs, correct design flaws, or comply with regulatory changes
- Communicate with turbine OEMs, specialty consultants, and project implementation teams to ensure successful project delivery
- Fulfill short-term and long-term engineering and technical leadership responsibilities impacting site operations
- Perform emergency response duties and department coverage as assigned
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Chemical, Civil/Structural, Electrical, Industrial, Mechanical, or Nuclear) or related science, OR Professional Engineer License, OR Active/Inactive SRO License with 5 years of engineering or related experience including minimum 1 year nuclear power plant experience
- Maintain minimum access requirements and unescorted access clearance
- Favorable medical examination/testing as applicable
- Knowledge of applicable nuclear industry codes and regulations (10 CFR, ASME, ANS standards)
structured_description.desired:
- Supervisory or managerial experience in nuclear plant engineering
- Professional Engineer (PE) Registration
- Advanced technical degree or related coursework
- 5+ years of nuclear plant design engineering experience

jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $152,100 to $169,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Performs advanced regulatory/technical problem solving and provides analysis/insights in support of plant and fleet regulatory activities. Possesses superior knowledge in functional discipline and its practical application and has excellent knowledge of applicable industry codes and regulations. Understands interrelations and functions of various organizations (e.g., Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), Institute of Nuclear Power Operators (INPO), Nuclear Energy Institute (NEI), North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) etc.) related to nuclear power. Functions as a leader with recognized level of expertise in the regulatory area and is a subject matter expert. As a subject matter expert, leads industry trade groups and works with regulatory agencies to influence programs, projects and regulatory decisions. Develops relationships with key stakeholders and is recognized by peers and supervisors as an expert in his/her respective regulatory area. Trains, mentors and coaches' station and corporate regulatory personnel, and develops and provides training as appropriate. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Perform advanced regulatory and technical tasks and provide insights in support of plant and fleet regulatory activities. Provide initial or complete task management of regulatory issues. Assure all regulatory products prepared or overseen are in accordance with applicable procedures, licensing basis, industry codes, standards, and all regulatory requirements. Provide regulatory/technical expertise and leadership through direct involvement to resolve regulatory issues in assigned programs and other regulatory areas, as necessary. Perform independent and in-depth research, reviews, studies and analyses to develop, manage and implement regulatory activities, related to plant safety, reliability, maintainability, and availability ensuring compliance to site's needs, regulatory requirements, and corporate policies. Provide industry leadership and interface directly with regulatory decision-makers and support staff to influence and drive regulatory decisions and policies consistent with Constellation's goals.. Train and mentor corporate and station regulatory personnel and other functional areas in subject matter areas of expertise.. Provide strategic recommendation on technologies and techniques to improve performance, simplify operation, reduce costs, reduce regulatory burden, correct regulatory non-compliances, or comply with changes in codes or regulations. All other job assignments and/or duties pursuant to company policy or as directed by management to include but not limited to: (Emergency Response duties and/or coverage, Department duty coverage and/or call out, and positions outside of department in support of outage activities etc.). - Meet unescorted access or badging requirements and pass favorable medical examination per position duties

- Advanced technical degree or related graduate coursework

jobs.description:

Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. TOTAL REWARDS Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $147,600 to $164,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION Responsible for daily supply function at a Nuclear site for Supply Operations. Assists Director of Nuclear Supply Operations in development and implementation of Supply Chain Strategies at the Nuclear site. - Serve as single point of accountability for Supply at assigned Nuclear generating station. - Drive improved Supply Chain Management efficiencies and increased standardization throughout assigned generating locations - Work closely with Supply chain and business leadership to develop and implement effective strategic and operational initiatives that support the objectives of Cost Effective and Efficient operations within the Site and throughout Exelon - Maintain oversight of the materials management', warehousing, logistics and fulfillment management, work management interface via the Management Model - Supports an environment that fosters a high performance culture and a learning organization. - Participates in activities for promoting diversity, both in the workplace and in the supplier base. - Seeks feedback on performance and opportunities for continuous improvement. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES Lead the execution of Supply and Procurement operations for nuclear. Serve as the single point of accountability for Supply, Procurement and support operations. Manage customer relationships. Focus on maximum service within desired cost levels, while supporting safety, production, standardization and reliability objectives. Collaborate with GenCo Supply leadership to drive increased efficiency and effectiveness through increased standardization, better work practices, and improved coordination for Supply. Also, co-lead the identification and implementation of Supply Chain Management opportunities and improvements that result in improved efficiencies in cost and service levels. Support the implementation of Supply and Procurement standards, guidelines, and policy requirements across GenCo. Execute Supply Chain Management processes including, procurement, materials management, warehousing, logistics and fulfillment management. Also, monitor business plans and Supply objectives, process controls and performance metrics to manage operational costs, inventory investment, obsolescence reserves, and investment recovery. Provide leadership, development, and coaching to the Site Supply leadership team. Provide accurate and timely feedback and coaching regarding performance, behaviors, and developmental opportunities. Conduct performance planning and appraisal processes with direct reports and ensure effective execution of those processes throughout the organization. Develop and maintain an environment that fosters a high performance culture and a learning organization. Lead the Site Supply team in promoting diversity, both in the workplace and in our supplier base. Serve as a change agent for business initiatives and ensure timely recognition of employee contributions. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS BS in engineering, Business Administration, or related field with minimum 8 years professional experience, including experience at supervisory / managerial level OR Associate's degree with minimum 10 professional years' experience with 5 at the supervisory level OR H.S. Diploma with minimum 12 years professional experience with 5 at the supervisory level Support the Business Units' Emergency Response activities as assigned. Demonstrated knowledge of supply chain management, including strategic procurement, warehousing and logistics, inventory management, investment recovery, etc. Demonstrated business acumen, customer awareness and focus, ability to manage complex issues, and ability to create value. Demonstrated ability to effectively interface with senior management, and build consensus, establish trust, communicate effectively, and foster culture change PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS CPM accreditation is preferred Advanced degree preferred.

structured_description.about: Lead supply chain operations at a nuclear generating station for Constellation, the nation's largest clean energy producer. structured_description.responsibilities:

- Execute supply chain management processes including procurement, materials management, warehousing, and inventory fulfillment to support reactor operations
- Drive supply chain efficiencies and standardization across assigned generating locations while managing vendor relationships and service levels
- Monitor business plans, supply objectives, process controls, and performance metrics to manage operational costs and inventory investment
- Collaborate with GenCo Supply leadership to identify and implement supply chain improvements that support safety, production, and reliability objectives
- Support implementation of supply and procurement standards, guidelines, and regulatory policy requirements across nuclear facilities
- Provide leadership, development, and performance management to the Site Supply team
- Serve as change agent for business initiatives and support site emergency response activities as assigned
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in engineering, business administration, or related field with minimum 8 years professional experience including supervisory/managerial level; OR Associate's degree with minimum 10 years professional experience (5 at supervisory level); OR High school diploma with minimum 12 years professional experience (5 at supervisory level)
- Demonstrated knowledge of supply chain management including strategic procurement, warehousing, logistics, and inventory management
- Demonstrated ability to manage complex business issues and interface effectively with senior management
- Nuclear industry experience strongly preferred
structured_description.desired:
- CPM (Certified Professional Manager) accreditation
- Advanced degree
- Prior experience supporting nuclear generating stations
structured_description.location_details: Onsite at assigned Constellation nuclear generating station

structured_description.about: Structural Engineer at Constellation Energy supporting nuclear plant operations through structural analysis, design, and technical problem-solving. Entry, Mid-level, and Senior positions available. You'll develop engineering analyses, manage technical programs, and serve as a subject matter expert on structural systems and equipment at operating nuclear facilities.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Develop, manage, and implement structural engineering analyses and technical programs supporting plant operations
• Provide technical expertise as a subject matter expert to identify and resolve structural equipment and system problems
• Perform structural design, analysis, and load calculations (cable supports, cable trays, panels, foundations, concrete and steel structures)
• Conduct independent research, studies, and analyses for capital improvements and modifications
• Ensure accuracy and compliance with design criteria, codes (ASME, ACI, AISC), procedures, and regulatory requirements
• Recommend equipment upgrades, design improvements, and process enhancements to improve performance and reduce costs
• Support emergency response, on-call duties, and outage activities as needed
structured_description.qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree in Civil/Structural, Mechanical, Chemical, Electrical, Industrial, or Nuclear Engineering
• 2+ years of nuclear or related engineering experience (Mid-level) or 5+ years (Senior-level)
• Ability to obtain and maintain unescorted access to nuclear facilities
• Favorable medical examination and clearance for position duties
structured_description.desired:
• Professional Engineer (PE) license or Structural Engineer (SE) license
• Advanced degree or coursework in Structural Engineering
• Structural support design experience (cable supports, cable trays, mounting, load analysis, concrete and steel analysis, foundation design)
• Experience with structural analysis software (STAADPro, SAP2000, SAFE)
• Large-scale capital improvements and nuclear plant modification experience
structured_description.location_details: Multiple Constellation Energy nuclear plant locations

structured_description.about: Advanced technical engineering role at Constellation's Byron nuclear station focused on mechanical valve systems and engineering analysis. You will provide expert technical support for valve-related engineering projects, troubleshoot complex mechanical systems, and ensure compliance with nuclear industry codes and NRC regulations. Two career tracks available: Mid-level ($92,700–$114,000) or Senior level ($122,400–$154,000).
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Provide in-depth technical expertise to develop, manage and implement engineering analysis, activities and programs
- Identify and resolve equipment and system problems through direct technical consultation and root cause analysis
- Manage engineering issues from initiation through resolution, ensuring proper configuration management
- Apply standard design criteria, practices, procedures and nuclear codes (ASME, ANSI standards) in preparation of engineering plans and specifications
- Conduct independent research, reviews, studies, and analyses in support of technical projects
- Recommend equipment, new concepts and techniques to improve valve performance, reduce costs, correct design flaws, or ensure regulatory compliance
- Support emergency response, departmental coverage, and refueling outage activities as directed
structured_description.qualifications:
- B.S. in Mechanical Engineering or related discipline
- Minimum 2 years nuclear experience or related engineering experience (Mid-level); minimum 5 years (Senior level)
- Meet requirements outlined in ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 Standard for Nuclear Power Plant Personnel
- Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access as applicable
- Favorable medical examination and testing in accordance with position duties
structured_description.desired:
- Hands-on background with valves, AOVs (air operated valves), MOVs (motor operated valves), or other active mechanical components
- Professional Engineer (PE) Registration or eligibility
- Advanced technical degree (M.S. Engineering) or related graduate coursework
structured_description.location_details: Byron Clean Energy facility, Byron, Illinois. Position includes on-call emergency response duties and outage support requirements.

jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $89,100 to $99,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). SR. Expected salary range of $113.400 to $126,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for providing technical, regulatory, program support, and expertise on Constellation Nuclear Environmental compliance issues and projects in all relevant areas. Serve as the program administrator working with functional areas for assigned programs that directly affect the compliance status of the facility (e.g., Air Permits, DOT shipment of hazardous wastes, National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permits, hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste regulations, storage tanks, drinking water, SPCC, Superfund Amendments Reauthorization Act (SARA)/Emergency Plan Community Right to Know Act (EPRA), International Standardization Organization/Environmental Management System, OSHA chemical safety, hazmat). Attends and presents at Site /corporate meetings when requested. Interacts with industry trade groups and regulatory agencies to increase knowledge base and provide new insights to the organization. Develops relationships with stakeholders and works to develop subject matter expertise. Develops and provides environmental training to site personnel as needed. With the assistance of others, interacts with regulatory officials providing tours and obtains approvals, permits, certifications, and licenses relating to Environmental projects, pollution control systems, or waste management activities. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Responsible for providing technical guidance in assigned programs. Provides regulatory support for all environmental program areas as necessary. Maintains environmental contracts as assigned. Responsible to represent the site during environmental audits conducted by both internal and external organizations. React to and assist the station and fleet in responding to emergent environmental issues. Manage environmental projects including development of scope, solicitation of proposals, proposal evaluation, soliciting funding, project timeline, budget and deliverables. Serve as technical expert to support and participate in representing Constellation Nuclear in meetings with regulatory agencies, engineering consultants, Legal Counsel (internal and external) and serve on technical committees as assigned. Support and perform the evaluation of new technology, processes, and programs and recommend specific actions for Constellation Nuclear operations. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Environmental or Chemical Engineering, Biology, Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geology or related program/equivalent with 2 years of experience working with environmental programs (i.e., Clean Air Act; Clean Water Act; Safe Drinking Water Act; SARA; Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA); and/or other environmental program areas) Experience presenting issues and advocating positions Base level knowledge of Federal and State Environmental statutes and regulations Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties SR. Minimum Qualification Bachelor’s degree in Environmental or Chemical Engineering, Biology; Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geology or related program/equivalent with 6 years of experience working with environmental programs (i.e., Clean Air Act; Clean Water Act; Safe Drinking Water Act; SARA; Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA); and/or other environmental program areas) Minimum of 2 years of experience presenting issues and advocating positions Demonstrated knowledge of multi-media Federal and State Environmental statutes and regulations Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications Master's degree or graduate work in engineering, sciences, law or business is preferred Experience in the nuclear utility industry Professional certification or other recognition

structured_description.about: Senior-level engineering analyst role at Constellation Energy supporting nuclear plant operations. You will perform advanced technical problem-solving on reactor systems and plant equipment, manage complex engineering analyses, and ensure compliance with NRC regulations and industry codes (ASME, IEEE). This is a hands-on technical role requiring independent judgment on safety-significant issues.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Provide in-depth technical expertise to develop, manage, and implement engineering analyses, activities, and programs supporting plant operations
- Identify and resolve equipment and system problems through direct technical consultation and root cause analysis
- Manage engineering issues from initial assessment through completion, ensuring adherence to design criteria, procedures, and applicable codes (ASME, 10 CFR, NEI)
- Perform independent research, reviews, studies, and analyses in support of regulatory compliance and technical projects
- Recommend equipment upgrades, design modifications, and process improvements to enhance safety, performance, reduce costs, and address regulatory changes
- Support emergency response, refueling outages, maintenance activities, and other departmental assignments as directed
- Maintain configuration management and ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of all technical work and documentation
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in engineering or technical field with 5+ years nuclear/related industry experience, OR Associate's degree with 7+ years nuclear/related experience, OR High School Diploma/GED with 8+ years nuclear experience (5 years at exempt level)
- Working knowledge of engineering processes and systems specific to assigned functional area (reactor systems, mechanical, electrical, instrumentation & control, etc.)
- Ability to maintain unescorted access/security clearance and pass medical examination as required
- Strong technical writing and communication skills; ability to prepare engineering reports and justify technical decisions to management and regulators
structured_description.location_details: Onsite at Constellation nuclear plant facility. Must maintain NRC unescorted access authorization and pass baseline medical examination. Availability for emergency response, refueling outages, and weekend/off-hours support as required.

jobs.title: Senior Reactor Operator
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the nation's largest producer of clean, carbon-free energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: accelerating the transition to a carbon-free future. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things and help fight climate change. Join us to lead the clean energy future. Total Rewards Constellation offers a wide range of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, we offer a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including a robust wellness program; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. During initial license training, Total compensation earnings eligibility ranging from $159,000.00 to $182,000.00 per year (depending on experience and past license status) including a 15% annual incentive bonus, an initial sign-on bonus, $10,000 bonus spread through training milestones, a comprehensive benefits package, and a 401k with employer matching on contributions. After obtaining SRO license, Total compensation earnings eligibility from $191,000.00 per year, including a target 15% annual bonus, License Premium, as well as, extended hours pay at 1.5 times the base salary (expressed on hourly basis) involving mandated operation shift coverage. Relocation Assistance: Relocation assistance may be offered to those who meet the conditions in the Company’s policy. Constellation is seeking Initial License Trainees (ILT) to become a licensed Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) supporting Bryon Nuclear Power Station located in Byron, IL. These Initial License Trainees (ILT) are for the Byron Nuclear Station in Byron, IL – ILT class beginning in August, 2026. The primary purpose of this position is to train and prepare to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor who holds a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO)License. Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. They will also be trained to directly supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators generally. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Attend Initial License Training (ILT) which includes classroom training and examination, along with on-the-job training and evaluation. Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an SRO license in order to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma/GED Meet or exceed one of the following: 1 year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility or 1.5 years at a noncomparable facility. Comparable is PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR and noncomparable is PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR. 1.5 years or more in a position equivalent to Reactor Operator (or Supervisor) at a military reactor (i.e., Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Reactor Operator, Engineering Officer of the Watch, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor). Must have 1.5 years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5 years as a qualified non-licensed operator or Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. Managerial, staff engineer, or supervisor experience at a noncomparable plant may be credited on a 1.5:1.0 basis (i.e., over 2.25 years required for noncomparable facility), and 3 years required for a nontraditional degree. Current or previous SRO Instructor certification and 4 years experience at a comparable facility or 6 years experience at a non-comparable facility. Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties.

jobs.title: Supervisor Chemistry Programs
jobs.description:

We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Expected salary range of $133,200 to $148,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Provide a leadership role in the supervision of operational chemistry programs. Provide coaching and mentoring for all operational activities. Maintain Chemistry programs including all performance and regulatory aspects of the program. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Coach and mentor the Chemists who are responsible for various Clean Energy Center (CEC) programs (e.g. Laboratory Instrumentation Quality Assurance (QA) / Quality Control (QC), Radiological Effluent and Environmental Programs, Raw Water Chemistry, Boiling Water or Pressurized Water Chemistry). Provide technical challenge and guidance for program Chemists in resolving CEC chemistry issues or decisions. Lead and provide support for Chemistry related projects. Participate and support the Chemistry Technician Training Program and ensure technical education for Chemistry program Chemists. Lead and provide an interface role with other departments and in schedule reviews and troubleshooting. Participate in Emergency Preparedness (EP) rotation. Subject to call out as required in support of CEC requirements. Advocate for CEC support of chemistry equipment to provide good material condition. Represent Chemistry Manager at CEC leadership meetings. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High School diploma/GED with 9 years overall experience, which includes 3 years related experience that shall include 1-year nuclear power plant experience and 0.25 years on-site experience OR Associate's degree with 7 years overall experience, which includes 3 years related experience that shall include 1-year nuclear power plant experience and 0.25 years on-site experience OR Bachelor's degree with 5 years overall experience, which includes 3 years related experience that shall include 1-year nuclear power plant experience and 0.25 years on-site experience Must meet additional requirements as outlined in ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 (4.4.5) Standard, "Selection, Qualification, and Training of Personnel for Nuclear Power Plants" Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications Bachelor's degree in chemistry or chemical engineering with 5 years of chemistry experience in an operating nuclear power plant Supervisory experience Radiochemistry Supervisor ANSI qualifications (Radiochemistry Training and 2 years Gamma Spectroscopy)* *Note, dependent on current staffing in other chemistry leadership roles, this may be a minimum requirement

jobs.title: Maintenance Planner Electrical
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $110,700 to $123,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Plan and prepare work packages for work to be performed by the respective maintenance departments to ensure that the Clean Energy Center (CEC) maintenance is performed in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner, in compliance with the Quality Assurance (QA) manual, company policies, procedures, and applicable codes and standards. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Plan and prepare work instructions that provide direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, performance of preventive maintenance and surveillances, and the implementation of modifications. Select and approve purchase or make procurement recommendations for all materials needed for work package execution. Conduct field walkdowns to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance, modification, and surveillance activities and to provide input for refining and improving work package quality. Interface and collaborate with Work Management, Supply, Operations, and Engineering to identify and resolve work package issues. Define procedures, drawings, equipment, and materials required to perform planned work packages. Facilitate package preparation by preparing and/or submitting Out of Service (OOS's), Radiation Work Permit (RWP's), plant barrier impairments, welding/grinding permits, fire impairments, Operability impact statements, As Low As Reasonably Achievable (ALARA) task outs, Regulatory/Code documentation, Nuclear Oversight (NOS) hold/witness points, freeze seal and temporary leak repair documentation, and other requests. Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable CEC procedures including Nuclear Safeguard Protocol (NSPs), National Strategy for Space Nuclear Power and Propulsion (NSWPs), American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) Code, etc. Approve and review all required work packages to ensure safety, high quality, technical rigor, and 10CFR50.59 screening or evaluation (if required). Develop revise, and review maintenance procedures to facilitate and enhance work package development, maintain compliance with codes, standards, and vendor recommendation, and maintenance direction. Provide project management of other system planners or contractors to develop detailed elements of overall package preparation. Participate in work control/scheduling meetings, planning feedback/craft interface time, detailed contract requisition reviews, complex troubleshooting, EQR2 reviews, detailed Operating Experience (OPEX) searches, Maintenance risk request reviews, Planner peer reviews, and system interaction reviews to ensure applicable CEC processes and applicable changes are supported. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree with 2 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator license with 2 years of related experience OR Associate's degree with 3 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 4 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications 5 years of experience in nuclear power plant operations

structured_description.about: Constellation seeks a Performance Improvement Specialist at Braidwood Nuclear Power Station (Morris, IL) to implement and oversee nuclear performance improvement programs including Corrective Action Program (CAP), self-assessment, benchmarking, human performance initiatives, and INPO compliance activities. This is a multi-level exempt position reporting to site management.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Implement performance improvement programs and provide oversight of program execution
• Review site performance data and identify adverse trends using observations, CAP, self-assessment, benchmarking, OPEX, and performance indicator data
• Support CAP investigations and issue reviews; manage performance indicators
• Coordinate cross-functional teams to prepare Management Review Meeting (MRM) packages
• Support nuclear safety programs including Human Performance, Operating Experience, Safety Culture, and Nuclear Safety Review Board activities
• Support INPO-related activities including evaluation readiness, scheduling, and course coordination
• Perform emergency response duties and other assignments as directed by management
structured_description.qualifications: E02 level: Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) license with 2 years related experience, OR Associate's degree with 4 years related experience, OR High school diploma/GED with 6 years related experience. E03 level: Bachelor's degree with 5 years related experience, OR current/previous SRO license with 5 years related experience, OR Associate's degree with 7 years related experience, OR High school diploma/GED with 9 years related experience. Minimum 2 years exempt level experience (E03). Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements as applicable. Favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties.
structured_description.desired: Experience in PI/OR (Performance Improvement/Operational Readiness) programs; Previous SRO license/certification; Root Cause Analysis qualified; Previous experience in Engineering, Maintenance, or Operations departments
structured_description.location_details: Onsite at Braidwood Nuclear Power Station, Morris, IL; includes emergency response duty coverage

In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $133,200 to $148,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Directly responsible for the supervision, assignment, and coordination of Maintenance Division activities to ensure a productive workforce that supports all divisional, departmental, Clean Energy Center (CEC), and corporate goals while in accordance with all company policies and local, state, and federal regulations. Interfaces with the department schedulers and planners to plan and execute work. Provides recommendations on training, procedure changes, and other needs to support safe and efficient execution of work activities. Participates as an Emergency Plan team member. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Effectively supervise craft personnel to ensure that all assigned maintenance activities are performed safely and effectively by qualified workers in accordance with CEC procedures, Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) regulations, and the CEC schedule. Ensures department personnel are properly supervised, trained, and qualified to perform assignments, including emergency plan qualifications. Administer coaching, mentoring and performance management, including initiation of disciplinary action, for assigned employees. Effectively communicating significant CEC and personnel issues to Maintenance Manager and all applicable levels of CEC management. Fostering open communications and collaboration between all organizations internal and external to the CEC including Institute of Nuclear Power Operations (INPO) and regulatory agencies. Ensure effective communication of company programs, announcements, policies, etc. to assigned employees and feedback to the company from assigned employees. Participate in various aspects of work control coordination, scheduling, and engineering meetings involving online and refuel outage activities, to ensure that assigned work is properly assigned, planned and ready to work. Understands, supports and administers all Maintenance processes, such as the training program, surveillance program, procedure revisions and Corrective Action Program (CAP) to ensure effective work practices. Identify and support improvement opportunities, when applicable. Supervise vendor inspections and repair of CEC equipment. Assure effective procurement and use of material, parts, tools, equipment, and Maintenance and Technical (M&TE). Participates in all aspects of Maintenance Department management to support safe and efficient operation of the CEC, including the development, implementation, and performance monitoring of all corporate, CEC, and divisional business goals and objectives. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $110,700 to $123,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Plan and prepare work packages for work to be performed by the respective maintenance departments to ensure that the Clean Energy Center (CEC) maintenance is performed in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner, in compliance with the Quality Assurance (QA) manual, company policies, procedures, and applicable codes and standards. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Plan and prepare work instructions that provide direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, performance of preventive maintenance and surveillance's, and the implementation of modifications. Select and approve purchase, or make procurement recommendations for all materials needed for work package execution. Conduct field walkdowns to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance, modification, and surveillance activities and to provide input for refining and improving work package quality. Interface and collaborate with Work Management, Supply, Operations, and Engineering to identify and resolve work package issues. Define procedures, drawings, equipment, and materials required to perform planned work packages. Facilitate package preparation by preparing and/or submitting Out of Service (OOS's), Radiation Work Permit (RWP's), plant barrier impairments, welding/grinding permits, fire impairments, Operability impact statements, As Low As Reasonably Achievable (ALARA) task outs, Regulatory/Code documentation, Nuclear Oversight (NOS) hold/witness points, freeze seal and temporary leak repair documentation, and other requests. Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable CEC procedures including Nuclear Safeguard Protocol (NSPs), National Strategy for Space Nuclear Power and Propulsion (NSWPs), American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) Code, etc. Approve and review all required work packages to ensure safety, high quality, technical rigor, and 10CFR50.59 screening or evaluation (if required). Develop revise, and review maintenance procedures to facilitate and enhance work package development, maintain compliance with codes, standards, and vendor recommendation, and maintenance direction. Provide project management of other system planners or contractors to develop detailed elements of overall package preparation. Participate in work control/scheduling meetings, planning feedback/craft interface time, detailed contract requisition reviews, complex troubleshooting, EQR2 reviews, detailed Operating Experience (OPEX) searches, Maintenance risk request reviews, Planner peer reviews, and system interaction reviews to ensure applicable CEC processes and applicable changes are supported. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree with 2 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator license with 2 years of related experience OR Associate's degree with 3 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 4 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications 5 years of experience in nuclear power plant operations

jobs.review_notes: Valid nuclear maintenance role (welding + turbine maintenance at Constellation); consolidated verbose responsibility bullets into 6 clear, action-oriented statements; cleaned formatting and removed redundancy; added radiation protection emphasis to 'about' field.

structured_description.about: Maintenance Welder in the Mobile Turbine/Rotating Department at Constellation Energy, performing welding and mechanical maintenance on steam/gas turbines, reactor feed pump turbines (RFPT), pumps, gearboxes, and balance-of-plant equipment across nuclear facilities. This role requires nuclear plant access, radiation protection qualifications, and the ability to work safely in radioactive and high-temperature environments.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Perform fitting and welding tasks on turbines, pumps, gearboxes, fans, bearings, boilers, pipe systems, valves, and rotating equipment; execute disassembly, part repair/replacement, and assembly per work packages
• Demonstrate maintenance expertise on steam/gas turbines, turbine valves, reactor feed pump turbines (RFPT), terry turbines, and balance-of-plant systems; perform alignment on rotating equipment
• Review technical manuals and power plant prints; recommend procedure revisions and support development of troubleshooting and repair plans
• Coordinate with plant personnel, work groups, and vendors on equipment maintenance; provide technical support to operations, maintenance, and engineering staff
• Report unsafe conditions, participate in corrective action programs, and perform verification checks on maintenance items
• Maintain nuclear qualifications and Radiation Protection Qualification; work safely in radiological controlled areas and confined spaces
structured_description.qualifications:
• Associate's Degree in Power Plant Technology/Engineering Technology or High School Diploma + 12 months trade/technical experience in Power Plant Technology, OR Vocational Technical Diploma + 6 months shop experience
• Pass MASS test and complete nuclear plant training requirements
• Valid driver's license with insured vehicle; ability to lift minimum 50 lbs
• Obtain and maintain unescorted nuclear plant access and Radiation Protection Qualification
• Ability to work in power plant environment: climbing, high elevations on ladders/scaffolds, confined spaces, heat/dust/smoke/noise exposure, shift work/weekends
• Basic computer skills and strong oral/written communication
structured_description.location_details: 80%+ travel within nuclear facilities; shift work and weekends required; work at height, in confined spaces, and in radioactive environments

structured_description.about: Senior Mechanical Engineer at a Constellation nuclear facility providing advanced technical expertise in mechanical systems, equipment analysis, and problem-solving in direct support of reactor operations. Acts as a subject matter expert and technical resource for site engineering decisions impacting plant operations and long-term capital/maintenance programs.
responsibilities: Develop, manage, and implement mechanical engineering analyses, technical activities, and programs supporting plant operations
Provide technical expertise to identify and resolve equipment and system problems affecting reactor safety and availability
Lead short-term and long-term engineering programs impacting site operations with appropriate technical and regulatory accountability
Perform engineering analyses applying mechanical engineering principles; ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work products
Maintain proper configuration management and adherence to design criteria, industry codes (ASME, etc.), and site procedures
Conduct independent research, reviews, and technical studies in support of engineering projects and regulatory compliance
Recommend equipment upgrades, new technologies, and techniques to improve performance, reduce costs, address design deficiencies, or meet regulatory changes
Support emergency response, planned outages, and departmental coverage as needed
qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Nuclear Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Civil/Structural Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or Industrial Engineering
Minimum 5 years of nuclear power plant experience or closely related engineering experience in a regulated facility
Meet ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 Standard requirements for Selection, Qualification, and Training of Personnel for Nuclear Power Plants
Maintain minimum unescorted access or escorted access requirements as applicable to the role
Pass favorable medical examination and/or occupational health testing in accordance with position duties
desired: Professional Engineer (PE) Registration/Licensure
Advanced technical degree (Master's) or specialized coursework in nuclear engineering or mechanical systems

Assists shift supervision in the performance of any and all of the duties involved in directing and coordinating the actions of the other personnel assigned to assist in operation of the station. equipment as required. Required to be thoroughly familiar with entry conditions and cautions for the emergency operating procedure and the basis of each step in the procedures. Responds to and takes action as required in mitigating consequences of plant transient. Responds to plant indicators and alarms in accordance with plant procedures including identifying those parameters that are indicative of an emergency condition and initiating designated actions. Responsible to identify and correct deficient conditions through the use of the station Corrective Action program, initiating action requests for material condition issues and submitting Procedure change requests. Starts, stops, controls and monitors nuclear reactor and auxiliary reactor systems, as required for plant operations, including taking manual control of equipment as conditions warrant. Alternate relevant experience examples include operation and maintenance of equipment such as HVAC, electrical and maintenance technicians OR 2-years' experience in operations or maintenance of a non-generating nuclear power facility (e.g. Navy Nuclear operations or maintenance training/experience or operations or maintenance experience at a DOE facility OR Bachelor's degree in technical discipline (engineering, engineering technology, or related science program) OR Associates degree in technical discipline (engineering, engineering technology, or related science program) OR 43 credit hours of post-secondary education of which 75% (at least 8 courses or 32 hours) is advanced math, physics, chemistry and/or engineering/engineering technology

jobs.review_notes: Clean, well-structured nuclear operator trainee role with comprehensive responsibilities and qualifications. Minor clarification added to 'about' section referencing NRC licensing. No substantive changes needed.

structured_description.about: Trainee operator role at a nuclear power station under direct supervision. You'll perform operational activities including equipment monitoring, system operations, valve alignments, and control room duties while learning nuclear facility operations under NRC-licensed supervision.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Start, stop, adjust, test and operate pumps, valves, switchgear, controls and other system components as directed
- Perform log taking, equipment evaluation, system valve alignments, and administrative duties
- Monitor recorders and indicators in control room for nuclear and turbine generator systems to ensure operation within prescribed limits
- Manipulate controls and observe nuclear and radiation monitoring instruments during reactor refueling in accordance with procedural controls
- Implement tagging and system out-of-service requests, directing others to realign valves and hang tags
- Initiate Emergency Plan procedures in accordance with implementing guidelines
- Obtain overview data and perform calculations as required by procedure
- Operate station communications systems
- Participate as member of station fire brigade and function as fire brigade leader when required
- Perform shift turnovers and maintain operational logs in accordance with NRC license requirements
- Monitor plant indicators and alarms; identify emergency conditions and initiate designated actions
- Respond to plant transients and take action to mitigate consequences
- Identify and correct deficient conditions through the Corrective Action program
- Assist shift supervision in directing and coordinating actions of assigned personnel
- May be required to assist in reactor refueling operations
structured_description.qualifications:
- High school diploma or equivalent
- Satisfactory completion of Plant Operator Selection System (POSS) test and Basic Math and Science Test (BMST)
- Nuclear unescorted access eligibility
- One of the following: 1 year of technical experience in power production, petrochemical or industrial process plants (operational, maintenance, design, or training); OR operation and maintenance experience with HVAC, electrical, or maintenance equipment; OR 2 years' experience in operations or maintenance at non-generating nuclear facilities (Navy Nuclear or DOE facility); OR Bachelor's degree in technical discipline (engineering, engineering technology, or related science); OR Associates degree in technical discipline; OR 43+ credit hours of post-secondary education with 75% (at least 32 hours) in advanced math, physics, chemistry, and/or engineering
structured_description.location_details: On-site nuclear power station operation; training class starts January 2027; starting pay $43.71–$48.51/hr depending on experience

structured_description.about: Machinist in the Shops Department performing mechanical maintenance on power plant equipment at Constellation nuclear facilities. Responsibilities include disassembly, repair, and reassembly of pumps, gearboxes, fans, bearings, valves, and balance-of-plant systems. Role requires nuclear plant access, radiation protection qualifications, and ability to work in a nuclear environment with radiation dose exposure.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Perform maintenance and repair work on plant equipment such as pumps, gearboxes, fans, bearings, pipe systems, valves and limit torque operators, including disassembly, part repair or replacement, and assembly using sound technical decisions
• Demonstrate strong maintenance skills on various pumps, valves, and balance of plant (BOP) equipment using correct tools and equipment such as rigging, forklifts and cranes
• Execute assigned work packages and interpret power plant prints
• Maintain nuclear qualifications and Radiation Protection Qualification and training, including respirator qualifications as needed
• Work on cross-functional teams on major projects and provide technical support to plant operations, maintenance staff and engineering
• Perform verification checks on maintenance items covered in maintenance technician training
• Adhere to established maintenance standards and practices while performing assigned tasks in a safe and efficient manner
• Participate in continuous improvement culture through training, reinforcing standards, reporting, and mitigating unsafe conditions
structured_description.qualifications:
• Associates Degree in Power Plant Technology, Engineering Technology, or Electronics OR HS Diploma with minimum 12 months of trade/technical experience in Power Plant Technology OR Vocational Technical High School diploma with 6 months of shop experience
• Successfully complete training requirements and time-in-job requirements including passing the MASS test
• Ability to obtain unescorted nuclear plant access
• Valid driver's license and access to an insured vehicle
• Ability to lift a minimum of 50 pounds
• Basic computer skills and good oral and written communication skills
• Ability to work in power plant environment including climbing, walking long distances, exposure to heat, dust, smoke, dirt, high noise areas, confined spaces, and nuclear power plant environment with radiation dose exposure
• Ability to work at high elevations on ladders and scaffolds and perform shift work or weekends as required
• Ability to perform tasks inside radiological controlled areas
structured_description.location_details: Onsite at Constellation nuclear power plant facility. Shift work and weekends required. Up to 20% travel may be required. Work involves exposure to nuclear environment, radiation dose potential, high elevations, confined spaces, and industrial hazards.

Primary Purpose of Position Functionally directs the Clean Energy Center (CEC) operations training groups and demonstrates mastery of skills and extensive subject matter knowledge in carrying out assignments associated with the development, implementation, evaluation, and modification of discipline related training programs. Acts as the CEC subject matter expert for their functional area. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Drives CEC and personnel performance improvement using training. Develops, schedules, coordinates, and conducts performance-based training programs as required by user community in accordance with Constellation's Systematic Approach to Training (SAT) model, department procedures, and with proper application of the analysis, design, development, implementation, and evaluation phases. Conducts counseling sessions for trainees, evaluates the results, and recommends/develops individualized training programs. Assures the training programs are maintained current and incorporate information gained from Institute of Nuclear Power Operations (INPO) Significant Operating Experience Reports and Constellation Nuclear Event Reports (NERs), Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) Bulletins and Information Notices, CEC design change and backfit information, CEC procedure changes and revisions, new regulations, and training effectiveness reports. Participates in NRC, INPO and management audits, and prepares responses to audit findings. Supports CEC and fleet in the areas of business initiatives, project management, refuel outage support, emergency preparedness duties, and special projects. Maintains knowledge of CEC operations, disciplinary skills and techniques, commensurate with the responsibility to develop and conduct effective, high-quality training. Coordinates the efforts of instructors in the development and conduct of training and provides expertise in the implementation of the SAT process by directing and counseling instructors and other staff as assigned by the manager. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Current or previous SRO license OR SRO certification with 6 years of related (e.g. test reactor, commercial, industrial, or military power plant operator) experience 2 years of training or education experience The requirements as outlined above meet or exceed ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014 (4.4.3) Standard, "Selection, Qualification, and Training of Personnel for Nuclear Power Plants" Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications Previously Operations Instructor and Simulator Instructor qualified Experience in training self-assessments and INPO Continuum process.

jobs.title: Maintenance Planner I&C
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $110,700 to $123,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Plan and prepare work packages for work to be performed by the respective maintenance departments to ensure that the Clean Energy Center (CEC) maintenance is performed in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner, in compliance with the Quality Assurance (QA) manual, company policies, procedures, and applicable codes and standards. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Plan and prepare work instructions that provide direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, performance of preventive maintenance and surveillance's, and the implementation of modifications. Select and approve purchase, or make procurement recommendations for all materials needed for work package execution. Conduct field walkdowns to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance, modification, and surveillance activities and to provide input for refining and improving work package quality. Interface and collaborate with Work Management, Supply, Operations, and Engineering to identify and resolve work package issues. Define procedures, drawings, equipment, and materials required to perform planned work packages. Facilitate package preparation by preparing and/or submitting Out of Service (OOS's), Radiation Work Permit (RWP's), plant barrier impairments, welding/grinding permits, fire impairments, Operability impact statements, As Low As Reasonably Achievable (ALARA) task outs, Regulatory/Code documentation, Nuclear Oversight (NOS) hold/witness points, freeze seal and temporary leak repair documentation, and other requests. Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable CEC procedures including Nuclear Safeguard Protocol (NSPs), National Strategy for Space Nuclear Power and Propulsion (NSWPs), American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) Code, etc. Approve and review all required work packages to ensure safety, high quality, technical rigor, and 10CFR50.59 screening or evaluation (if required). Develop revise, and review maintenance procedures to facilitate and enhance work package development, maintain compliance with codes, standards, and vendor recommendation, and maintenance direction. Provide project management of other system planners or contractors to develop detailed elements of overall package preparation. Participate in work control/scheduling meetings, planning feedback/craft interface time, detailed contract requisition reviews, complex troubleshooting, EQR2 reviews, detailed Operating Experience (OPEX) searches, Maintenance risk request reviews, Planner peer reviews, and system interaction reviews to ensure applicable CEC processes and applicable changes are supported. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree with 2 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator license with 2 years of related experience OR Associate's degree with 3 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 4 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications 5 years of experience in nuclear power plant operations. I&C related work experience and/or education.

- Conduct field walkdowns to identify and analyze corrective/preventive maintenance, modification, and surveillance activities

- Approve and review all required work packages to ensure safety, quality, and technical rigor

- Participate in work control/scheduling meetings, planning feedback sessions, troubleshooting, EQR2 reviews, Operating Experience searches, and peer reviews
- Support emergency response, departmental coverage, and outage activities as directed

jobs.title: Senior Reactor Operator
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the nation's largest producer of clean, carbon-free energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: accelerating the transition to a carbon-free future. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things and help fight climate change. Join us to lead the clean energy future. Total Rewards Constellation offers a wide range of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, we offer a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including a robust wellness program; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. During initial license training, Total compensation earnings eligibility ranging from $159,000.00 to $182,000.00 per year (depending on experience and past license status) including a 15% annual incentive bonus, an initial sign-on bonus, $10,000 bonus spread through training milestones, a comprehensive benefits package, and a 401k with employer matching on contributions. After obtaining SRO license, Total compensation earnings eligibility from $191,000.00 per year, including a target 15% annual bonus, License Premium, as well as, extended hours pay at 1.5 times the base salary (expressed on hourly basis) involving mandated operation shift coverage. Relocation Assistance: Relocation assistance may be offered to those who meet the conditions in the Company’s policy. Constellation is seeking Initial License Trainees (ILT) to become a licensed Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) supporting Quad Cities Nuclear Power Station located in Cordova, IL. These Initial License Trainees (ILT) are for the Quad Cities Nuclear Station in Cordova, IL – ILT class beginning in January, 2027. Primary Purpose of Position The primary purpose of this position is to train and prepare to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor who holds a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO)License. Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. They will also be trained to directly supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators generally. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Attend Initial License Training (ILT) which includes classroom training and examination, along with on-the-job training and evaluation. Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an SRO license in order to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma/GED Meet or exceed one of the following: 1 year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility or 1.5 years at a noncomparable facility. Comparable is PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR and noncomparable is PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR. 1.5 years or more in a position equivalent to Reactor Operator (or Supervisor) at a military reactor (i.e., Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Reactor Operator, Engineering Officer of the Watch, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor). Must have 1.5 years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5 years as a qualified non-licensed operator or Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. Managerial, staff engineer, or supervisor experience at a noncomparable plant may be credited on a 1.5:1.0 basis (i.e., over 2.25 years required for noncomparable facility), and 3 years required for a nontraditional degree. Current or previous SRO Instructor certification and 4 years experience at a comparable facility or 6 years experience at a non-comparable facility. Maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties

• Ability to maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements

The primary purpose of this position is to train and prepare to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor who holds a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO)License. Upon completion of training the candidate will be fully licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to supervise the licensed activities of licensed reactor operators in the reactor control room. They will also be trained to directly supervise bargaining unit reactor operators and equipment operators generally. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Attend Initial License Training (ILT) which includes classroom training and examination, along with on-the-job training and evaluation. Successfully complete all phases of training to obtain an SRO license in order to perform the duties of an Operations Shift Supervisor. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma/GED Meet or exceed one of the following: 1 year Reactor Operator license at a comparable facility or 1.5 years at a noncomparable facility. Comparable is PWR to PWR or BWR to BWR and noncomparable is PWR to BWR or BWR to PWR. 1.5 years or more in a position equivalent to Reactor Operator (or Supervisor) at a military reactor (i.e., Power Plant Watch Engineer, Engineering Watch Supervisor, Reactor Operator, Engineering Officer of the Watch, Propulsion Plant Watch Supervisor). Must have 1.5 years after qualification. Degree from a 4-year program or equivalent in engineering, engineering technology, or physical sciences, or professional engineer license, and more than 1.5 years as a qualified non-licensed operator or Staff member, Supervisor or Manager in work control, operations, engineering, outage management, maintenance, radiation protection, chemistry, or accredited training at a comparable (BWR or PWR) facility. Managerial, staff engineer, or supervisor experience at a noncomparable plant may be credited on a 1.5:1.0 basis (i.e., over 2.25 years required for noncomparable facility), and 3 years required for a nontraditional degree. Current or previous SRO Instructor certification and 4 years experience at a comparable facility or 6 years experience at a non-comparable facility. Maintain minimum access or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties
