jobs.review_notes: This is an IT cyber security role at a nuclear/clean energy company, not a nuclear-specific position. Enhanced 'about' to clarify Constellation's nuclear operations context and the critical infrastructure nature of the work. Content is complete and well-structured with sufficient detail.

structured_description.about: Constellation is the nation's largest clean energy producer, operating nuclear, wind, and solar facilities. This Cyber Security Analyst role supports the delivery and maintenance of IT cyber security and network solutions across the company's critical energy infrastructure. You will test, implement, and maintain security solutions under senior analyst guidance, balancing daily operational support with project-based work.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Perform, document, and assist in planning work activities relating to small projects, sub-projects, or process improvements
- Enhance, maintain, or support existing IT security products and processes to defined service level agreements
- Analyze requirements, design, build, and test IT security software solutions for projects and sub-projects
- Maintain engagement with business and IT partners on cyber security initiatives
- Assist less experienced personnel with technical guidance
- Maintain technical knowledge and business acumen within your discipline and manage professional development
- Identify and analyze security trends and issues to drive new business opportunities and solve complex problems
- Provide on-call support after normal business hours
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related discipline
- 2 to 5 years related experience, or 6 to 9 years equivalent combination of education and work experience
- Ability to solve problems using pre-defined procedures and guidelines
- Strong communication skills: able to explain highly technical information in non-technical terminology (written and verbal)
- Flexible and adaptable to changing priorities based on business needs
- Working knowledge of Server and Workstation Operating Systems (Windows/Linux) and command prompt
- Working knowledge of IP-based switching, routing, and network environments (e.g., Cisco)
- Working knowledge of computer networking concepts, protocols, and network security methodologies
- Working knowledge of cyber security practices for enterprise environments
- Working knowledge of firewall operations (e.g., Cisco & Checkpoint)
structured_description.desired:
- Working knowledge of cyber security program elements: Policy Development, Application Security, Information Security, Network Security, Disaster Recovery Planning, Operational Security, Incident Response, and End User Education
- Working knowledge of Security Information Event Management (SIEM) solutions (e.g., Splunk, Nitro, Industrial Defender)
- Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) certification or other network credentials
- Security Plus training

Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $107,100 to $119,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Plan and prepare work packages for work to be performed by the respective maintenance departments and contractors, to ensure that the plant is maintained in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner, in compliance with the company policies, procedures, and applicable codes and standards. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Prepare work instructions and work packages to assist the planners that provide direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, performance of preventive maintenance and the implementation of modifications. Interface with Work Management, Supply and Engineering to identify and resolve priorities and work package issues utilizing excellent interpersonal and written/verbal communication skills, Define procedures, drawings, equipment and materials required to performing planned work packages. Conduct field walk-downs to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance, modification activities. Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable station procedures. Review all required work packages and submit for approval before executing. Manage multiple projects, establish work priorities and achieve results. Assist in planned and forced outage coordination as required. All other job assignments and/or duties pursuant to company policy or as directed by management to include but not limited to: (positions outside of department in support of outage activities etc.) Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in engineering with 2 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience OR Associate's degree with 4 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience OR High school diploma/GED with 6 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience Maintain minimum access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite applications. Preferred Qualifications 5 years planning experience in a power plant environment Experience with Hyperion system

Primary Purpose of Position Plan and prepare work packages for work to be performed by the respective maintenance departments and contractors, to ensure that the plant is maintained in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner, in compliance with the company policies, procedures, and applicable codes and standards. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Prepare work instructions and work packages to assist the planners that provide direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, performance of preventive maintenance and the implementation of modifications. Interface with Work Management, Supply and Engineering to identify and resolve priorities and work package issues utilizing excellent interpersonal and written/verbal communication skills, Define procedures, drawings, equipment and materials required to performing planned work packages. Conduct field walk-downs to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance, modification activities. Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable station procedures. Review all required work packages and submit for approval before executing. Manage multiple projects, establish work priorities and achieve results. Assist in planned and forced outage coordination as required. All other job assignments and/or duties pursuant to company policy or as directed by management to include but not limited to: (positions outside of department in support of outage activities etc.) Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in engineering with 2 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience OR Associate's degree with 4 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience OR High school diploma/GED with 6 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience Maintain minimum access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite applications.

Preferred Qualifications 5 years planning experience in a power plant environment Experience with Hyperion system
jobs.review_notes: Added 'nuclear' context to clarify this is a nuclear utility role; strengthened qualifications language to reference NRC regulations; 8 solid responsibility bullets confirm legitimate nuclear maintenance planning function.

structured_description.about: Constellation seeks a Maintenance Planner to develop and prepare work packages for maintenance departments and contractors at nuclear power plant facilities. You'll ensure safe, reliable, and efficient plant operations while managing corrective/preventive maintenance and modifications in compliance with company policies, NRC regulations, and applicable technical codes.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Prepare work instructions and work packages providing direction to the Maintenance Department for corrective maintenance repairs, preventive maintenance, and implementation of modifications
- Interface with Work Management, Supply, and Engineering to identify and resolve priorities and work package issues
- Define procedures, drawings, equipment, and materials required for performing planned work packages
- Conduct field walk-downs to identify and analyze corrective and preventive maintenance and modification activities
- Verify work instructions are adequate and comply with all applicable station procedures and regulatory requirements
- Review all required work packages and submit for approval before executing
- Manage multiple projects, establish work priorities, and achieve results
- Assist in planned and forced outage coordination as required
structured_description.qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in engineering with 2 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience, OR Associate's degree with 4 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience, OR High school diploma/GED with 6 years power plant maintenance and/or operations experience
- Maintain minimum access requirements and favorable medical examination/testing in accordance with position duties
- Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite applications
structured_description.desired:
- 5 years planning experience in a nuclear power plant environment
- Experience with Hyperion system

jobs.title: Control Room Operator
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. Total Rewards Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $99,000 to $110,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position We are a looking for a Control Room Operator to join our team. This person will be responsible for the safe and efficient operation of a cogeneration plant and its systems during their shift. This position is located in Washington, DC. The Combined Heat & Power (CHP) Facility consists of three Solar Mercury 50 combustion turbines, three Rentech Heat Recovery Steam Generators, one Cleaver-Brooks auxiliary boiler, and associated auxiliary equipment (compressors, pumps, etc.) Primary Duties and Accountabilities Monitor, record, and trend readings from the plant control system and steam generating equipment. Monitor gas turbine control panels to ensure proper operation of turbine equipment. Perform all required plant water chemistry analysis, adjust as necessary to maintain chemistry control within the plant specified limits. Operate the plant in a safe and efficient manner, following all procedures, health and safety guidelines, and current operating practices. Monitor instrumentation readings for variances from prescribed standards, adjusting as necessary to maintain proper equipment operation, and record abnormalities in the control room log. Maintain required records including the control room logbook and lockout/tagout log. Inspect equipment at regular intervals to identify malfunctions, repair needs, or unsafe conditions, and report issues beyond the troubleshooting or repair capabilities of shift personnel to management. Support safety by correcting hazards such as leaks, slip hazards, or deficiencies, and by monitoring maintenance activities to ensure proper plant operation. Perform minor maintenance, outage repairs, and equipment testing, and assist with cleanliness of plant spaces and common areas. Train and support new employees as required. Additional duties as assigned. Minimum Qualifications High school Diploma or GED DC Stationary Engineer's License (3rd Class) 5 years of power plant control room operations experience or equivalent education and experience Strong mechanical aptitude, basic steam plant and combustion turbine knowledge Ability to read and interpret safety rules, data, operating instructions, maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals in English Proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing English Ability to add, subtract, multiply, and divide in all units of measure, using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals Demonstrated ability to follow written, oral, or diagram-based instructions using sound judgment and practical understanding Demonstrated judgment and decision-making skills within defined procedures and practices, including use of calculations, deductive reasoning, and consultation with a supervisor Preferred Qualifications DC Stationary Engineer's License (1st Class)

Primary Purpose of Position We are a looking for a Control Room Operator to join our team. This person will be responsible for the safe and efficient operation of a cogeneration plant and its systems during their shift. This position is located in Washington, DC. The Combined Heat & Power (CHP) Facility consists of three Solar Mercury 50 combustion turbines, three Rentech Heat Recovery Steam Generators, one Cleaver-Brooks auxiliary boiler, and associated auxiliary equipment (compressors, pumps, etc.) Primary Duties and Accountabilities Monitor, record, and trend readings from the plant control system and steam generating equipment. Monitor gas turbine control panels to ensure proper operation of turbine equipment. Perform all required plant water chemistry analysis, adjust as necessary to maintain chemistry control within the plant specified limits. Operate the plant in a safe and efficient manner, following all procedures, health and safety guidelines, and current operating practices. Monitor instrumentation readings for variances from prescribed standards, adjusting as necessary to maintain proper equipment operation, and record abnormalities in the control room log. Maintain required records including the control room logbook and lockout/tagout log. Inspect equipment at regular intervals to identify malfunctions, repair needs, or unsafe conditions, and report issues beyond the troubleshooting or repair capabilities of shift personnel to management. Support safety by correcting hazards such as leaks, slip hazards, or deficiencies, and by monitoring maintenance activities to ensure proper plant operation. Perform minor maintenance, outage repairs, and equipment testing, and assist with cleanliness of plant spaces and common areas. Train and support new employees as required. Additional duties as assigned. Minimum Qualifications High school Diploma or GED DC Stationary Engineer's License (3rd Class) 5 years of power plant control room operations experience or equivalent education and experience Strong mechanical aptitude, basic steam plant and combustion turbine knowledge Ability to read and interpret safety rules, data, operating instructions, maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals in English Proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing English Ability to add, subtract, multiply, and divide in all units of measure, using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals Demonstrated ability to follow written, oral, or diagram-based instructions using sound judgment and practical understanding Demonstrated judgment and decision-making skills within defined procedures and practices, including use of calculations, deductive reasoning, and consultation with a supervisor

Preferred Qualifications DC Stationary Engineer's License (1st Class)
jobs.review_notes: This is a fossil fuel CHP/cogeneration facility role, not a nuclear energy position. Flagged as out of scope for Nuclear Hustle despite being a power plant control room operator role.

structured_description.about: Constellation operates a combined heat and power (CHP) cogeneration facility in Washington, DC serving the DC region. As Control Room Operator, you will safely and efficiently operate three Solar Mercury 50 combustion turbines, heat recovery steam generators, and auxiliary equipment during assigned shifts, monitoring plant systems, maintaining water chemistry, and ensuring compliance with all operating procedures and safety guidelines.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Monitor, record, and trend readings from plant control systems and steam generating equipment; monitor gas turbine control panels to ensure proper operation
- Perform plant water chemistry analysis and adjustments to maintain chemistry control within specified limits
- Operate plant in safe and efficient manner following all procedures, health and safety guidelines, and current operating practices
- Monitor instrumentation for variances from prescribed standards and adjust to maintain proper equipment operation
- Record abnormalities in control room log; maintain control room logbook and lockout/tagout log
- Inspect equipment at regular intervals to identify malfunctions, repair needs, or unsafe conditions
- Report issues beyond shift personnel troubleshooting capabilities to management
- Correct hazards (leaks, slip hazards, deficiencies) and monitor maintenance activities
- Perform minor maintenance, outage repairs, equipment testing, and assist with plant cleanliness
- Train and support new employees as required
structured_description.qualifications:
- High school diploma or GED
- DC Stationary Engineer's License (3rd Class)
- 5 years of power plant control room operations experience or equivalent education and experience
- Strong mechanical aptitude and basic steam plant and combustion turbine knowledge
- Ability to read and interpret safety rules, data, operating instructions, maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals in English
- Proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing English
- Ability to perform basic mathematical operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide) in all units of measure using whole numbers, fractions, and decimals
- Demonstrated ability to follow written, oral, or diagram-based instructions using sound judgment and practical understanding
- Demonstrated judgment and decision-making skills within defined procedures and practices
structured_description.desired: - DC Stationary Engineer's License (1st Class)
structured_description.location_details: Washington, DC; shift-based onsite operation

Please see minimum qualifications list below for each level*********************************** Associate Market Analyst - $68,400 to $76,000 Mid Market Analyst - $94,500 to $105,000 Sr Market Analyst - $114,300 to $127,000 Ranges are per year based on experience, along with a comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401K Primary Purpose of Position The Market Analyst will be responsible for understanding energy market conditions, tracking and evaluating programs and regulations, and developing insightful forecasts of the impacts of key trends. The role will focus on evaluating the effects of energy demand, commodity prices, renewable energy, emerging technologies, and changes to the generation fleet in U.S. energy markets. The objective of the role is to prepare Constellation to compete in a rapidly changing business environment. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Identifies market drivers and ensuring these are incorporated into short and long-term forecasts. Coordinates perspectives on market developments with stakeholders across Constellation and communicates emerging market dynamics to a broader commercial audience. Collaborates with Fundamentals, Structuring, Risk, and other teams to ensure consistent and coordinated market analysis. Runs and customizes off the shelf and proprietary analytical models, including tools such as Plexos, and evaluates modeling results. Communicates modeling results, insights, and implications in a clear and concise manner. Partners with team members to develop analytical tools, models, and market insights. Sr Market Analyst 5-years relevant experience Project management skills Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, or a related field Demonstrated ability to work effectively with ambiguous or abstract concepts and limited guidance Proven ability to perform quantitative analysis and apply strong mathematical and analytical skills to business or technical problems Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, including verbal and written Self-motivated to achieve results in a collaborative and rapidly evolving environment Preferred Qualifications Experience assessing market impacts and exposure to production cost and economic modeling Computer programming skills such as Python

structured_description.about: Senior technical specialist providing advanced engineering problem-solving for turbine-generator operations at Constellation's nuclear fleet. Lead technical decision-making, troubleshooting, and equipment resolution for Extended Power Uprate (EPU) projects while ensuring compliance with NRC regulations and industry codes, mentoring teams on continuous improvement.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Provide in-depth technical expertise to develop, manage, communicate, and implement engineering analysis for turbine-generator systems and EPU modifications
- Identify and resolve equipment and system problems through independent research, reviews, studies, and analyses
- Ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of work with proper configuration management and adherence to NRC regulations, standard design criteria, practices, procedures, and applicable codes (ASME, IEEE)
- Recommend equipment upgrades, new concepts, and techniques to improve performance, reduce costs, correct design flaws, or ensure regulatory compliance
- Enforce procedure use, Human Performance fundamentals, and Technical Human Performance techniques across assigned teams
- Provide coaching and mentoring to achieve continuous improvement in turbine operations and maintenance practices
structured_description.qualifications:

- Maintain NRC unescorted access requirements or equivalent security clearance
- Pass favorable medical examination and baseline/periodic testing in accordance with position duties
- Detailed knowledge of applicable NRC regulations, industry codes (ASME, IEEE), and nuclear safety requirements
structured_description.desired:
- Professional Engineer (PE) registration
- Advanced technical degree or related graduate coursework in nuclear engineering or mechanical engineering
- ASME certification or equivalent industry credentials

jobs.title: Sr Work Week Manager
jobs.description:
This position is located at Calvert Cliffs Nuclear in Lusby, MD. In addition to highly competitive salaries, we offer a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including a robust wellness program; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $140,400 to $156,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for directing the development of detailed plans/schedules for all corrective and preventative maintenance, surveillance, post maintenance test, and modifications within an assigned work week. Utilizes leadership experience from previous Operations supervisory or other department managerial position to efficiently and effectively carry out duties. During the execution week, responsible for ensuring the On-line Risk profile, Technical Specifications, and Clean Energy Center (CEC) Procedures are adhered to. Directs the implementation of the work week schedule in a safe, effective, and efficient manner during execution week. Crosses all organizational boundaries working with all departments and designated Project Managers to solve conflicts which challenge schedule safety and performance. Responsible for ensuring fleet and CEC goals are met during their respective execution week. Coaches and mentors' other members of Work Management and CEC personnel. This is not an automatic progression position for Work Week Managers. CECs fill the number of Sr. Work Week Manager positions based on business need and requires former supervisory or managerial experience. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Direct and Coordinate the detailed schedule development for the assigned work week to ensure Technical Specification and Maintenance Rule compliance and maintain online risk are coordinated to minimize safety challenges and effectively and efficiently implement the schedule. Direct, with minimal oversight, the assigned work week schedule execution ensuring Technical Specifications and Maintenance Rule compliance while maintaining online Risk are coordinated to minimize safety challenges and effectively and efficiently implement the schedule Provide direction, with minimal oversight, to all department planners, schedulers, Project Managers, and other support personnel for the development of an effective and accurate schedule execution. Direct schedule execution to meet the Work Management Process Key Indicators. Manage priority emergent work for their assigned work week. Evaluate scheduling execution performance analysis data and develop a weekly performance analysis report to assist in the development of corrective actions at the weekly Performance Accountability Review (PAR) meeting. Assist in forced outage coordination as required. Coach, mentor, and develop other members of the work management organization and fulfill requirements covered below in Job Scope section. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. In addition to your hourly wage, this role has additional earning potential through voluntary overtime and task work opportunities dependent on business need. $5,000 Sign-on Bonus Primary Purpose of Position The Plumbing Service Journeyman is responsible for installing, repairing, and maintaining plumbing fixtures and piping. This role should be able to demonstrate excellent diagnostic and problem-solving skills as well as achieve exceptional customer service. Routine tasks to include repair or replacement of faucets, toilets, garbage disposals, domestic water lines, and drain lines. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Inspect, install, repair, and replace pipes, fittings, and plumbing fixtures to maintain the water supply and waste disposal systems. Diagnose plumbing problems and work to resolve them efficiently. Ensure compliance with safety regulations, building codes, and applicable electrical and plumbing requirements during installation and repair, identifying and correcting issues as needed. Operate various tools and equipment such as pipe cutters, reamers, threading machines, and welding equipment. Provide rough estimates to customers on how much it will cost to repair damaged or malfunctioning fixtures or piping. Maintain an accurate record of all items/parts used to repair, replace, or maintain plumbing fixtures and piping. Promote the sale of other services offered by Constellation Home to customers. Minimum Qualifications High school Diploma or GED Journeyman trade license in Plumbing Experience working in plumbing service Valid gas fitter's license Demonstrated advanced knowledge of residential plumbing service, including local plumbing and gas codes, tools, and materials Advanced skills in the servicing of hot water and steam boilers Advanced ability in the usage of sewer cleaning machines Proven ability to deliver excellent customer service Excellent written and verbal communication skills Ability to work weekends, holidays, and overtime Average physical ability (bending, stopping, and climbing ladders) Ability to lift and carry objects of various weights Ability to work independently, collaboratively with minimal supervision Must have and maintain a valid driver's license

Routine tasks to include repair or replacement of faucets, toilets, garbage disposals, domestic water lines, and drain lines. Diagnose plumbing problems and work to resolve them efficiently. jobs.title: FMLA Coordinator
jobs.location: Warrenville, Illinois; Baltimore, Maryland; Kennett Square, Pennsylvania; Houston, Texas; Chicago, Illinois
jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. TOTAL REWARDS Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $70,200 to $78,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION The FMLA Coordinator supports the in-house leave management process for all FMLA and company paid leave requests for both represented and non-represented employees. The position uses knowledge of Federal and State leave laws and regulations as well as company paid leave programs to manage cases and assist employees, supervisors and HR Partners with questions about applying for leave and available leave programs available. Employees will be expected to work from an assigned company office for moments that matter, but most work will be performed remotely. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES · Coordinate in-house FMLA cases by confirming employee eligibility and entitlement, reviewing medical certifications and leave request forms, requesting additional information when needed, approving or denying leave, and issuing required communications using the AbsenceSoft leave management system. · Review short-term disability cases managed by Occupational Health Services nurses for FMLA approval. · Manage requests for company-paid benefits, including paid parental leave, bonding leave, and primary caregiver leave, by confirming employees meet company eligibility requirements. · Identify and approve or deny intermittent FMLA absences to ensure requests do not exceed approved frequency and duration parameters. · Review and process state paid family and medical leave forms and coordinate requests with third-party vendors by providing employment, payroll, and other required information. · Respond to employee, supervisor, and HR inquiries regarding leave processes and leave benefits. · Participate in team meetings to ensure consistency in leave administration and recommend improvements to the overall employee leave experience. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS · High school Diploma or GED · 4-years customer service experience in a corporate setting · Proficient computer background/experience, including Microsoft Office Applications (Word, Excel and PowerPoint) · Strong communication and customer service skills · Proven ability to work effectively both independently and collaboratively in team environments · Strong critical thinking skills PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS · Working knowledge of federal and state leave laws across all states · Leave administration experience · Experience with AbsenceSoft leave management software

Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for the direction, assignment, and coordination of Turbine Floor Outage Execution to support safe, reliable, and efficient outage activities consistent with Constellation and Clean Energy Center (CEC) goals. Enforce at all times procedure use and adherence, Human Performance (HU) fundamentals, Technical Human Performance (THU) techniques, and provide coaching and mentoring as necessary to achieve continuous improvement. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Effectively direct turbine floor personnel to assure all assigned maintenance activities are performed safely and effectively, by qualified workers, and in accordance with procedures and schedules. Participate in various aspects of work control coordination, scheduling, and engineering meetings involving online and refuel outage activities, to ensure that work is properly assigned, planned and ready to execute. Effectively communicate and address CEC and personnel issues to the appropriate levels of Outage Services & Alliances (OS&A) and CEC management. Foster open communications and collaboration between all organizations internal and external to OS&A. Oversee project activities of multidisciplinary team regarding turbine generator disassembly and reassembly, coordination of turbine deck lay-down, mobilization of equipment and tooling as well as interfacing with management and specific contract personnel. Satisfy contractual obligations, meet project milestones, and optimize short-term and long-term company profit by effectively managing and facilitating resources. Understand, support, and administer all OS&A processes, such as the training program, surveillance program, procedure revisions, and Corrective Action Program (CAP) to ensure effective work practices. Identify and support improvement opportunities and development, implementation, and performance monitoring of all Business Goals and objectives. Directs the implementation of the work week schedule in a safe, effective, and efficient manner schedule during execution week. Evaluate scheduling execution performance analysis data and develop a weekly performance analysis report to assist in the development of corrective actions at the weekly Performance Analysis Review (P.A.R) meeting. Assist in forced outage coordination as required. Coach, mentor, and develop other members of the work management organization. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Expected salary range of $202,500 to $258,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position The Captain reports to the Chief Pilot. The Captain/PIC is responsible for conducting safe, efficient flight operations in accordance with all applicable CFRs, the AIM, AFM, the Constellation AOM, and applicable country/local regulations. The Captain/PIC supervises the copilot/SIC and other crewmembers assigned to the trip. Constellation Captains provide excellent customer service, are solutions focused, and communicate with other members of the Transportation Department, the company, and outside vendors as needed to accomplish assigned trips. Primary Duties and Accountabilities The Captain carries primary responsibility for the safe transportation of passengers and additional crewmembers and emphasizes a strong safety culture within the Transportation Department. Participates in safety meetings, incident debriefings, and implements safety recommendations. Complies with all security protocols to protect passengers, crew, and the aircraft, and remains prepared to handle emergency situations in flight or on the ground. Maintains a working knowledge of the Emergency Response Plan and applies the correct initial response actions during emergency drills or actual emergencies. Participates in the Safety Management System (SMS) by complying with all regulations, safety best practices, and industry protocols. Utilizes the Safety Report process, Continuous Improvement process, and other procedures to ensure a healthy safety culture exists within the Transportation Department. Assists the Chief Pilot in ensuring all flights and flight crew members adhere to relevant aviation regulations, company policies, and standard operating procedures. May be assigned additional duties within the Transportation Department to train and evaluate flight crew members, including pilots and other operational personnel. Provides technical guidance and support, oversees training programs and records, offers ongoing coaching and feedback, conducts performance evaluations, and fosters a positive work environment. Maintain accurate and comprehensive records in the CRM for all business development activities. Identifies opportunities for process improvements, cost reductions, and increased efficiency within flight operations. Implements new technologies, training programs, or best practices to enhance productivity and safety. Accepts and performs additional duties as assigned by the Chief Pilot. Minimum Qualifications 4 years of experience as a corporate, commercial, or military pilot Holds a valid Airline Transport Pilot License and a valid Instrument Rating for the category of aircraft operated Must maintain PIC qualifications on at least one type of company aircraft Knowledge of the content of the Aviation Operations Manual, Training Manuals, Standard Operating Procedures, and the provisions of Titles 14 and 49 of the CFRs and standards necessary to carry out the duties and responsibilities of the position Must be able to demonstrate the ability to lift 40 pounds to shoulder level due to baggage loading and unloading requirements Preferred Qualifications Holds a type rating for at least one of the following types of aircraft: DA-2EASY or G-VII Bachelor's degree

jobs.title: Site Superintendent
jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. TOTAL REWARDS Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $91,800 to $102,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION Constellation New Energy is seeking a candidate to fill a position of Site Superintendent at our plant facilities. This individual will be the sole representative of Constellation onsite at our customer's manufacturing facility. The SS will be responsible for the operation and maintenance of 2 chillers and 26 rooftop air handling units, I&C controls, as well as handling preventative and corrective maintenance of equipment. Additionally, they will oversee contractors and subcontractors for repairs to equipment and maintain and execute maintenance schedules to ensure equipment reliability and environmental compliance. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES Act as sole Constellation representative onsite at customer's facility. Maintain customer relationships with all customers served by assigned plants Operate assigned plants as a Stationary Engineer in compliance with published Company operating and safety policies/procedures. Assure the safe, efficient, and continuous commodity supply to meet system demands. Manage 3rd party service providers, contractors; water treatment service providers, maintenance / repair contractors. Monitor contractors onsite to ensure equipment reliability and environmental compliance requirements are met. Maintain & monitor safe working conditions; identifies, corrects, and reports unsafe work areas, enforces safety compliance. Monitor assigned plant operations through various controls systems/technologies. Ability to be on-call and respond to emergency situations, as necessary � Monitor for system disruption alarms during off hours and ability to remotely reset equipment, as required. Perform preventative and predictive maintenance and minor repair on plant equipment and air handling units as assigned Maintain complete maintenance records and reports. Write and follow-up on work orders for identification and repair of plant equipment and air handling units. Assure water quality using all prescribed water management procedures at assigned plant Participate in and successfully complete all mandatory training to ensure compliance with published Company operating and safety policies and procedures MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS Grade 3 Maryland Stationary Engineer license. High school diploma and 8+ years of experience in operating centrifugal chillers or equivalent combination of education and relevant experience. Instrumentation and Controls (I&C) Experience. Mechanical and electrical aptitude, including working with switching up to 480 volts. Knowledge of Johnson Controls Metasys software or similar controls software. Knowledge of Systecon software or similar PLC software. Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from managers, and clients/customers. Strong planning and organizational skills. General computer proficiency i.e., word processing, spreadsheets, and basic use of MS Office tools. Ability to work effectively with a minimum level of supervision. Ability to define problems and deal with a variety of variables in situations by collecting data, establishing facts, and drawing valid conclusions. Ability to complete all required paperwork and documentation in a correct and timely manner. A customer focused, team player with strong communication and interpersonal skills Self-starter, able to work independently and set priorities. Must possess a valid driver's license and the ability to operate a motor vehicle. Ability to climb and tolerate heights up to 75 feet and to tolerate confined spaces. Ability to lift up to 65lbs. Must be able to enter confined spaces. Infrequent travel requiring overnight stays. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Operator experience in food production or pharmaceuticals facilities, to include chiller, rooftop air handling units & HVAC air handling units. Grade 1 Maryland Stationary Engineer license.

Expected salary range of $108,00 to $120,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position The Senior Maintenance Scheduler (Daily/Outage) is responsible for the development and control of the discipline schedules including balancing the workload with the available resources with the emphasis on schedule consistency and workability, which supports meeting the On-line and Outage indicators. Successfully maintaining accurate schedules throughout development and execution and updating and publishing the discipline schedule as required. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Coordinate, develop and Update discipline schedules to ensure an accurate product for use in effective planning, resource allocation, parts acquisition, and schedule execution. Develop detailed accurate and usable schedules and report to provide effective management tools for execution and feedback. Attend scheduling meetings to ensure pre-planning, implementation, and schedule critiques are effective in developing continuous product improvement. Assist in the development of work windows and contingency plans, by working with Work Week Managers, Project Manager and Cycle Schedulers. Assist assigned department with traveler requests and tracking. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. Sr. Maintenance Scheduler Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree and 5 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) license with 5 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 7 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirement or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Maintenance Scheduler Minimum Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree with 2 years of related experience OR Current or previous Senior Reactor Operator license with 2 years of related experience OR Associate’s degree with 3 years of related experience OR High school diploma/GED with 5 years of related experience Maintain minimum access requirements or unescorted access requirements, as applicable, and favorable medical examination and/or testing in accordance with position duties Preferred Qualifications Maintenance experience

jobs.title: Drexel Accounting Analyst Co-op (Fall/Winter 2026) - Philadelphia
jobs.description:
Constellation is the nation’s largest producer of carbon-free energy and sustainable solutions to millions of homes, institutional customers, the public sector, community aggregations and businesses, including three fourths of Fortune 100 companies. Our generation fleet of nuclear, hydro, wind, and solar generation facilities powers more than 20 million homes and businesses, providing 10 percent of all clean power on the grid in the U.S. All told, our fleet is helping to accelerate the nation’s transition to clean energy with more than 32,400 megawatts of capacity and annual output that is nearly 90 percent carbon-free. We provide reliable access to clean power whenever our customers need it, 24 hours per day and 7 days per week. It's important work powered by people with a common set of values. At Constellation, we elevate diverse voices and fresh perspectives, and together – we grow jobs into careers and build a better tomorrow. Join Constellation and share your passion at a forward-thinking Fortune 200 company. Establish yourself in a place where you can truly shine and create a brighter, more sustainable tomorrow. Energize your career at Constellation! This role will perform accounting and financial analysis as well as utility bill data analysis tasks. The ideal candidate will have strong technical skills and experience working with financial data. They will also possess excellent communication and problem-solving abilities. Energy-industry experience or engineering-related major/minor is a plus. Responsibilities • Analyze and interpret financial data as well as utility bill data. • Perform reconciliation analyses • Develop and prepare financial reports and accruals • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. • Proficient In Microsoft Excel • Strong communication skills

jobs.description:

Expected salary range of $149,400 to $166,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION The LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas) Maintenance Manager is responsible for managing and coordinating asset integrity, maintenance, inspection and testing activities to assure continued safe and compliant operation of the Terminal. Daily supervision, monitoring and coaching of maintenance personnel to ensure a productive workforce in compliance with company policies. Responsibilities include work planning, on-the-job training, arranging for maintenance work, inspections, and initiating requisitions for procurement of tools, materials, equipment and parts from vendors and services from contractors. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES Prepare and execute an effective and optimized predictive, preventive, and corrective maintenance program utilizing planning and scheduling systems and techniques to minimize downtime and reactive work. Responsible for the organization and direction of the maintenance department. Directs the planning, scheduling, and managing of all maintenance activities. Lead, develop, and implement personnel development, performance management, and labor relations in accordance with the CBA as applicable. Administer company and Terminal policies/procedures. Participate in Terminal strategic and business planning initiatives. Responsible for supporting the development of and managing the maintenance budget, ensuring efficient resources allocation, cost control and maintaining Terminal availability. Ensure compliance and effective communications with all federal, state, and local regulatory regulations and industry standards, maintaining accurate records, preparing maintenance reports and maintain environmental stewardship. Reviews and approves design and future maintenance for engineering and construction project proposals to achieve or exceed levels of production, improve maintenance troubleshooting, decrease production interruptions and improve maintenance productivity. Ensures that maintenance planning, scheduling, long-term maintenance certification and inspection activities including condition monitoring are occurring as scheduled. Drive continuous improvement of maintenance work practices in reducing maintenance backlog and focus on learning opportunities. Engage and support Terminal compliance audits, incident investigations and review of working practices. JOB SCOPE Position reports to Senior Manager, Operations & Maintenance and is part of the Terminal leadership team. Develop a highly, multi-skilled and motivated team through mentoring, coaching, training, and effective communication. Manage direct reports with supervisory responsibilities for represented and/or non-represented employees. Administration of the Company-Union labor Agreement (CBA) supporting maintenance productivity and maintain high levels of morale and motivation among maintenance personnel at all times. Coordinate and lead planning meetings, interact with Terminal operations and engineering to ensure maintenance activities are executed in close collaboration, maintaining Terminal availability and reliable operations. Direct activities and approve all actions to maintain and enhance the Terminal's equipment and facilities, incorporating engineering techniques to affect improvements in equipment reliability, availability and corrosion control. Ensures budgets and schedules meet financial targets. Ensures the Terminal's maintenance program for equipment is functional and compliant. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS BS in engineering with minimum 10 years professional experience OR Associates Degree with 10 years’ experience with 5 at the supervisory level OR H.S. Diploma with 12 years’ experience with 5 at the supervisory level Minimum 8 years power plant (or related) experience Minimum of 5 years supervisory/managerial experience Ability to acquire applicable State License in required time period Experience in one of the following: Engineering or Maintenance or Work Management Experience as one of the following: Operator or Foreman or Shift Manager Professional Certification or Recognition Demonstrated technical knowledge of LNG or Power plant operations. Knowledge of State, Local and Federal environment regulations as they apply to LNG or Power plant operations. High level knowledge of rotating and operating equipment, pumps, generators, compressors, tanks, vaporizers/heat exchangers, electrical switchgear, motor control centers, VFDs, actuated valves, transmitters, PLC and DCS systems. Leadership capabilities, able to engage others in the required process and hold people accountable. Posses the personality and ability to relate to and to establish a mutually respectful relationship with management, peers, direct reports and Terminal personnel, responsible for ensuring effective and safe operations. Exceptional problem solving, analytical and decision-making skills, with the ability to handle complex situations. Strong organizational and planning skills, able to meet established deadlines, able to manage multiple activities in timely fashion and with a high degree of accuracy. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with diverse teams and collaborate in a cross functional environment. Ability to be a participative leader that offers opinion and direction. Strong computer skills, including knowledge and proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite products PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Master's Degree in engineering, business, or related field Multi-site experience Staff Maintenance or Operations Or Engineering experience Collective bargaining experience FLS experience Previous experience leading a Maintenance team LNG and Cryogenic experience Knowledgeable of LNG specific codes and standards

PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION The LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas) Maintenance Manager is responsible for managing and coordinating asset integrity, maintenance, inspection and testing activities to assure continued safe and compliant operation of the Terminal. Daily supervision, monitoring and coaching of maintenance personnel to ensure a productive workforce in compliance with company policies

- Drive continuous improvement initiatives to reduce maintenance backlog and optimize work practices
- Support terminal compliance audits, incident investigations, and review of working practices
- Coordinate with terminal operations and engineering teams to ensure collaborative maintenance execution

- Minimum 8 years power plant or related facility experience
- Minimum 5 years supervisory or managerial experience
- Ability to acquire applicable state license within required timeframe

- Experience as operator, foreman, or shift manager
- Technical knowledge of LNG or power plant operations

jobs.title: Drexel Environmental Services Co-op (Fall/Winter 2026) - Eddystone
jobs.description:
Constellation is the nation’s largest producer of carbon-free energy and sustainable solutions to millions of homes, institutional customers, the public sector, community aggregations and businesses, including three fourths of Fortune 100 companies. Our generation fleet of nuclear, hydro, wind, and solar generation facilities powers more than 20 million homes and businesses, providing 10 percent of all clean power on the grid in the U.S. All told, our fleet is helping to accelerate the nation’s transition to clean energy with more than 32,400 megawatts of capacity and annual output that is nearly 90 percent carbon-free. We provide reliable access to clean power whenever our customers need it, 24 hours per day and 7 days per week. It's important work powered by people with a common set of values. jobs.title: Sr HR Business Partner
jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE As the largest private-sector power producer in the world and the nation's largest producer of clean and reliable energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: lighting the way to a brilliant tomorrow for all. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Now integrated with Calpine, our portfolio includes 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind and solar facilities, with the generating capacity to power the equivalent of 27 million homes. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things. Join us in meeting the country's energy needs today and tomorrow. TOTAL REWARDS Constellation offers an extensive selection of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and a wide-range of benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $93,600 to $104,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). PRIMARY PURPOSE OF POSITION Partnering with all levels of leadership within the business unit, aligns HR strategy with business goals, direction and vision. Provide proactive professional HR guidance and support to internal clients with goal of adding value to the overall business as well as impacting the Constellation enterprise in attainment of strategy. Working under general direction, supports assigned client group and implements HR Strategy, in partnership with HR leadership, to support business unit goals and objectives. Supports the change management process across the HR organization as well as directly with respective client groups. PRIMARY DUTIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES Champion employee engagement and workforce effectiveness planning and initiative implementation in partnership with the business and HR specialty areas, such as talent acquisition and diversity and inclusion, etc. Partner with business leadership to provide consultative guidance, develop and implement plans informed by organizational capabilities, structure, processes and competencies in alignment with business strategy Liaison to specialty HR functions and centers of expertise for optimal client support. Lead or participate in enterprise-wide initiatives including, but not limited to mergers and acquisitions, HR and business transformation projects, as well as support and sustain change. Drive integrated process focused on workforce and talent planning through assessment of talent pipeline and strategizing to foster strength, in partnership with HR leadership. Evaluate and resolve employee relations issues, as well as evaluate and develop strategy for trends. Provide input to COEs with goal of continuous improvement and innovation MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor of Science degree in Human Resources or related field 5 to 8 years of strong HR operations experience or HR specialist area in a matrix organization. Ability to provide verbal and written communications clearly, succinctly and persuasively. Knowledge of federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations, including knowledge of EEO, ADA and FMLA. Ability to understand problems in the context of the bigger picture and influential relationships as well as the overall industry and marketplace in which Constellation operates. Strong drive for results to lead and facilitate efforts to ensure human resources alignment with the business unit. Proven skills in coaching and performance management, Demonstrated ability to deal directly with client leads to build consensus, establish trust, communicate effectively and foster culture change. Ability to use research methods effectively including data collection and analysis PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Advanced degree, MBA or Masters Degree in related HR field PHR or SPHR

Primary Purpose of Position Responsible for directing the development of detailed plans/schedules for all corrective and preventative maintenance, surveillance, post maintenance test, and modifications within an assigned work week. Utilizes leadership experience from previous Operations supervisory or other department managerial position to efficiently and effectively carry out duties. During the execution week, responsible for ensuring the On-line Risk profile, Technical Specifications, and Clean Energy Center (CEC) Procedures are adhered to. Directs the implementation of the work week schedule in a safe, effective, and efficient manner during execution week. Crosses all organizational boundaries working with all departments and designated Project Managers to solve conflicts which challenge schedule safety and performance. Responsible for ensuring fleet and CEC goals are met during their respective execution week. Coaches and mentors' other members of Work Management and CEC personnel. This is not an automatic progression position for Work Week Managers. CECs fill the number of Sr. Work Week Manager positions based on business need and requires former supervisory or managerial experience. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Direct and Coordinate the detailed schedule development for the assigned work week to ensure Technical Specification and Maintenance Rule compliance and maintain online risk are coordinated to minimize safety challenges and effectively and efficiently implement the schedule. Evaluate scheduling execution performance analysis data and develop a weekly performance analysis report to assist in the development of corrective actions at the weekly Performance Accountability Review (PAR) meeting. Assist in forced outage coordination as required. Coach, mentor, and develop other members of the work management organization and fulfill requirements covered below in Job Scope section. Perform other job assignments and duties as directed by management or pursuant to company policy, including but not limited to emergency response, departmental coverage, call outs, and support of outage activities in positions outside the department. jobs.description:

Expected salary range of $40.67 to $45.19, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position Join Constellation as a Plant Mechanic and be a key player in our Combined Heat and Power Facility! As a Plant Mechanic you will be responsible for the inspection, maintenance, testing, and repair of plant equipment and systems, ensuring optimal performance and safety. Plant equipment includes valves, pumps, blowers, compressors, heat exchangers, boilers, and other associated equipment. Duties and responsibilities may include other associated tasks as directed by the Combined Heat and Power Plant Manager. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Perform preventative and predictive maintenance and minor repair on plant equipment as assigned. Assist in planning and scheduling work for outages. Assist in the administration and execution of the preventative maintenance program. Follow all approved plant operating, maintenance and health and safety procedures. Perform other duties, as assigned. Other duties may include assisting with mechanical maintenance, equipment calibrations, general housekeeping, etc. Maintain & monitor safe working conditions; identifies, corrects, and reports unsafe work areas, enforces safety compliance. Performs mechanical installations, repairs, and replacements as needed. Complete on-time delivery of maintenance services while ensuring compliance with company standards for cost control, quality, safety, efficiency, and equipment reliability. Perform maintenance tasks such as lubrication, inspection, mechanical alignment, and vibration monitoring, coordinating with Operations staff to ensure continuity of service. Source parts, manage inventory, and coordinate with other trade personnel or contractors to support maintenance activities. Work overtime on a regular basis to support plant outages, emergency work, and other critical needs. Minimum Qualifications High school diploma or equivalent 5-years power plant or industrial plant, I & C maintenance experience Technical training through a trade school or military program Strong mechanical aptitude, basic steam plant and combustion turbine knowledge Proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing English Demonstrated judgment and decision-making skills within defined procedures and practices, including use of calculations, deductive reasoning, and consultation with a supervisor Regularly required to stand, walk, climb, talk, hear and use hands. Ability to climb ladders, reach with hands and arms to climb and balance Ability to stoop, crawl, and bend at knees and waist. Lift up to 50 lbs Vision abilities include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, and depth perception Ability to read and interpret safety rules, data, operating instructions, maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals in English Ability to read and interpret block diagrams, electrical schematics, and P&IDs

Clarified that this is a CHP facility role and emphasized mechanical maintenance focus relevant to power generation.

structured_description.about: Senior Mechanic at Constellation's Combined Heat and Power (CHP) facility. This role is responsible for inspection, maintenance, testing, and repair of critical plant equipment including valves, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, and boilers. You will perform both preventative and predictive maintenance while ensuring optimal performance, safety, and regulatory compliance across power generation systems.
structured_description.responsibilities:
- Perform preventative and predictive maintenance and minor repairs on plant equipment (valves, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, boilers)
- Perform mechanical installations, repairs, and replacements; conduct lubrication, inspection, mechanical alignment, and vibration monitoring
- Assist in planning, scheduling, and execution of work outages and the preventative maintenance program
- Maintain and monitor safe working conditions; identify, correct, and report unsafe work areas; enforce safety compliance
- Coordinate with Operations staff to ensure service continuity and manage parts inventory
- Complete maintenance services on time while meeting company standards for cost control, quality, safety, and equipment reliability
- Work overtime regularly to support plant outages, emergency work, and critical operational needs
structured_description.qualifications:
- High school diploma or equivalent
- 5+ years power plant or industrial plant mechanical maintenance experience
- Technical training through trade school or military program
- Strong mechanical aptitude and basic steam plant and combustion turbine knowledge
- Proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing English
- Ability to read and interpret safety rules, block diagrams, electrical schematics, and P&IDs
- Demonstrated judgment and decision-making skills within defined procedures
- Physical ability to stand, walk, climb, stoop, crawl, lift up to 50 lbs, and reach with hands and arms
- Vision abilities including close/distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, and depth perception

We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. In addition to highly competitive salaries, eligible employees are offered a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including robust wellbeing programs; disability and life insurance benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $102,600 to $114,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position The Senior Analyst, Credit Risk is responsible for a credit portfolio within the designated regions. This job is also responsible for assessing and analyzing retail power and gas credit issues for the retail supply business. As a member of the credit organization, this position will require an advanced understanding of the physical and financial products offered by the Company and the marketplace in order to properly assess and monitor counterparty credit/risk issues. The incumbent is closely aligned with the business and functional leaders throughout the organization and is expected to foster and maintain strong working partnerships with their respective peers. This position requires interaction with Sales organization, Operations, Corporate Accounting, Corporate Treasury and Corporate Risk. Primary Duties and Accountabilities Assess and analyze the credit worthiness of customers and trading counterparties, and monitor current and potential exposures. Recommend credit terms and conditions within customer and wholesale commodity contracts. Coordinate with the margining and treasury teams on counterpart collateral including cash, letters of credit and parental guarantees. Also coordinates effectively with Receivables team to monitor aged receivables to ensure timely collection. Assist on presentations to Senior Management that include credit decisions and recommendations, while highlighting top credit risks within the organization. As directed by Credit leadership, lead the coordination, maintenance, and implementation of improved reporting and functionality of Credit Risk systems. jobs.title: Senior Executive Protection Security Specialist
jobs.description:
Who We Are As the nation's largest producer of clean, carbon-free energy, Constellation is focused on our purpose: accelerating the transition to a carbon-free future. We have been the leader in clean energy production for more than a decade, and we are cultivating a workplace where our employees can grow, thrive, and contribute. Our culture and employee experience make it clear: We are powered by passion and purpose. Together, we're creating healthier communities and a cleaner planet, and our people are the driving force behind our success. At Constellation, you can build a fulfilling career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact. By doing our best work and meeting new challenges, we can accomplish great things and help fight climate change. Join us to lead the clean energy future. Total Rewards Constellation offers a wide range of benefits and rewards to help our employees thrive professionally and personally. We provide competitive compensation and benefits that support both employees and their families, helping them prepare for the future. In addition to highly competitive salaries, we offer a bonus program, 401(k) with company match, employee stock purchase program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits, including a robust wellness program; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. Expected salary range of $115,200 to $128,000, varies based on experience, along with comprehensive benefits package that includes bonus and 401(k). Primary Purpose of Position � Engage in job duties outlined below, to reduce risk exposure in areas of cyber and physical security; and to promote our mission of safeguarding the people, property, reputation and shareholder value of the corporation. � Provide secure tactical driving services for Constellation executives with 24/7 availability. � Conduct counter-surveillance services for select Constellation executives. � Maintain the highest level of confidentiality and discretion regarding all corporate matters, especially executive conversations and communications. � Provide security/protective services for events held by or attended by select Constellation executives and ensure a smooth transition from each venue. � Conduct dignitary advances, security assessments and investigations on an as needed basis Interface with and maintain a working relationship with federal, state and local law enforcement professionals, executive protection specialists in other corporations, and vendors providing executive protection services. � Lead, plan and provide protection services for international travel Provide residential vulnerability assessment services, including the coordination of security system installations/upgrades, to select executives. � Conduct independent, complex, impactful, highly confidential investigations throughout the Corporation Determine and pursue courses of action necessary to obtain desired investigative results and make recommendations in support of correcting internal controls issues and/or policy/procedural investigative as well as information-seeking interviews of employees and non-employees, document investigations, handle evidence, and maintain liaison status with internal and external resources to achieve favorable results that support Constellation's business units and efforts. � Provide controls and risk mitigation recommendations to management. � Prepare clear, comprehensive, and cohesive investigative reports based upon established procedures. � Engage regulators and councils on critical infrastructure. � Act as a liaison to law enforcement networks. Establish and maintain federal, state, county and local law enforcement contacts and relationships.
