jobs.title: Core Structural Materials Engineering Manager
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Core Structural Materials Engineering Manager

The Natrium Core Structural Materials Engineering Manager reports directly to the Senior Manager, Core Material Qualification. This position is focused on managing the development and qualification of Natrium reactor core assembly materials, establishing a supply chain, and providing material performance data to the design organization. In this role, you will manage a dedicated team of engineers developing material performance tools and performing analysis and testing activities in support of TerraPower’s advanced nuclear reactor project. This position also supports the senior manager in project scheduling and reporting activities.

Responsibilities

Oversee the development and deployment of material performance models for metallic materials used to fabricate reactor core assembly components

Approve material property datasets generated via Qualification of Existing Data per ASME NQA-1-2015

Oversee materials test programs to validate models of material performance and ensure materials development and testing efforts meet programmatic objectives of the Natrium reactor

Provide materials engineering support for the procurement of engineered components and systems including leading technical development of suppliers to increase the technology readiness level of key components

Oversee development, optimization, and qualification of new materials for sodium fast reactor applications

Coordinate with the Fuel Development and Qualification, Fuel Fabrication and Core Design groups to develop fuel technologies supporting Natrium Kemmerer Unit 1 and future commercial reactor concepts

Inform and support the licensing process for advanced reactor fuel

Work with the Senior Manager and Project Management team to develop and maintain the schedule and budget

Manage the Core Materials Qualification team to deliver project work within schedule and cost constraints

Communicate the status of engineering progress to the team, stakeholders and management

Assure compliance to procedures, standards and regulatory criteria

Ensure that engineering work is performed with a high level of integrity through rigorous enforcement of work processes and technical reviews of team deliverables

Demonstrate visible, continuous leadership in the areas of safety and Quality

Establish and enforce procedures to ensure a safe working environment

Implement quality assurance policy and requirements for the project

Interface with regulators, stakeholders, and customers on materials engineering design issues

Recommend staff changes (new additions, replacements due to attrition, de-staffing) and work with Human Resources to manage approved staffing changes

Support the development of direct reports through mentoring, training, and challenging assignments

Key Qualifications and Skills

8+ years of related nuclear experience and a Bachelor’s degree in a relevant engineering discipline from an accredited university; or 8 years and a Master’s degree; or a PhD with 5 years experience; or equivalent experience

Proven track record in successful management and leadership with 2 years of previous supervisory or mentoring experience or equivalent people leadership experience

A solid understanding of metallurgy including fabrication and thermomechanical processing of ferrous (e.g., ferritic-martensitic steels) and nonferrous (e.g., nickel-base, refractory) alloys

Advanced knowledge and experience in nuclear quality programs, design control, and configuration management principles, practices, and tools

Experience working in stringent quality systems (e.g., ISO-9001, AS9100) is required, experience working to ASME NQA-1, 10 CFR 50 Appendix B highly desired

A general understanding of the design of reactor plant and related systems and experience with sodium cooled fast reactors would be considered a plus

Self-starter and possess ability to perform both individually and as a team contributor; proven ability to direct small teams with minimal oversight

A high degree of trust and integrity, able to communicate openly, and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork

Proven track record in planning, budgeting, and execution of testing or modeling activities

Effective verbal and written communication skills

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.title: Corporate Counsel
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Corporate Counsel, Commercial

 

TerraPower is seeking a Corporate Counsel to provide legal support on commercial and regulatory matters for TerraPower. The Corporate Counsel will report to the Associate eneral Counsel, and work with the Associate General Counsel and the other TerraPower lawyers (together, the “TerraPower Lawyers”) as requested. The Corporate Counsel will work collaboratively with TerraPower’s business and technology personnel, outside counsel and counsel for TerraPower’s suppliers, R&D collaborators, and other contractors both in the US and abroad.

 

Responsibilities:

Provides programmatic support for the TerraPower Lawyers, and reports to the Associate General Counsel.

Prepares drafts of agreements and other documents and handles both routine and first line non-routine contract review and negotiations in support of the Procurement team and Contracts team.

Creates and formats documents electronically including letters, memos, status reports, presentations (PowerPoint), spreadsheets (Excel), and other related documents.

Provides legal support for TerraPower government contracts and grants and associated compliance requirements.

Prepares updates to and provides administration of legal policies and procedures in consultation with other TerraPower Lawyers.

Supports the creation of, and updates to, standard contract templates for TerraPower’s business.

Files, tracks and updates a variety of business and legal templates and documents.

Understands the nature of the business in order to provide value-added service and enhance working relationships.

Facilitates daily contact with other TerraPower Lawyers and other TerraPower personnel via email, phone and videoconference.

Manages external work with outside lawyers and others regarding matters on which the TerraPower legal team is working.

Initiates and sustains continuous improvement in TerraPower contracting advice, negotiations, templates, drafting, implementation and management, undertaking negotiations and contract management when needed.

Initiates and implements technological improvements to streamline legal review and support.

Determines the priority and responds appropriately and coordinates communications with stakeholders. Makes recommendations as needed regarding appropriate action and follow-up.

Conducts legal and other research (electronic and other resources), creating deliverables ready to share directly with the other functions within TerraPower.

Provides back-up support to other TerraPower Lawyers and other legal team members.

Participates actively in group meetings to understand short and long term goals of legal team and the business.

Coordinates communication and service requests from various parts of the TerraPower business.

Performs other tasks and projects as requested from time to time by the TerraPower Lawyers and in support of the TerraPower business.

Advises on legal and regulatory/export control issues.

Coordinates with intellectual property team on intellectual property issues.

Manages outside counsel.

Periodic domestic travel may be required.

 

Qualifications and Skills:

Minimum of 5 years’ relevant experience, including law firm experience, preferably in commercial contracting, technology, and/or energy sectors, and/or inhouse experience; experience in the nuclear sector a plus.

Strong contract and commercial law experience, including technology transactions. 

Experience drafting and negotiating government contracts, grant agreements and related supporting contracts.

Experience documenting complex contracts and business transactions in a clear and organized manner.

Familiarity with regulatory matters, including export control, preferably in the nuclear context.

Ability to manage outside counsel effectively and efficiently.

Demonstrated creativity in legal approaches to complex problems and issues that balance business needs and legal risks.

History of integrity, sound legal and business judgment and an ability to communicate effectively.

Entrepreneurial mindset and ability to work independently across time zones and cultures to quickly address novel situations and change.

Technical background or proven ability and interest in learning new technologies.

J.D. required with license to practice in at least one state. Member of the Washington State Bar, or eligible for admission via in-house rule APR 8(f) which must be obtained promptly after start date.

The successful candidate will have a high degree integrity, communicate openly and display respect, be trustworthy and have a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

CAP implementation Specialist

The CAP implementation specialist reports directly to the Procedure Manager and will fill one or more of the following roles to support the Natrium Demonstration Project (NDP) based on qualifications and experience. The Corrective Action Specialist is responsible for the day-to-day operations of the Kemmerer Power Station (KPS) Corrective Action Program including analyzing corrective action data and utilizing the data to understand causes for quality, safety, and business process impacts. The ideal candidate will have experience in issue identification, resolution, categorization, cause analysis, preventive action, and trend analysis. The successful candidate will integrate and coordinate with other departments, project partners, regulators, and outside organizations working on procedure development.

Responsibilities

Develop and execute a plan for configuring and implementing the Kemmerer Power Station (KPS) CAP software.

Develop and maintain processes and standards for the KPS corrective action program

Coordinate with other departments for cross-discipline support of software and CAP development

Use smart procedures or other software-based support tools to develop and maintain the Corrective Action Program

Consider and integrate use of cross-trained resources in Software development to improve organizational efficiency.

Identify existing industry guidance for processes that can be adopted with minimal modifications to meet KPS goals

Use industry practices to develop high-quality procedures which minimize risk of safety or human performance events.

Engage with industry peers, 3rd party vendors, NEI, EPRI, and other organizations to find innovative methods and technologies for developing and using software and AI in the Corrective Action Program.

Ensure Corrective Action Program in Software complies with company standards, NRC regulations, and other codes, standards, and regulatory requirements.

Maintains the Corrective Action Program by monitoring, tracking and identifying trends for corrective action data to provide performance improvements.

Develops and maintains status reports as directed.

Facilitate CAP/PI screening committee meetings.

Coordinate and participate in self-assessments, critiques, and benchmarking activities.

Manage and maintain the operating experience program

Monitor and maintain the site performance indicators

Discipline-Specific Education and Experience

5 years’ experience in working in the development and management of software based corrective action program with at least 3 years’ experience specific to Software

5 years’ experience with implementing or using a Nuclear Corrective Action Program

Key Qualifications and Skills

High level of knowledge of nuclear power plant systems and terminology.

Prior experience in Corrective Action programs

Experience with interpreting relevant nuclear information and standards in strict procedural and regulatory compliance.

Working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

Experience with smart procedures or other software-based procedure tools.

Excellent technical writing and communication skills.

Attention to detail and aspiration for procedural excellence.

Self-starter who can develop innovative solutions with minimal supervision.

Cause analysis training and/or certification

The successful candidate will:

- possess a high degree of trust and integrity, openly communicate, and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

- understand how individual actions and decisions can positively and negatively affect the ability of the total organization to meet its requirements with regulatory agencies and the public.

- adapt well to emergent changes due to changes in plant design, plant procedures, schedule changes, or other challenges associated with the design, construction, and commissioning of a new nuclear power plant.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Data Architect

TerraPower is searching for an experienced Data Architect to lead the design, governance, and standards of our enterprise data ecosystem. Our organization continues to add applications and data and we are looking to design and implement a scalable, governed data infrastructure, potentially centered around a unified Data Hub strategy to accelerate AI-training readiness, and to provide frictionless data-sharing and data interoperability. This role will establish the policies, patterns, and platforms that enable confident decision-making across the business.

The role will work hands-on within our Azure-centric stack, primarily Azure Data Factory and Azure Synapse Analytics, while partnering closely with Data Engineers, Application, Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity resources. This is a high-impact, cross-functional role with visibility across the entire organization.

Key Responsibilities

Data Governance & Standards

Establish and champion enterprise-wide data governance policies, standards, and best practices for data creation, storage, access, quality, and lifecycle management

Define and maintain a data catalog, data dictionary, and metadata management framework

Develop and enforce data classification standards in collaboration with Legal and Cybersecurity to ensure appropriate handling of sensitive and regulated data

Create and own data modeling standards (conceptual, logical, and physical) across all platforms

Create and own data quality and process monitoring frameworks to ensure the health, reliability, and lineage of data across the enterprise.Architecture & Design

Design and evolve the enterprise data lake into a data lakehouse architecture, ensuring it supports current workloads and scales to accommodate future application acquisitions and integrations

Lead the architectural design of data ingestion, transformation, and storage patterns within Azure Data Factory and Azure Synapse Analytics

Define reference architectures and design patterns for data integration, ensuring new applications and data sources are onboarded consistently and securely

Evaluate and recommend tools, frameworks, and emerging technologies to continuously improve the data platform

Evaluate a Data Hub architecture to streamline the exchange of data between operational applications and the analytical lakehouse, reducing point-to-point integration complexity

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with the Infrastructure Architect to align data platform design with infrastructure, networking, and cloud resource management strategies

Work with Cybersecurity to implement data access controls, encryption standards, auditing, and compliance requirements

Guide and mentor a team of Data Engineers, providing technical leadership, reviewing designs, and ensuring adherence to architectural standards

Collaborate with project teams and business stakeholders across multiple concurrent projects to define data requirements and integration approaches for new and acquired applications

Project & Integration Support

Serve as the data architecture lead across multiple simultaneous projects, providing guidance from initiation through deployment.

Define repeatable integration patterns for acquired applications being onboarded into the company data lake, including data mapping, transformation logic, and quality validation.

Document architecture decisions, data flows, and integration specifications to maintain a clear and accessible architectural record.

Qualifications

Required

9+ years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related technical discipline.

Demonstrated experience designing and governing enterprise data lakes or large-scale data platforms.

Deep hands-on expertise with Azure Data Factory and Azure Synapse Analytics (dedicated sql pool and serverless).

Strong experience with data modeling (relational, dimensional, and NoSQL data models).

Proven ability to establish and drive data governance programs including metadata management, master data management, data quality, and lineage.

Experience collaborating with security and infrastructure teams to implement compliant, secure data architectures.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to present complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

Preferred

Experience with Azure Purview or a similar enterprise data catalog / governance platform

Familiarity with Delta Lake, Apache Spark, or similar open-source data processing frameworks within a cloud environment. Familiarity with Azure Data Lake Storage, various database platforms (Oracle, MySQL, SQL Server, Postgres, Cosmos), Fabric, Azure Service Bus, Event Hubs, and Logic Apps

Exposure to application acquisition and data integration onboarding processes

Knowledge of data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA) and their architectural implications.

Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate, Azure Solutions Architect Expert or related Azure certification.

Experience in a multi-project or program environment with competing priorities.

 

Job Functions:

Job Functions are physical actions and/or working conditions associated with the position. These functions may also constitute essential functions for the job which the employee must be able to fulfill, with or without accommodation. Information provided below is to help describe the job so that the applicant has a reasonable understanding of the job duties/expectations. An applicant's ability to perform and/or tolerate these actions and conditions will be discussed and workplace accommodations may be made on a case-by-case basis following an individualized assessment of the applicant and other considerations, including but not limited to any governing safety standards.

Motor Abilities: Sitting for extended periods, bending/stooping, grasping/gripping, fine motor control (hands)

Physical exertion and/or requirements: Minimal, with ability to safely lift up to 25 pounds.

Repetitive work: Prolonged

Special Senses: Visual and audio focused work

Work Conditions: Stairs, typing/keyboard, standard and/or sitting working environment of >8 hours/day

Travel required 0-10%

jobs.title: Deputy Manager, Intermediate Heat Transport
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

TerraPower, LLC. Is seeking a highly motivated Deputy Manager for the Intermediate Heat Transport (IHT) system design team on TerraPower’s advanced nuclear reactor projects. In this role, you will support personnel and technical management of the IHT team, and report directly to the Manager of the Intermediate Heat Transport system. 

The IHT Deputy Manager is responsible for supporting a geographically diverse team focused on the design of reactor equipment and components supporting TerraPower’s Natrium project. The successful candidate will integrate and coordinate a team working on the reactor and it’s interfacing systems as part of a high-performing team of engineers and scientists. 

Responsibilities: 

Coordinate and oversee multidisciplinary engineering activities, ensuring project teams meet scope, technical performance, schedule, and budget expectations.

Develop, integrate, and analyze system-level schedules, including identifying acceleration opportunities and ensuring alignment with overall project schedules.

Coordinate technical interfaces and handoffs between TerraPower, partners, and interfacing project teams to enable cohesive design and fabrication strategies.

Plan, monitor, and manage task progress, including defining required scope, maximizing efficiency of individual contributors, and ensuring complete and accurate work execution.

Develop and maintain engineering staffing and resource plans, evaluating team skillsets and adjusting staffing to meet long-range needs and project priorities.

Support recruitment, onboarding, and integration of new engineering personnel, ensuring smooth assimilation into project processes and expectations.

Identify, communicate, and help resolve emerging technical issues and risks, ensuring Engineering and Project Management are consistently informed.

Maintain and align system-level risk registers with project-level risk registers, ensuring consistency and visibility of key risk items.

Maintain project documentation and records in accordance with corporate procedures and configuration management requirements.

Lead and support incorporation of plant information into licensing basis and regulatory submittals, contributing to compliance with Licensing Modernization Project requirements.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

Minimum of 6+ years of related experience. A Bachelor's and a Master’s degree; or a PhD with 3+ years experience. Previous supervisory or mentoring experience is desirable, but not required.

Proven ability to lead and collaborate within highly multidisciplinary, matrixed engineering teams.

Experience supervising personnel performing complex technical or analytical tasks for nuclear power applications.

Strong independent problem-solving skills, with the ability to work effectively with minimal direction.

Direct experience executing projects under NRC or DOE regulatory frameworks.

Ability to manage and deliver projects with competing objectives and complex design constraints, resolving conflicts across stakeholders.

Proficiency in project planning, scheduling, execution, and performance management.

Experience with collaboration and workflow tools, with preference for Atlassian Confluence/JIRA.

Familiarity with advanced project scheduling tools, such as Primavera P6.

Strong knowledge of nuclear-quality fabrication, construction, and lifecycle management processes, including inspection, surveillance, vibration, and equipment qualification programs.

Excellent oral and written communication skills.

Ability to manage numerous competing priorities concurrently during a given work week and work with minimal direction to solve multi-disciplinary problems.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

Job Functions:

These functions may also constitute essential functions for the job which the employee must be able to fulfill, with or without accommodation. An applicant's ability to perform and/or tolerate these actions and conditions will be discussed and workplace accommodations may be made on a case-by-case basis following an individualized assessment of the applicant and other considerations, including but not limited to any governing safety standards.

Motor Abilities: Sitting for extended periods, bending/stooping, grasping/gripping, fine motor control (hands)

Physical exertion and/or requirements: Minimal, with ability to safely lift up to 25 pounds.

Repetitive work: Prolonged

Special Senses: Visual and audio focused work

Work Conditions: Stairs, typing/keyboard, standard and/or sitting working environment of >8 hours/day

Travel required 0-10%

TerraPower’s technology is controlled for export by various agencies of the U.S. Government. TerraPower must evaluate applicants who are foreign nationals (other than asylees, refugees, or lawful permanent residents) in accordance with U.S. Government export control requirements. To facilitate TerraPower’s export control reviews, you will be asked as part of the application process to identify whether you are a U.S. Citizen or national, asylee, refugee, or lawful permanent resident of the United States. Government export authorization approval times vary. Based on the business needs for a particular position, TerraPower may not consider a foreign national from a country if it is impracticable to obtain timely Government export approval.

Job details

Salary Range: $138,685 - $208,027

*We typically place offers in the lower to middle portion of the range to maintain internal equity and allow room for growth. Any salary offered within the posted salary band is based on market data and commensurate with the selected individual’s qualifications experience. This range is specific to Washington State.

Benefits:

Competitive Compensation

Salary, eligible to participate in discretionar

jobs.title: Director, Nuclear Quality and Continuous Improvement
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.

 

Director, Nuclear Quality and Continuous Improvement

The Director, Nuclear Quality and Continuous Improvement has overall responsibility for the 10 CFR 50 Appendix B, NQA-1, and ASME Section III Quality programs for TerraPower. The Director is responsible for maintaining and growing their team through hiring/staffing the correct mix of skillset, experience and knowledge to implement and oversee the TerraPower Quality Programs. They are responsible for growing the personnel within their team through annual goal setting and monitoring, career growth opportunities and encouraging professional development. The Director will build communication among the departments including Engineering, Procurement, and Quality Assurance and educating all organizations of nuclear quality standards.

The Director should have QA management experience in NRC 10 CFR 50, Appendix B, ASME NQA-1, and ASME Section III regulated environments. Experience with industry codes, standards and guides such as ASME, AWS, AISC, ASTM, EPRI, NEI, ISO, NEC and IEEE are strongly desired.

Responsibilities

Develop and manage a highly effective team focused on meeting and defining quality requirements:

Demonstrate visible, continuous leadership in the areas of safety and quality

Identify and propose staffing needs, prepare job descriptions, interview candidates

Set yearly goals, hold mid-year checks, and perform annual performance reviews

Support employee professional development and career objectives

Coach for performance

Help resolve employee concerns

Recommend promotions, transfers, and other staffing changes

Ensure the project and technical scope are being met while meeting quality requirements:

Estimate effort and durations required for scheduled activities

Participate and provide input and feedback to Pre-Job Plan (PJP) meetings

Assign applicable personnel to activities

Monitor and report status of activities

Oversee partner and vendor technical work as required

Approve timecards and contractor invoices

Help develop and review statements of work with Procurement personnel for supplier technical activities

Identify and communicate opportunities for work process improvements with department business manager

Coach team members, including employees, partners and contractors on Nuclear Safety Culture, Procedural Use and Adherence expectations

Develop and ensure growth opportunities for employees

Ensure the technical adequacy and quality of work products:

Participate in Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA), Continuous Improvement, Lessons Learned/Operation Experience and Risk Management processes

Review quality affecting procedures (engineering, procurement, document control, records, testing, etc.)

Ensure compliance with TerraPower Engineering procedures and guidelines, including applicable quality standards

Keep team technically synchronized with each other and within the project; help remove roadblocks, break down silos and promote resilience and resourcefulness

Facilitate definition of technical interfaces/handoffs from other teams

Keep senior managers, department directors, and lead technical staff informed of major emerging technical issues, while working to solving these problems with others

Assist in the identification of regulations, codes and standards applicable to the work

Ensure technical documents meet applicable regulatory guides, codes and standards

Ensure design conducted in accordance with project requirements, configuration management and change control plans and practices.

Key Qualifications and Skills

S. engineering related discipline from an accredited university and 10+ years direct experience, or an M.S. or Ph.D. with commensurate experience.

12+ years’ experience in a Quality Assurance function with 5+ year as a Manager in a nuclear (NRC, DOE) Quality Assurance function preferred or in a regulated environment such as EPA, aerospace, or similar industries.

Experience successfully managing and leading a team of technical QA professionals.

Exceptional communication skills to integrate with a geographically and organizationally diverse Quality and engineering teams are required.

Experience managing projects executed under a high degree of regulatory oversight, with a preference for experience building Nuclear Power Plants. 

Strong technical acumen, with the ability to understand complex technical concepts is highly desirable.

Excellent oral and written communication skills.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

Job Functions:

An applicant's ability to perform and/or tolerate these actions and conditions will be discussed and workplace accommodations may be made on a case-by-case basis following an individualized assessment of the applicant and other considerations, including but not limited to any governing safety standards.

Motor Abilities: Sitting for extended periods, bending/stooping, grasping/gripping, fine motor control (hands)

Physical exertion and/or requirements: Minimal, with ability to safely lift up to 25 pounds

Repetitive work: Prolonged

Special Senses: Visual and audio focused work

Work Conditions: Stairs, typing/keyboard, standard and/or sitting working environment of >8 hrs./day, visits to construction site include walking on uneven surfaces and wearing required PPE

Travel required 20-30%

jobs.title: Director of Engineering, TPI
jobs.description:
The TerraPower Isotopes® (TPI™) division has been established to pursue medical isotopes development--advancing nuclear science for significant human health benefits. We seek to support revolutionary radiotherapies for treatments of various cancers through the supply of radioisotopes. TerraPower currently has unique access to a supply of thorium for the long-term production of actinium-225 in the growing alpha therapy market and intends to expand into other nuclear related medical treatments.

The TerraPower Isotopes program is an integral part of the Washington State based TerraPower team, a company working to raise living standards globally. In 2006, the company originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field.

TPI is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced radiopharmaceutical applications and influencing change within nuclear medicine. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Director of Engineering, TPI

The TerraPower Isotopes® (TPI™) Director of Engineering will oversee design, construction, integration and commissioning of new TPI Ac-225 production facilities for cGMP production of Actinium-225. Reporting to the President, TPI, the Director of Engineering will build and lead the team in the detailed design, equipment integration and commissioning of the new actinium-225 production facility, capable of safely producing a cGMP product from challenging long-lived alpha emitting radionuclides. The ideal candidate will be a strong leader with experience relevant to the design of shielded hot cell containment systems, radiological transfer systems, radiological exhaust systems, and will have a demonstrated track record in delivery with cost and schedule adherence in major capital projects . 

 

Responsibilities

Direct a team of engineering staff delivering full scope of design for new radioisotope production lines and cGMP radioisotope facilities.

Oversee engineering quality, project delivery, continuous improvement, and corrective action response.

Identify and ensure effective application of correct design codes into the design of facility and lab systems such as ASME, ASCE, ANS, ASPE, ASTM, IEEE, NFPA.

Coordinate, lead and oversee assembly of technical packages for Project design reviews, ensuring effective review processes and implementation of best engineering practices within a stage gate technical project management framework.

Ensure complete delivery against requirements for broad scope of design work for complex radiological operations involving nuclear containments (hot cells and gloveboxes), mechanical systems, transfer systems, and cGMP facility controls.

Oversee quality of information from TPI Engineering team and communication of technical deliverable status, risks and issues to TPI project managers.

Communicated effectively with TPI technology, operations, quality, and customer service teams, in addition to TerraPower Facilities, Safety and Radiation Safety Officers to collaboratively develop the full technical scope and to safely and efficiently deliver on project objectives.

Oversee technical components of acceptance testing, IQ/OQ/PQ, turnover planning, initial maintenance plan development, troubleshooting of equipment and coordinating across TerraPower disciplines and teams to ensure full compliance with all relevant regulations and TerraPower processes.

Support development of procurement packages, and supporting efforts to maximize efficiency and success of TPI Engineering procurements through effective communication with contract administrators, and TerraPower management teams.

Oversee effective management of technical scope for external vendors to ensure complete and reliable designs, on time delivery, and thorough review to ensure accurate fabrication work, and successful acceptance of equipment.

Ensuring solid and effective engineering design processes and best practices are applied evenly for internal engineering design and external engineering procurement projects.

Provide technical guidance during routine operations, preventative maintenance, and upgrades of facilities, systems and equipment and ensure clear and effective communication of lessons learned

 

Key Qualifications and Skills

At least 18+ years of experience in the nuclear and/or radiopharmaceutical industry with a B.S. in Mechanical, Chemical, or Nuclear Engineering, or other relevant program from an accredited university, or an M.S. or Ph.D. and commensurate experience.

Extensive experience leading engineering design and major capital project delivery for nuclear or radiopharmaceutical facilities from project inception to turnover to operations.

At least 6 years in a combined technical management role with responsibilities for quality and delivery of project technical scope, in addition to other manager responsibilities.

Comprehensive knowledge of engineering codes relevant to the design of nuclear and radiopharmaceutical facilities, including ASME NQA-1, ASCE, ANS, ASTM, IEEE, NFPA and ICH and ISO standards relevant to the design of cGMP radioisotope production facilities.

Experience designing, and managing the install and commissioning of nuclear containments including hot cells, gloveboxes, or other shielded mechanical systems is essential.

Familiarity with radiological and aseptic material transfer systems is essential.

Experience leading a team demonstrating discipline, rigor, attention to detail, attention to safety, and procedural adherence is required.

Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills, such as those developed in fast-paced environments including the military, and other nuclear or radiopharmaceutical environments is highly preferred.

Excellent technical writing, communication, and presentation skills.

Ability to handle projects that include many competing objectives and design constraints, with ability to resolve conflicts between objectives and lead productive delivery of engineering deliverables within a fast paced environment.

Experience working and communicating productively in a multidisciplinary team including R&D, production, quality assurance, and engineering design functions.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, able to communicate openly, and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.title: Director of Tax
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Director of Tax

This leadership role will be responsible for overseeing the Company's tax filing requirements, including federal and state income tax returns, as well as providing strategic support for tax incentives and tax mitigation projects. The Director will also lead tax research and special project work, ensuring compliance with all applicable tax laws. The successful candidate will demonstrate advanced expertise in US partnership taxation, GAAP accounting consolidated and international tax returns, and multi-state tax concepts.

Responsibilities:

Lead the management of partnership tax returns, LLC members' GAAP and tax capital account roll forward, ensuring proper income (loss) allocations.

Provide strategic leadership and oversight partnership tax reporting, including reviewing and reconciling amounts between tax returns and source data.

Direct R&D tax credit and expenses, ensuring accurate and compliant reporting

Oversee the accumulation, analysis, and processing of financial data in preparation for tax returns, budgets, forecasts, and financial accounting estimates related to tax.

Perform tax research and write memos to support tax positions.

Support the tax team on all tax audits, ensuring efficient resolution.

Support state and local tax (SALT) matters, including SALT compliance and operations, exemption certifications, and matters related to remote workers across multiple states, ensuring compliance with multi-state tax laws.

Provide guidance and review for financial statement reporting related to taxes.

Support tax strategies and advise leadership of trends and changes in tax requirements and impact on business planning

Monitor, evaluate and analyze emerging tax trends and provide expertise on impact to organization

Stay updated on federal, state, and international tax law changes, maintaining a high level of knowledge in the tax field.

Work cross departmentally with entities on tax opportunities and compliance, finance on budgeting and forecasting, legal on entity set-up and compliance, and other departments to gather the necessary information for reporting purposes

Manage external tax advisors and consultants for outsourced tax compliance and advisory services.

Including preparation of quarterly review tax process, annual audit process, documentation, and associated internal control work

Key Qualifications and Skills

Master’s degree in Taxation or related field.

Extensive experience in LLC partnership tax, corporate tax, and state income tax compliance.

Minimum of 15 years of work experience in an accounting or tax environment, with at least 5 years in a leadership role.

CPA license preferred.

Big 4 and industry tax experience strongly preferred.

Deep knowledge of federal, state, and local income tax forms, including Form 1065, Form 6765, and various state partnership returns

Industry experience in engineering, nuclear, aerospace, and defense preferred.

Proficient with Microsoft Excel; ability to quickly adapt to other Finance and Accounting systems (e.g., Oracle Cloud ERP, Concur).

Knowledge of Oracle preferred.

Experience with direct and indirect cost, allowable and unallowable expenses.

Familiarity with government grants and government contracts preferred.

Strong organizational skills, high attention to detail, and accuracy.

Ability to quickly grasp new information and technologies.

Experience working in fast-paced, dynamic environments.

Excellent communication skills, comfortable working with cross-functional teams and non-accounting professionals.

Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks effectively.

Proven leadership skills, with the ability to foster teamwork and collaboration in a multi-disciplinary environment.

High degree of integrity and trustworthiness, with a commitment to open communication and respect.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

Director of Financial Reporting & Technical Accounting

This is a senior-level finance leader responsible for managing financial reporting functions across multiple business units within a diverse organization. This position provides leadership and technical accounting expertise in the financial reporting function of the Company. The role ensures accurate financial statements, maintains internal controls, and compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements.

Key Responsibilities

Provide primary support on all technical accounting matters including technical accounting memos and white paper authoring.

Provides accounting guidance related to proposed transactions to ensure conformity with GAAP and company policies and procedures.

Provide strategic leadership on all technical accounting matters on a global scale.

Serve as a technical accounting resource and guide the organization through complex accounting issues.

Provide training and ongoing US GAAP support to the Controller’s organization on all technical accounting matters.

Drive all Financial Reporting aspects of the processes, assess, implement, and maintain technical accounting policies.

Collaborate with Operations, FP&A, Tax, Treasury, and Legal departments to ensure alignment and accurate reporting.

Communicate complex financial information clearly and effectively to both financial and non-financial stakeholders.

Oversee the quarterly and annual financial reporting and disclosures, ensure statements are in compliance with US GAAP and regulations.

Spearhead research initiatives to stay abreast of U.S. GAAP, global accounting trends and regulatory changes.

Foster a culture of continuous improvement and uphold the highest standards of financial integrity.

Ensure all reporting requirements are met and that all significant accounting matters are appropriately evaluated as part of the financial reporting process.

Prepare and present reporting packages to executive leadership and external stakeholders.

Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency and accuracy in reporting and accounting operations.

Coordinate and manage external audits, including preparing supporting documentation and addressing auditor inquiries. Be the point person for complex accounting matters.

Spearhead special projects as required by executive management such as, but not limited to, automation around accounting processes and financial reporting, due diligence, Sox testing, and continuous process improvements.

Qualifications

A Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field is required.

CPA certification is required.

12+ years of accounting experience, including significant roles in BIG 4 and public companies.

A leadership role overseeing accounting in a multi-business segment environment

Experience with complex global consolidations, intercompany accounting, and reporting across different industries or sectors.

Prior experience with ERP systems (Oracle Cloud) and financial reporting tools.

Technical proficiency in ASC 730, ASC 360, ASC 606, ASC 842 and other complex accounting transactions

Strong communication and presentation skills with ability to instill confidence and trust

Strong technical knowledge of US GAAP and SEC regulations is required

Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and superior organization skills is required.

Experience with MD&A, SEC filing

Director, Supply Chain Operations

TerraPower is seeking a Director of Supply Chain Operations to lead the day-to-day execution of TerraPower’s global supply chain for the Natrium sodium-cooled fast reactor program.

You’ll run the operational side of supply chain, including managing supplier delivery, material flow, logistics, inventory, and performance with a goal to ensuring the Natrium demonstration plant and the future commercial fleet stays on schedule and on budget. This role will report directly to the VP, Commercial Supply Chain.

Responsibilities

Own end-to-end supply chain execution for all critical nuclear components, modules, fuel, and balance-of-plant equipment.

Lead supplier performance management, on-time delivery tracking, and corrective action programs.

Build and run the logistics, warehousing, and material kitting strategy for modular construction sites.

Manage supply chain systems, ERP integration, planning, and forecasting for long-lead nuclear items.

Drive continuous improvement in cost, quality, and delivery across the entire supplier network.

Partner closely with the Director of Supply Chain Strategy to hand off qualified suppliers into live execution.

Lead a team responsible for expediting, quality surveillance, and field material support.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor’s degree (Supply Chain, Business or Engineering) required; advanced degree preferred. Supply chain certification preferred.

15+ years in supply chain operations, with at least 5 years in nuclear, aerospace, or other highly regulated industries.

Strong experience managing complex, long-lead item supply chains for large energy or industrial projects.

Deep knowledge of nuclear quality standards, ASME NQA-1, and supply chain compliance.

Proven ability to build, develop and lead high-performing operational teams.

Strong written and verbal communication skills

jobs.title: EHS Coordinator
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, as a federal contractor, TerraPower has instituted an Affirmative Action Plan (AAP) in an effort to proactively recruit, hire, and promote women, minorities, disabled persons and veterans.

EHS Coordinator

TerraPower, LLC. is seeking a safety professional to provide the following services: The EHS Coordinator is responsible for overseeing and managing environmental, health, and safety program. The candidate is responsible for providing leadership and guidance to ensure location compliance with regulations, promoting a safe work environment, and driving continuous improvement. This position will support the safeguarding of worker health and safety in the workplace by anticipating, recognizing, evaluating, and controlling environmental and physical hazards that can cause injury or illness.

Responsibilities:

EHS Program Implementation and Management:

Ensure site specific EHS programs, policies, and procedures aligned with company goals and objectives.

Serve as the primary contact for implementing EHS programs in the region's facilities or sites.

Regulatory Compliance:

 Ensure compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local EHS regulations (e.g., NRC, OSHA, EPA, etc.).

Conduct inspections and audits to assess compliance with regulations and company policies.

 Serve as the main point of contact for EHS regulatory agencies and external EHS audits.

Risk Assessment and Incident Prevention:

Ensure risks, unsafe practices and implement controls to mitigate those risks are identified an implemented.

Review workplace risk/exposure assessments.

Lead incident investigations to identify root causes and implement corrective actions.

Training and Education:

Ensure EHS training programs for employees at all levels have been adopted.

Ensure employees and managers are trained and knowledgeable of their EHS responsibilities.

Performance Tracking and Improvement:

Track and analyze EHS performance metrics.

Develop and implement annual improvement plans based on performance data.

Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance safety performance.

Leadership and Support:

Provide EHS leadership and guidance to site employees and management.

Support and mentor field service employees and local leadership teams.

Collaborate with various teams (e.g., operations, training, etc.) to drive program success. 

Hazard Identification and Evaluation: Identifying and analyzing potential workplace hazards such as chemical, physical, ergonomic, and biological exposures, and conducting worksite analyses to pinpoint problem areas.

Exposure Assessment: Measuring the level of worker exposure to identified hazards through environmental monitoring.

Hazard Control and Mitigation: Developing and recommending controls to minimize hazards, including engineering controls (e.g., ventilation systems, isolating the hazard), work practice changes, administrative controls (e.g., scheduling changes), and recommending personal protective equipment (PPE).

Key Qualifications and Skills:

Education: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Health and Safety, Environmental Science, Industrial Hygiene, Engineering, or a related scientific or engineering discipline

Experience: 7-10 years of experience in EHS or environmental programs, often in a manufacturing, production, laboratory or service operations setting. Experience in a large, complex environment may be desired.

Proven leadership or management experience.

Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, excellent communication and interpersonal skills, leadership qualities, and the ability to work independently or as part of a team.

Technical expertise in specific areas such as electrical safety or hazardous materials may also be necessary depending on the industry.

Certified Industrial Hygienist (CIH) designation from the Board for Global EHS Credentialing is highly recommended and can enhance career prospects.

Strong communication skills are desired.

The service provider will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect, and a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.title: EHS Technician
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

EHS Technician

The Environmental Health and Safety Technician will support daily site safety operations by helping maintain safe working conditions through routine inspections, audits, and facility walkdowns, and by assisting with basic sampling and program implementation. The role also helps manage chemical receiving, inventory, bulk dispensing, and supports the hazardous waste program and basic spill response activities. The Environmental Health and Safety Technician will contribute to workplace safety through delivering training and assisting with investigations and corrective action follow-up.

This position reports to the Manager, Industrial Hygiene and Waste.

Responsibilities

Assist in establishing and monitoring safe working conditions

Conduct weekly and monthly inspections of fire extinguishers, eyewash and safety showers, AEDs, first aid kits, etc.

Perform routine inspections and audits

Support implementation of EHS programs

Assists with basic EHS sampling

Assist with the management of hazardous waste

Receive, maintain, and reconcile chemicals and chemical inventory

Dispense and bulk chemicals

Deliver basic safety training to workforce members

Basic spill response

Assist facility safety walkdowns

Assist in investigations into accidents, injuries, illness, or near misses

Key Qualifications and Skills

S. in, Environment Health, Public Health, Environmental Science, Chemistry, Biology, or related field

0-2 years of related experience

Basic understanding of 29 CFR 1910 &1926.

Knowledge of 10CFR & 40CFR is a plus

First Aid / CPR/ AED Certification

HAZWOPER 24/40 hr, DOT, IATA, RCRA is a plus

Understanding of occupational exposure limits (ACGIH TLV, OSHA PEL).

Ability to wear all levels of PPE

Effective written and verbal English communication skills

Can effectively work independently or as a member of a team

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork

Passion working in a fast-paced Research & Development environment and communicating with a wide variety of workers with different backgrounds and knowledge levels

jobs.title: Engineer, Nuclear Containment Systems
jobs.description:
The TerraPower Isotopes® (TPI™) division has been established to pursue medical isotopes development--advancing nuclear science for significant human health benefits. We seek to support revolutionary radiotherapies for treatments of various cancers through the supply of radioisotopes. TerraPower currently has unique access to a supply of thorium for the long-term production of actinium-225 in the growing alpha therapy market and intends to expand into other nuclear related medical treatments.

The TerraPower Isotopes program is an integral part of the Washington State based TerraPower team, a company working to raise living standards globally. In 2006, the company originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field.

TPI is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced radiopharmaceutical applications and influencing change within nuclear medicine. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Engineer, Nuclear Containment Systems - TPI

The TerraPower Isotopes® (TPI™) Nuclear Containment Systems Engineer will play a key role in the design, development, integration and commissioning of mechanical systems within TPI Ac-225 production facilities with particular emphasis on hot cells and gloveboxes suitable for handling of alpha emitting and long-lived radionuclides. The TPI Nuclear Containment Systems Engineer will join the team designing new containment systems and related equipment as TPI expands our production of Ac-225 and go on to support installation and commissioning of that equipment for TPI. The ideal candidate will have experience in the specification and design of radiological enclosures, transfer systems, HVAC and/or other systems commonly employed in radiological containments.

 

Responsibilities: 

Lead or support the specification and design of containment systems for complex radiological operations, transfer systems, remote manipulation and equipment integration within hot cells and gloveboxes from requirements development through to final design, successful commissioning and startup.

Serve as either design lead or design backup for hot cells (HC) and gloveboxes (GB), contributing to overall layout planning, shielding and containment strategies for new production lines and facilities.

Originate, review and maintain equipment requirements, specifications, calculations, process narratives, commissioning and installation plans, and IQ/OQ/PQ documents associated with the design and installation of mechanical systems.

Participate in the integration of mechanical systems with facility infrastructure and process workflows.

Identify and ensure effective application of correct design codes into the design of facility and lab systems such as ASME, ASCE, ANS, ASTM, IEEE, NFPA, AGS, etc.

Communicate effectively across TPI team, and with key stakeholders to support review of engineering designs and gather necessary design input information.

Collaborate with engineering and project teams during facility design and construction phases to ensure mechanical system, interface and facility requirements are met.

Support troubleshooting of mechanical equipment on an as-needed basis, maintaining, and modifying equipment, and coordination across the TPI team to prepare and execute work procedures.

Assist in developing procurement packages, communicating with procurement contract administrators, and overseeing fabrication and delivery of custom mechanical equipment.

Communicate effectively with external vendors to review designs, maintain design documentation, manage timelines, ensure accuracy and efficiency of fabrication work, and support factory acceptance testing.

Provide technical guidance during routine operations, preventative maintenance, and upgrades of mechanical systems.

 

Key Qualifications and Skills 

B.S. in mechanical, nuclear, systems engineering or related discipline from an accredited university and 5+ years direct experience with proven equipment design, testing and commissioning skills in an industrial setting.

Understanding of mechanical engineering codes, standards, and design best practices.

Proficiency in CAD software (e.g. SolidWorks, AutoCAD) is preferred.

Experience designing hot cells, gloveboxes, or other shielded mechanical systems strongly preferred.

Familiarity with radiological handling and material transfer systems is highly desirable.

Prior experience designing, installing systems and performing acceptance testing for equipment that operates within nuclear facilities, radiopharmaceutical facilities, or radiological areas is strongly preferred.

Experience leading acceptance testing and full commissioning of equipment is essential.

Experience working to quality standards on design activities, such as ASME NQA-1 or ISO 9001.

Discipline, rigor, attention to detail, attention to safety, and procedural adherence are required.

Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills, such as those developed in fast-paced environments including the military, and other nuclear or radiopharmaceutical environments are strongly preferred.

Excellent technical writing, communication, and presentation skills.

Ability to handle projects that include many competing objectives and design constraints, with ability to resolve conflicts between objectives and productively generate engineering design documents accordingly in a fast paced environment.

Experience working and communicating productively in a multidisciplinary team including R&D, production, quality assurance, and engineering design functions.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, able to communicate openly, and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

Actual position starting level and title will be determined based on assessment of qualifications.
