jobs.title: Equipment Supply Manager
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Equipment Supply Manager

TerraPower, LLC. is seeking an Equipment Supply Manager to provide the service of Equipment Project Management. The Equipment Supply Manager is the single point of accountability for development, delivery, start up and close-out of equipment for the Natrium deployment at the Kemmerer Power Generating Station. The Equipment Supply Manager plans and manages the overall execution of plant equipment delivery. They are responsible for the coordination of multiple cross-functional teams needed to complete all aspects of delivery scope throughout the equipment delivery lifecycle. These teams include design engineering, procurement, QA, fabrication, testing, logistics, start-up/commissioning, construction and licensing teams. They are responsible for communication of information to key project stakeholders while ensuring that information is properly controlled.

Tasks:  

Define and lead execution strategy for equipment across multiple teams, functions, and organizations.

Articulate requirements, goals, and milestones for equipment delivery with appropriate stakeholders responsible for specific areas (system leads, senior managers, project managers).

Drive equipment delivery on time and on budget per the Natrium Project plan.

Work with project teams to develop plans, influence teams to deliver to them, and keep leadership informed on progress.

Coordinate the timely review of deliverables across cross functional teams.

Work with project teams to ensure that work focuses on the highest priority as appropriately reflected in the schedule.

Initiate and rapidly execute recovery efforts when cost and schedule items arise.

Escalate challenges promptly to ensure they receive adequate attention and resources for resolution.

Work with Corporate Procurement and Supply Chain teams to execute equipment specific procurement strategies

Coordinate and drive strategies for vendor engagement prior to RFP to ensure viability of design specifications and scopes of work.

Track and monitor the issuance and release of contracts for all equipment-related scopes.

Oversees vendor performance for vendor awarded scope.

Monitor contract budgets and approved values to verify that work is adequately funded and aligns with project financial parameters.

Work with Testing teams to ensure development, demonstration and qualification testing efforts support equipment delivery needs. This includes funding, timing and applicability of testing.

Manage risks to schedule and budget associated with testing programs.

Reinforce organizational accountability for testing programs and results.

Facilitate timely incorporation of any test results impacting design.

Work with Natrium Fabrication Oversight to manage fabrication vendors, to identify and manage witness or hold point notifications to ensure appropriate stakeholder involvement at critical stages.

Work with Natrium Construction team to ensure on-site requirements and strategies for assembly, installation and inspections are integrated with equipment delivery plans.

Work with TerraPower QA and Licensing groups to ensure adherence to quality requirements, regulatory guidelines, and industry standards as specified in equipment delivery efforts and documents.

Work with Project Management and Project Controls groups to ensure up to date cost and schedule forecasting, including identification of trends and risks.

Work with Commissioning team to ensure adequate documentation, materials, spare parts, and support is available to expeditiously perform andy planned testing on site.

Closely interface with Natrium Product Management to ensure that equipment design, performance, and requirements are aligned with product and project needs.

Implement accountability and governance checkpoints for testing programs, ensuring compliance with quality standards and timely integration of results into design and delivery plans.

Conduct regular prioritization and triage reviews to confirm that work efforts remain focused on the highest-value activities aligned with schedule and risk mitigation.

Lead development and implementation of recovery and get-well plans when deviations from cost or schedule occur, ensuring alignment with project objectives.

Establish and execute formal escalation management protocols, including triggers, escalation paths, and timing to ensure timely resolution of critical issues.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, or equivalent.

Minimum of 10 years of experience related to Supply Chain, Project Management, or Project Delivery.

Proven leader, with a strong technical background, in one or many areas of the scope.

Proficient in project management tools such as Jira and P6 (Primavera).

Strong familiarity with nuclear codes, standards, and regulations, including ASME BPVC, ASME QME-1, NQA-1, IEEE standards, and industry requirements.

Preferred qualifications include certifications such as PMP (Project Management Professional), Six Sigma, or Lean methodology.

Demonstrated experience leading a large project scope across multiple teams with strong dependencies.

Great communication skills, and effective cross-organization collaborator.

Thrive and succeed in a fast-paced and changing environment where plans need to adjustment to meet project goals.

Strong conflict resolution skills.

Creative, proactive, attentive to detail, and can take ownership of problems and their resolution.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.description:

At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Executive Assistant, Finance

TerraPower is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Executive Assistant to support the Chief Financial Officer (CFO). This role provides high-level executive and administrative support, including complex calendar and travel management, meeting coordination, preparation of presentation materials, and proactive problem-solving. The ideal candidate is a trusted professional who exercises sound judgment, manages confidential information with discretion, and contributes meaningfully to the efficiency and productivity of the Finance leadership team.

Responsibilities

Provide comprehensive executive support to the CFO and department leadership.

Manage calendars, meetings, conferences, and teleconferences, ensuring logistics, deadlines, and materials are handled efficiently and in compliance with TerraPower Facility Access Procedures.

Plan and support pre‑meeting preparation, develop agendas, capture and track action items, and maintain accurate records, notes, and documentation.

Manage highly confidential communications and information.

Assist with board-related materials, including preparation and coordination as needed.

Support leadership in developing high‑quality presentations and documents (Word, PowerPoint, Excel), including proofreading, formatting, and version management.

Serve as an on-site representative for the CFO, acting as a professional liaison between internal and external stakeholders and facilitating communication and information flow.

Provide general support to the Executive Leadership Team (ELT), helping coordinate internal/external requests and enabling effective interdepartmental communication.

Manage all travel logistics for the CFO, including itinerary planning, flights, hotels, ground transportation, event registration, and related scheduling.

Prepare, submit, and track expense reports accurately, promptly, and in compliance with company policies.

Participate actively in the administrative community, including offering backup support to other administrative staff, ensuring visitor access and security protocols, and attending administrative meetings.

Foster strong working relationships with key stakeholders, partners, and vendors.

Support special projects and ad hoc requests from senior leadership.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Facilities Day Porter Assistant

The Facilities Day Porter Assistant supports the daily operations, cleanliness, and organization of TerraPower facilities. This role helps maintain common areas, manages supply inventory, and provides general facilities support across both Bellevue and Everett office locations.

Responsibilities

Maintain cleanliness and organization of common areas, including conference rooms, breakrooms, copy rooms, and shared spaces.

Perform routine cleaning of appliances such as refrigerators, coffee machines, and microwaves.

Support Facilities and Administrative teams with building-related requests and general upkeep.

Stock and organize supplies in common areas, breakrooms, and copy rooms.

Conduct inventory counts and assist with ordering supplies and food.

Perform light janitorial duties, including trash removal, cardboard breakdown, spill cleanup, and sanitization as needed.

Assist with weekly, quarterly, and annual deep-cleaning initiatives.

Support meeting and event coordination, including catering delivery, room setup, and post-event cleanup.

Receive and distribute mail and packages, including inter-office deliveries, and assist with outgoing shipments.

Participate as a member of the Emergency Response Team.

Other duties as assigned.

Key Qualifications and Skills

1–3 years of experience in a porter or facilities support role, preferably in a corporate environment.

Strong customer service and hospitality mindset.

Reliable and flexible, with the ability to work varying hours between 6:30 AM and 5:30 PM.

High school diploma required; Associate degree preferred (or equivalent experience).

Strong organizational and prioritization skills with attention to detail.

Ability to problem solve, adapt, and manage multiple tasks effectively.

Proven ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

Sound judgment and proactive approach to identifying and addressing needs.

Professional demeanor with strong interpersonal and communication skills.

Demonstrated integrity, teamwork, and respect in a collaborative environment.

Additional Information

The starting level and title for this position will be determined based on the candidate’s qualifications and experience.

Facilities Workplace Coordinator

The Facilities Workplace Coordinator is responsible for coordinating facilities tasks and supporting day‑to‑day workplace operations across TerraPower’s corporate locations. This role serves as a key partner to the Real Estate & Facilities team by combining facilities coordination, workplace operations, and vendor support at TerraPower locations at Bellevue and Everett.

The position plays an essential role in ensuring operational efficiency, maintaining high service standards, supporting facility budgets, and fostering strong vendor and stakeholder relationships across the organization. The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, and detail‑oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced and evolving environment.

The successful candidate will demonstrate a high degree of trust, integrity, professionalism, and collaboration while consistently fostering teamwork and delivering exceptional customer service.

Responsibilities

Facilities Operations

Plans, organizes, maintains, and manages the operations, reliability, and overall functionality of TerraPower facilities and infrastructure systems.

Monitors facility support requests through Jira ticketing systems, ensuring timely resolution and excellent customer service.

Assists in maintaining facility access control and security protocols for employees, contractors, vendors, and visitors.

Coordinates with Security and Facilities teams to ensure compliance for controlled areas, physical safeguards rooms, and other secure spaces.

Supports troubleshooting efforts and coordinates responses to facility-related issues, including escalations and occasional after-hours support.

Program Coordination

Leads or supports special projects and continuous improvement initiatives assigned by the Director of Real Estate & Facilities.

Maintains organized project documentation and supports process improvement initiatives across the department.

Workplace Services

Support onboarding and offboarding activities for employees, contractors, interns, and secondees in partnership with HR and Access Control teams.

Supports front desk operations and general workplace coordination.

Provide operational support to the Director of Real Estate & Facilities and members of the Executive Suite.

Support information management processes including document organization, SharePoint administration, and records management.

Coordinates vendor activities to ensure compliance with company standards, contractual obligations, and operational requirements.

Coordinates vendor activities to ensure compliance with company standards, contractual obligations, and operational requirements.

Communications & Stakeholder Engagement

Build and maintain strong relationships with employees, executives, vendors, business partners, and external stakeholders.

Serves as a trusted liaison between executive leadership, administrative teams, Facilities, and the broader employee population.

Communicate information clearly, accurately, and professionally while maintaining confidentiality and discretion.

Bachelor’s degree required; equivalent combination of education and relevant experience may be considered.

Minimum of 5 years of experience in facilities coordination, workplace operations, project coordination, executive support, or corporate administration.

Experience supporting corporate facilities operations, workplace services, or office administration in a fast-paced environment.

Strong project coordination and organizational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities and shifting deadlines.

Experience with vendor coordination, facilities operations, and workplace service management preferred.

Demonstrated ability to handle confidential and sensitive information with professionalism and discretion.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships across all organizational levels.

Experience providing executive-level administrative support and coordinating cross-functional initiatives.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite including Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

Experience using SharePoint, Jira, Concur, and workplace management systems preferred.

Familiarity with access control systems, visitor management platforms, and space planning tools such as Lenel, Lobby Track, or Visio is a plus.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to develop reports, metrics, and process improvements.

Ability to work independently, exercise sound judgment, and adapt quickly in a dynamic environment.

Demonstrated leadership potential, professionalism, and commitment to teamwork and customer service.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Finance Director

Finance Director plays a key role in driving enterprise financial planning, capital investment oversight, and performance analysis across the organization. This role partners closely with business leaders and senior leadership to support financial planning, capital allocation decisions, and operational performance management. The Director leads financial modeling, capital project oversight, and forecasting activities while delivering actionable insights that support strategic and financial objectives.

You will serve as a trusted advisor to business leaders, translating complex financial data into clear, actionable strategies that drive efficiency, accountability, and growth.

This role requires close collaboration with technical teams, the PMO, and corporate support functions to streamline reporting, manage cost allocations, and support change management initiatives. You’ll lead efforts to refine and enhance processes, ensure data integrity, and maintain a single source of financial truth.

This is a high-visibility role ideal for a forward-thinking finance leader who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is passionate about making a measurable impact on business performance.

Responsibilities:

Establish consistent frameworks for evaluating capital investments and tracking project financial performance. Implement and maintain a standardized capital approval process aligned with the annual budgeting cycle.

Manage financial oversight for corporate capital construction projects, including financial modeling, tracking, and reporting.

Build and maintain financial models that evaluate project ROI, capital efficiency, and operational performance.

Support capital expenditure (CapEx) planning by business unit, ensuring alignment with company priorities and budget constraints.

Monitor project progress against approved budgets and timelines and report on risks, variances, and EAC.

Provide data-driven insights on forecasts, budgets, variance analysis to senior leaders to help guide strategic financial decisions, optimize resource management and expense controls.

Prepare and present monthly financial dashboards to communicate budget deviation, insights, providing visibility to stakeholders.

Translate financial insights into clear recommendations for business leaders and executives.

Develop and maintain cash vs. cost modeling for capital projects, identifying and explaining timing differences between expenses and cash payments.

Partner cross-functionally with accounting, operations, project management, and corporate leaders to ensure alignment between financial planning and operational execution.

Lead the development of best practices, processes, and analytical capabilities across the organization.

Mentor and guide junior finance team members where applicable.

Oversee current financial processes while identifying opportunities for internal control improvements and process efficiencies. Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and transparency in all finance operations. 

Lead ad-hoc projects

 Prepare and present board-level financial materials, including reporting packages, KPIs, and strategic insights.

Key Qualifications and Skills:

Minimum 16 years’ experience functioning in an FP&A or relevant finance position

Undergraduate degree in Finance or Accounting

Identify and report inconsistencies across business units while establishing standards for budgeting, performance metrics, and reporting processes. 

Ensure alignment of financial strategies and initiatives with overall corporate and business strategies.

C-level, Capital Portfolio Management experience a plus

Lead financial analysis efforts to support pricing scenarios and the development of innovative business models.

Ability to synthesize complex financial narratives for senior management.

Analyze business opportunities and risks, providing strategic recommendations and alternative solutions to senior management.

Serve as a key advisor in shaping data-driven financial decisions that enhance organizational performance and profitability.

As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

jobs.title: Human Resources Business Partner
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Human Resources Business Partner 

TerraPower is seeking a highly motivated Human Resources Business Partner to support our growing and dynamic team. Reporting to the Sr. HR Manager. The Human Resources Business Partner focused on operations supports the execution of core HR operational processes and employee lifecycle activities, ensuring HR programs, systems, and documentation are administered efficiently and in compliance with company policies.

This role combines HR business partnership responsibilities with HR operations, process improvement, systems support, and HR program administration. The ideal candidate is adaptable, solution-oriented, and thrives in a fast-paced environment. This individual will bring a strong customer-service mindset, operational rigor, and the ability to balance strategic partnership responsibilities with operational execution.

Responsibilities 

 HR Business Partnership

Partner with managers and leaders to provide day-to-day HR guidance and support across employee lifecycle matters.

Coach leaders on HR policies, employee concerns, performance conversations, and team effectiveness.

Support performance management processes, employee engagement initiatives, and organizational effectiveness efforts.

Assist leaders in navigating employee relations matters in partnership with Senior Employee Relations Specialist as appropriate.

Build collaborative relationships across departments and serve as a trusted HR resource for employees and managers.

HR Operations & Program Administration 

Support upkeep of HRIS systems and employee data, ensuring accuracy and proper documentation across HR systems.

Support HR reporting and dashboards, analyze workforce trends and HR metrics, and assist with operational reporting needs.

Identify opportunities to improve and automate HR processes through technology, AI tools, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Help drive scalable HR workflows and process improvements to enhance operational efficiency and consistency.

Partner in the administration, execution, and continuous improvement of HR programs and operational processes, including workforce mobility, alternative work arrangements (AWA), onboarding, offboarding, and employee lifecycle activities.

Serve as backup support for onboarding activities when needed, including onboarding documentation, workflows, and coordination with internal stakeholders.

HR Compliance & Documentation

Maintain employee documentation and records in accordance with company policies and regulatory requirements.

Facilitate periodic internal reviews of HR documentation, including I-9 forms and onboarding records, to support regulatory compliance.

Support compliance initiatives, records retention practices, and HR documentation standards.

Assist in the development and maintenance of HR procedures, internal guidance documents, toolkits, and training materials.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

B.S. degree in Business, Communications, Human Resources Management, or a related field.

4+ years of experience in progressive HR roles in a corporate environment with at least 2+ years of business partner responsibilities

Experience supporting managers and employees across HR processes and employee relations matters.

Experience supporting HR systems (HRIS), employee data management, and HR documentation practices.

Strong communication, collaboration, and relationship-building skills with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams.

Strong organizational and problem-solving skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Demonstrated initiative and continuous improvement mindset with interest in automation, AI tools, and scalable process solutions.

High level of professionalism, discretion, integrity, and sound judgment when handling sensitive employee information.

Ability to adapt quickly to changing business needs and organizational priorities.

Preferred Qualifications

HR experience supporting engineering, technical, manufacturing, energy, nuclear, or highly regulated organizations.

Familiarity with HRIS systems such as ADP Workforce Now or similar platforms.

Understanding HR compliance requirements including I-9 documentation, records management, and employee lifecycle processes.

Experience supporting process improvement, HR operations optimization, or HR program implementation initiatives.

HR certification (PHR, SHRM-CP, SPHR, or SHRM-SCP) is a plus.

jobs.title: IT Systems Engineer
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

IT System Engineer 

TerraPower, LLC is seeking a skilled and motivated hardware infrastructure engineer to join our Enterprise IT team. In this hands-on role, you will be the primary owner of our physical compute, storage, and power infrastructure spanning a small/mediums sized data center and several local MDF/IDF spaces across our facilities. You will work closely with our networking team and report directly to the Senior IT Infrastructure Manager.

This is a critical role for a self-starter who is comfortable owning infrastructure end-to-end: from racking servers and configuring VMware environments to managing backup operations, monitoring environmental conditions, and ensuring environmental continuity across all sites.

Key Responsibilities

Data Centers, Office and Lab Sites

Maintain and operate a medium-sized private data center, including rack management, cabling, and capacity planning

Support and manage multiple local MDF (Main Distribution Frame) and IDF (Intermediate Distribution Frame) spaces across company locations (9 in Puget Sound region)

Perform regular site walkthroughs and inspections to ensure physical infrastructure integrity

Coordinate with facilities and site management teams on space, power, and cooling requirements

Manage UPS systems and PDUs (Power Distribution Units) across the data center and MDF/IDF locations

Monitor UPS health, battery status, load levels, and runtime; coordinate battery replacements and preventive maintenance

Respond to power alarms, perform load balancing, and support planned maintenance windows involving power systems

Maintain documentation of power circuits, load maps, and single-line diagrams

Monitor data center and MDF/IDF environmental conditions including temperature, humidity, and airflow

Respond to environmental alerts and escalate to facilities or management as appropriate

Virtualization & Server Infrastructure

Deploy, configure, and maintain VMware vSphere/ESXi environments including vCenter management

Manage physical server hardware lifecycle: procurement support, racking, cabling, firmware updates, and decommissioning

Monitor host performance, resource utilization, and capacity; proactively address bottlenecks

Trouble shoot hardware failures including CPUs, memory, storage controllers, NICs, and drive arrays

Coordinate with vendors (Primarily HPE) on hardware support and RMA processes

Storage

Administer SAN, NAS, local storage solutions and cloud archives; manage volumes, datastores, and provisioning

Monitor storage health, performance, capacity and plan and execute expansions as needed

Manage storage replication and snapshots in coordination with retention policy and backup strategy

Collaboration

Partner closely with the networking team on rack and cabling coordination, infrastructure changes, and joint projects

Support change management processes including maintenance windows, change tickets, and post-implementation reviews

Contribute to IT documentation including runbooks, asset inventories, and infrastructure diagrams

Participate in on-call rotation for after-hours infrastructure emergencies

Backup & Recovery

Administer enterprise backup solutions for servers, VMs and Microsoft Office365/Azure assets

Schedule, monitor, and validate backup jobs; ensure recovery objectives (RTO/RPO) are consistently met

Perform and document restore tests on a regular cadence per policy

Required Qualifications

Bachelor of Science or Bachelor of Arts degree, or equivalent work experience

6+ years of hands-on experience in a data center or enterprise infrastructure role

Proficiency with VMware vSphere/ESXi and vCenter administration

Experience managing physical server hardware from major vendors (HPE preferred)

Working knowledge of SAN/NAS storage platforms and concepts (iSCSI, NFS, FC)

Experience administering enterprise backup solutions and validating recovery procedures

Hands-on experience with APC UPS systems or similar enterprise UPS platforms

Familiarity with PDU management and power capacity planning

Comfort working in data center environments including structured cabling, rack management, and physical hardware installation

Strong troubleshooting skills with an ability to diagnose and resolve complex hardware and infrastructure issues

Ability to lift and rack equipment up to 50 lbs. and work in physically demanding data center environment.

Ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues quickly, while leveraging IT change management processes

Preferred Qualifications

VMware VCP (vSphere) certification or equivalent experience

APC/Schneider Electric product certifications or formal training

Experience with environmental monitoring tools (Sunbird, or similar)

Exposure to DCIM (Data Center Infrastructure Management) tools

Familiarity with ITIL practices and formal change management processes

Experience in a multi-site or campus environment

Scripting or automation skills (Ansible, PowerShell, Bash) for infrastructure tasks

Professional Skills

Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users

Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail

Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

Experience partnering cross-functionally with networking, application and cloud technical teams

Customer service orientation with a problem-solving mindset

- Perform and document restore tests on a regular cadence per policy

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Project Scheduler 

TerraPower is seeking a Project Scheduler to support planning, scheduling, cost tracking, status reporting, schedule analysis, project performance reporting, and baseline change control for projects across the company. The ideal candidate will have working knowledge of Primavera P6, Microsoft Project, Excel, Jira, and Confluence.

The Project Scheduler will collaborate closely with project managers, engineers, group leads, system leads, and other stakeholders to support the assigned project area. The candidate should be able to manage multiple tasks, update and maintain schedules, integrate subcontractor inputs, follow up on action items, and support reporting of schedule trends and metrics. This role will be onsite three days per week at the Bellevue office

 

Responsibilities

Develop and maintain integrated project schedules in P6.

Update schedules regularly and support weekly progress reporting and publication of schedule layouts.

Identify and communicate variances from the plan and weekly baseline snapshots.

Evaluate schedule progress and performance and identify emerging issues.

Process project change notices into the P6 baseline and active schedule.

Support critical path analysis and assess impacts of progress or changes to key milestones.

Assist with P6 administration tasks, including maintaining EPPM data, global code fields, baseline copies, and data interfaces.

Support project review meetings and planning sessions; collaborate with project team members.

Contribute to continuous improvement of schedule controls metrics and performance indicators.

Apply EVMS principles and project management best practices while supporting project managers and group leads.

Assist in preparing monthly project status reports covering scope, schedule, budget, and risk.

Support development and maintenance of resource loaded schedules.

Provide regular schedule updates and progress reporting.

Support project specific processes and process improvement initiatives.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Business, or a related technical field preferred.

PMP, PMI SP, or AACE certification is a plus.

3–5 years of project scheduling experience, preferably in engineering, construction, R&D, or DOE related environments.

Proficiency in Primavera P6 and Microsoft Project.

Experience with MS Excel; familiarity with Power BI or other analytics tools is a plus.

Experience with Atlassian tools (Confluence, Jira) is beneficial.

Strong analytical and problem solving skills.

Effective communication and collaboration skills.

Demonstrated integrity, openness, respect, and commitment to teamwork.

Actual starting level and title will be based on assessment of qualifications

jobs.title: Licensed Exam Developer
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Nuclear Exam Writer

The Exam Developer will play a key role in designing and executing the licensing examination program for TerraPower’s FOAK fast sodium reactor (FSR). This includes all Initial License Training (ILT) program examinations and Licensed Operator Continuing Training (LOCT) examinations, ensuring full compliance with NUREG‑1021, 10 CFR Part 55, and future advanced‑reactor guidance (e.g., NEI 23‑01, NRC DRO‑ISG‑2301‑1, DANU‑ISG‑2022‑05).

A core element of this role is to develop and gain NRC approval of a customized Knowledge and Abilities (K/A) Catalog or equivalent competency framework tailored to fast sodium reactor technology, establishing the foundation for operator licensing, regulatory compliance, and long-term training program accreditation.

This individual will collaborate with internal reactor design, I&C, human factors, and training specialists—as well as external stakeholders such as the NRC—to ensure exam content validity, reliability, and traceability to plant systems and licensing requirements

Responsibilities

Obtain and maintain NATRIUM Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) Certification.

Develop and manage the Natrium licensing examination program in alignment with NUREG-1021, 10 CFR Part 55, advanced-reactor guidance (e.g., NEI 23-01, DRO-ISG-2301-1, DANU-ISG-2022-05), and all regulatory, accreditation, and QA requirements.

Design and manage all ILT program assessments using the Systematic Approach to Training (SAT), aligned with task, K/A, and KSA analyses.

Develops and administers periodic LOCT examinations—both written and simulator—to meet 10 CFR 55.59 requalification requirements.

Create and maintain reactor-specific Knowledge and Abilities (K/A) frameworks and catalogs, ensuring traceability to plant systems, procedures, design documents, and SAT-based training objectives.

Develop, update, and validate written examinations, simulator operating tests, JPMs, LOCT examinations, and exam scenarios using validated exam-development methodologies (item analysis, psychometrics, difficulty balancing, and peer review).

Maintain secure and accurate exam item banks, scenarios, and performance measures with strict confidentiality, version control, documentation standards, and auditable records.

Collaborate with simulator engineers, instructors, operators, SMEs, and licensing personnel to ensure exam content reflects Natrium design features, human-system interface characteristics, operator tasks, and operating expectations.

Coordinate with NRC examination and review staff to develop, submit, validate, and obtain approval for exam materials and K/A mappings; support audits, inspections, RFIs, and corrective actions.

Analyze exam results, candidate performance data, operating experience, and feedback to continuously improve exam quality, validity, and integration with the overall SAT-based training and qualification program.

Remain current on evolving NRC policies, advanced-reactor guidance, and industry best practices for exam development, validation, and program governance.

Key Qualifications and Skills Minimum

Previous NRC Reactor Operator (RO) or Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) license or certification (license preferred); advanced‑reactor or non‑LWR experience a plus.

Certified Nuclear Instructor (INPO) or equivalent, or the ability to obtain certification.

5 years of nuclear plant operations and/or operator training

Strong technical writing and editing skills; ability to translate complex technical concepts into fair, testable items.

High attention to detail, sound judgment, and commitment to regulatory compliance and exam integrity.

Effective collaborator with strong communication and interpersonal skills; able to manage multiple priorities and deadlines.

Eligibility for work on export-controlled projects (U.S. Citizen, National, or Permanent Resident).

Preferred

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, nuclear technology, or a related technical field preferred.

5+ years of experience developing licensing or qualification examinations in the nuclear power industry, preferably within a regulated environment.

Prior experience developing or maintaining a K/A Catalog or similar knowledge and abilities mapping for a nuclear training or licensing program.

Experience collaborating with NRC staff on exam plan development, validation, or licensing reviews.

Familiarity with fast reactor systems, sodium coolant behavior, and digital I&C architectures.

Demonstrated experience developing examinations in accordance with NUREG‑1021 and applying SAT, task, and KSA analyses.

Familiarity with proposed advanced‑reactor licensing/training guidance (e.g., NEI 23‑01, DRO‑ISG‑2301‑1, DANU‑ISG‑2022‑05); knowledge of 10 CFR Part 55 and associated NRC processes.

Demonstrate flexibility and ability to navigate and implement current and future regulator and industry guidance for license examinations to the NATRIUM reactor design while fostering effective open-communication and fostering trust with internal and external stakeholders.

Performance Expectations

ILT and LOCT exams are delivered on schedule, meet regulatory standards, and withstand NRC review.

Exam content reflects current procedures, plant modifications, and operating experience.

Operators are well‑prepared for licensing and demonstrate competence in required knowledge and skills.

Examination processes maintain full integrity and security; findings from audits and reviews are addressed promptly and effectively.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

An applicant's ability to perform and/or tolerate these actions and conditions will be discussed and w

We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Maintenance Supervisor

TerraPower is seeking a Maintenance Supervisor to support the Natrium Demonstration Project in Kemmerer, Wyoming. This role is responsible for overseeing maintenance activities during construction, commissioning, and operational readiness of the Natrium advanced reactor facility. The Maintenance Supervisor will lead maintenance personnel, ensure compliance with regulatory and procedural requirements, and support the development and execution of maintenance programs essential to safe and reliable plant operation.

This position reports to the Maintenance Execution Manager and works closely with Operations, Engineering, Work Management, Construction, and other internal stakeholders. The Maintenance Supervisor will play a key role in establishing maintenance processes, supporting system turnover from construction to operations, and reinforcing TerraPower’s nuclear safety culture.

Responsibilities

Lead and supervise maintenance technicians and contract personnel supporting the Natrium Demonstration Project.

Oversee preventive, corrective, and predictive maintenance activities for mechanical, electrical, and I&C systems as they transition from construction to operational status.

Ensure maintenance activities comply with NRC regulations, INPO guidelines, Natrium project procedures, and TerraPower quality standards.

Support system walkdowns, construction turnover, and verification of system readiness for testing, commissioning, and operation.

Coordinate maintenance planning and execution with Operations, Engineering, Work Management, Radiation Protection, and Construction teams.

Reinforce nuclear safety culture, human performance tools, and ALARA principles.

Ensure maintenance documentation, work packages, and Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) records meet regulatory and project requirements.

Support troubleshooting and resolution of equipment issues to maintain system reliability and support commissioning milestones.

Assist in developing maintenance procedures, staffing plans, and long‑term maintenance strategies.

Monitor maintenance performance metrics and implement corrective actions to improve reliability and efficiency.

Participate in the corrective action program, root cause evaluations, and event investigations.

Ensure maintenance tools, equipment, and materials are properly maintained and controlled.

Support outage planning and execution as the Natrium plant transitions to operational phases.

Key Qualifications & Skills

Minimum 6 years of maintenance experience in power generation, industrial operations, or a related field.

At least 3 years of leadership or supervisory experience.

Strong technical knowledge of mechanical, electrical, and I&C systems.

Experience working in highly regulated environments; nuclear industry experience strongly preferred.

Ability to interpret technical drawings, procedures, and regulatory requirements.

Demonstrated ability to lead multidisciplinary teams.

Strong organizational, analytical, and problem‑solving skills.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Proficiency with CMMS tools and maintenance planning processes.

Experience supporting construction‑to‑operations transitions or commissioning activities.

Experience with EVMS, project controls, or equipment reliability programs.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.title: Maintenance & Technical Training Instructor (Engineering)
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Maintenance & Technical Training Instructor 

The M&T Training Instructor (Engineering) reports directly to the M&T Training Manager and is focused on the development of M&T training programs and training materials for the Natrium Demonstration Project (NDP). In this role, you will develop the programs, processes, and procedures necessary for establishing and running M&T training. The successful candidate will integrate and coordinate with other departments, project partners, regulators, and outside organizations working on operator training.

Responsibilities

Develops Maintenance & Technical (M&T) training processes, programs, and procedures for a newly developed training department, ensuring compliance with company standards, NRC regulations, INPO accreditation criteria, and other codes, standards, and regulatory requirements.

Develops and executes a strategy for training program initial accreditation.

Uses the Systematic Approach to Training (SAT) to develop new high-quality training curriculum for maintenance and technical personnel.

Revises training programs to maintain instructional and technical accuracy based on plant design changes, modifications to plant procedures, and changes in plant processes.

Participates actively in the Training Curriculum Review Committee.

Coordinates with management, the emergency response organization, and other organizations in the development and improvement of training programs.

Prepares trainee examination material and trainee evaluations for classroom, laboratory, OJT, and simulator environments, as appropriate.

Prepares processes for training performance evaluations, trainee counseling sessions, and individualized training programs.

Incorporates information gained from INPO Significant Operating Experience Reports, NRC Circulars, Bulletins, and Information Notices, plant design changes, plant procedure changes and revisions, new regulations, and training effectiveness reports into the training program and various training material.

Participates in NRC, INPO and management audits, and prepares responses to audit findings.

Develops and maintains knowledge of plant design, plant operations, disciplinary skills and techniques, commensurate with the responsibility to develop effective, high-quality training.

Supervises the collection and maintenance of auditable training records and reports required to document training program activities.

Assists Training Management in establishing department goals, and cost estimates that result in budgeted activities for the assigned area including specific project or subject matter areas as assigned.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Minimum Qualifications (Engineering – Mechanical, Electrical, or I&C)

Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, I&C, Nuclear, or closely related discipline applicable to nuclear power plant systems) required; advanced degree preferred.

Proven ability to adapt to emergent changes driven by evolving plant design, engineering assumptions, procedures, schedules, and challenges associated with the design, construction, and commissioning of a first-of-a-kind nuclear power plant.

jobs.title: Maintenance & Technical Training Instructor (General Plant)
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

General Plant Instructor

The General Plant Instructor reports directly to the Maintenance & Technical Training Manager and supports the development, delivery, and maintenance of plant-wide training programs for the Natrium Demonstration Project (NDP).

This role serves as a cross-functional instructional resource, delivering general plant training (e.g., site access, orientation, industrial safety, and plant fundamentals), serving as a qualified NANTeL proctor, and supporting accredited training programs under the direction of qualified program owners. The position is designed to provide instructional flexibility and surge capacity during construction, commissioning, and early operations.

Responsibilities

Develops, maintains, and delivers plant-wide training programs and materials, ensuring alignment with company policies, applicable regulatory requirements, and approved training standards.

Applies the Systematic Approach to Training (SAT), commensurate with program scope, to support both accredited and non-accredited training activities.

Develops and delivers general plant training, including site access, orientation, industrial safety, and plant fundamentals.

Serves as a qualified NANTeL proctor, administering online training and examinations in accordance with NANTeL and site requirements.

Supports accredited training programs by delivering approved training content, administering examinations, supporting OJT documentation, and updating training materials under the direction of qualified lead instructors or program owners.

Coordinates with Operations, Maintenance, Security, RP, EHS, QA, and Engineering to ensure training content reflects current plant configuration, procedures, and organizational expectations.

Revises training materials to maintain instructional and technical accuracy based on plant design changes, procedure revisions, regulatory updates, and operating experience.

Prepares and administers written exams, performance evaluations, and practical demonstrations, as appropriate to assigned training activities.

Supports training effectiveness evaluations, continuous improvement activities, and corrective actions associated with assigned training programs.

Incorporates applicable INPO operating experience, NRC communications, and internal assessments into training materials and delivery, as appropriate.

Participates in NRC inspections, INPO activities, and audits in a supporting training capacity, as assigned.

Ensures the collection, maintenance, and retention of auditable training records in accordance with site and regulatory requirements.

Assists Training Management with planning, scheduling, and prioritization of training activities, particularly during construction, commissioning, and early operations.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Minimum of two (2) years of commercial nuclear power plant experience or equivalent experience in a regulated, high-hazard environment.

o Military nuclear experience of four (4) years or more may be considered.

o A combination of relevant experience and academic credentials may be considered.

Demonstrated ability to develop and deliver training and to support accredited programs under the direction of qualified program owners.

Experience or qualification as a NANTeL proctor, or ability to obtain qualification within a defined period.

Working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

Strong technical writing, communication, and presentation skills.

High level of attention to detail, flexibility, and commitment to training quality.

Demonstrated trust, integrity, and professionalism, with the ability to communicate openly and work effectively across organizations.

Understanding of how training execution and individual actions impact regulatory compliance, public confidence, and nuclear safety culture.

Ability to adapt to evolving plant conditions, changing procedures, schedule impacts, and the challenges associated with a first-of-a-kind nuclear project.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Materials Testing & Characterization Manager 

This position is focused on managing and technically supporting test programs for materials in support of TerraPower’s advanced nuclear reactor project. In this role, you will manage a dedicated team of engineers developing, executing, analyzing, and reporting on materials test programs to support reactor material selection and Natrium design and licensing. This position also supports the Manager in project scheduling and reporting activities.

Responsibilities: 
• Directly manage a diverse team of individual contributors supporting ongoing materials testing and characterization work
• Support individual contributors in developing, deploying, and executing materials testing programs in support of the Natrium reactor’s programmatic objectives
• Maintain in-laboratory presence to provide direct, immediate feedback and support to individual contributors
• Oversees and manages third-party suppliers conducting materials testing to ensure quality, compliance, and timely delivery
• Plan, monitor, and manage task progress, including defining required scope, maximizing efficiency of individual contributors, and ensuring complete and accurate work execution
• Work with the Manager and Project Manager to develop and maintain the project schedule and budget
• Effectively communicate project expectations and assignments to team members and management
• Ensure compliance to procedures, standards and regulatory criteria
• Ensure that engineering work is performed with a high level of integrity through adherence to work processes and technical reviews or approvals of team deliverables
• Communicate emerging technical issues with management and work with both the individual contributors and management to resolve efficiently
• Demonstrate visible, continuous leadership in the areas of safety and quality
• Interface with regulators, stakeholders, and customers on materials engineering design and testing issues
• Coordinate with the Plant Materials and Core Materials Qualification groups to develop material technologies supporting the Natrium Demonstration and future commercial reactor concepts
• Develop and maintain engineering staffing and resource plans, evaluating team skillsets and adjusting staffing to meet long-range needs and project priorities
• Support recruitment, onboarding, and integration of new engineering personnel, ensuring smooth assimilation into project processes and expectations
• Support the development of direct reports through mentoring, training, and challenging assignments 

Key Qualifications and Skills: 
• Bachelor’s degree with a minimum of 10 years of related experience or Master’s degree with minimum 6 years of related experience; or a PhD and with a minimum of 3 years of related experience. A degree in Materials Science/Engineering, Chemistry/Chemical Engineering, or a related technical discipline is required
• Proven ability to lead and collaborate with a diverse team is required
• Advanced knowledge and experience in nuclear quality programs, and configuration management principles, practices, and tools or similar quality programs is required
• Ability to manage numerous competing priorities concurrently during a given work week and work with minimal direction to solve multi-disciplinary problems is required
• Experience supervising personnel performing materials testing or characterization work for nuclear power applications or other related field is strongly desired
• Experience working to ASME NQA-1, 10 CFR 50 Appendix B control programs, or other NRC and DOE regulatory frameworks is strongly desired
• Candidate must be a self-starter and possess ability to perform both individually and as a team contributor; proven ability to direct small teams with minimal oversight is preferred
• Proven track record in planning, budgeting, and execution of testing or characterization activities is required
• Experience with collaboration, workflow, and planning tools, such as Atlassian Confluence/JIRA, Primavera P6, or other related tools is required
• Effective verbal and written communication skills is required
• Nuclear Industry knowledge / experience is strongly desired
• Knowledge of metallic materials testing and characterization is required
• Knowledge of metallic fuel and/or sodium fast reactor technologies is strongly desired
• Ability to work with minimum direction to solve challenging problems is required
• The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

Manager, Natrium Fuel Handling Systems

The Manager, Natrium Fuel Handling Systems role is responsible for the engineering and delivery of systems and components for Natrium Reactor Plants. This position serves as the system lead and design authority for the Water Pool Fuel Handling System and the Fuel Transport and Storage System – you will be responsible for the design and delivery of the spent fuel pool and dry cask storage equipment. The role functions as the manager of people for a multidisciplinary design engineering team of TerraPower employees and also oversees subcontracted resources and suppliers. The Manager, Natrium Fuel Handling Systems role reports to the Senior Manager of Fuel Handling Systems, and works closely with project management, adjacent engineering teams, supplier parters, and support organizations to deliver safely operating systems and equipment.

Responsibilities

Review and approve engineering design documentation

Ensure design configuration and interfaces are controlled

Ensure engineering work is performed in accordance with all quality, technical, and procedural requirements

Oversee supplier/partner/vendor technical work

jobs.title: Market Development Associate
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Market Development Associate, Business Development & Strategy

The Market Development Associate, Business Development & Strategy is responsible for monitoring State and local level power market trends as well as trends with regional and global impacts, tracking industry trends, assessing competitive landscapes, and identifying strategic growth opportunities. This role reports to the SVP of Business Development, and supports business and market development, partnership and customer decisions, and TerraPower’s market entry strategy through data-driven insights and analyses. 

Responsibilities: 

Conduct in-depth tracking and analysis of US power market trends in key states and localities of interest as well as targeted third country power market analyses.

Monitor policy and other trends to inform TerraPower nuclear deployment.

Track electricity demand trends, decarbonization targets, low carbon technology deployments with a view toward understanding Natrium’s market fit in the context of low carbon strategies.

Monitor and track energy technologies and deployments in key areas and markets of interest.

Provide insights to support new market entry strategies.

Prepare market development trend briefings and presentations for senior leadership.

Produce status reports as assigned.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

Bachelor’s degree in business, Energy, Engineering, or related field (Master’s preferred).

3-5+ years’ experience, preferably nuclear.

Strong understanding of:

US and global power markets

Low carbon technologies, including nuclear reactor technologies

Energy transition policies

Electricity market structures

Experience with energy databases and market intelligence platforms.

Excellent analytical and presentation skills.

Knowledge of low carbon technologies, nuclear power also preferred

Familiarity US Independent System Operators, Regional Transmission Operators, as well as state-level analogues.

Program Controller

This role will be responsible for overseeing financial management and reporting of Natrium Project. This role ensures that project cost, recoveries, and financial performance are accurately recorded, monitored, and reported in compliance with company policies and accounting standards. This role collaborates with project team managers, finance team, and other stakeholders to provide financial insights, control budget, and improve financial processes for project success. The ideal candidate is highly motivated and eager to collaborate, strengthening accounting operations in a fast-paced, rapid growth and data-driven environment.

Responsibilities:

Responsible for financial and accounting aspects of Natrium project, from planning to execution, which include but limited to setup of standalone project cost budget, accounting, analysis, and reporting.

Prepare technical accounting memos and guidance on long-lead procurement and constructions

Create monthly financial summary of project cost analysis and present to stakeholders.

Review draft contracts for Natrium, provide input from financial and accounting perspectives.

Assess financial risks of project and develop mitigation strategies.

Develop good working relationships with internal/external stakeholders; regularly interact with business owners and management on accounting matters.

Prepare monthly financial statements, reporting, reconciliations, related supporting schedules and analysis

Enhance existing accounting procedures and internal controls, and seek opportunities to improve accounting operational efficiency and effectiveness

Support the general accounting functions including, but not limited to accounts payable, receivable, general ledger, and fixed asset management.

Develop, document and process cost allocations among different legal entities and projects

Setup and implement cost accounting system for operations

Perform indirect cost allocation, consolidation and reporting

Prepare financial and profitability analysis as requested

Support with financial projects such as year-end close, audit, tax package, and budgets

Responsible for assisting with the annual financial audit with independent auditors

Maintain financial statement integrity and accuracy

Assist accounting system related projects to improve work efficiency and effectiveness.

Develop and maintain project cost tracking matrix to measure and monitor project financial performance.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

Bachelor’s degree in accounting with preference for CPA.

Public accounting experience required or strongly preferred.

Prior experience in external audit, internal audit, and SOX compliance is preferred.

Minimum of 12 years of progressive experience in an accounting role with strong preference for experience supporting an engineering environment.

Strong working knowledge of all aspects of accounting (Cash, Payroll, Prepaid, A/P, A/R, Fixed Assets, intercompany, consolidation).

Proficient with Excel as a business tool.

Experience with Oracle Cloud ERP system.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team, under tight deadlines at times, and in an ever changing / dynamic environment.

Candidate should possess high level of attention to detail and accuracy, and strong analytical skills.

Ability to communicate effectively and to interact professionally with all levels of an organization.

Capable of working in a fast-paced environment.

Proven ability to handle sensitive information in a confidential manner.

Excellent written and oral communication skills.

Excellent organizational skills.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trustworthiness and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.
