jobs.title: Nuclear Analysis Engineer
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Nuclear Analysis Engineer

TerraPower, LLC. Is seeking a highly motivated Nuclear Design Engineer. The Nuclear Analysis Engineer will support advanced reactor core design and analysis activities, including fuel management, power distribution, reactivity coefficients, control rod worth, shutdown margin, and sensitivity/uncertainty studies. This role will contribute to nuclear design solutions, evaluate modeling assumptions, document analysis results, and support the development of methods needed to solve complex reactor design challenges:

Perform core design and analysis activities of advanced nuclear reactors, including calculation and optimization of fuel management, power distributions, reactivity coefficients, control rod worth and shutdown margin, sensitivity and uncertainties, consistent with required functional and quality requirements

Design and analyze new systems for solving complex problems

Design and execute sensitivity studies to understand the effects and validity of various modeling assumptions, including meshing, physics coupling, transport options, cross section preparation, fission product treatment, spatial kinetics, etc., and help build corrected methods if necessary

Establish and maintain documentation of analysis results and nuclear design solutions

Work at TerraPower is dynamic and evolutionary. As such, new responsibilities often arise, and you should be capable of and excited about learning and applying specialized skills.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Experience:

B.S. degree in Nuclear Engineering with 5-20 years’ experience

M.S. degree in Nuclear Engineering with 3-15 years’ experience

Ph.D. degree in Nuclear Engineering with 2-10 years’ experience

Competitive candidates will typically have the above experience, but less experienced candidates with highly desired experience with be considered on a case-by-case basis

Knowledge of basic nuclear reactor design concepts to meaningfully support engineering team with understanding of systems being designed and code being reviewed

Experience in core design and analysis including fuel management optimization, reactivity coefficients, shielding and activation, control rod worth and uncertainty analysis.

Strong computational expertise: we like to consider computers as the universe at our fingertips rather than burdensome necessities

Experience with nuclear engineering software such as MCNP, DIF3D, MC2, PARTISN, RELAP, SCALE, PARCS, APA, CASMO/SIMULATE, DRAGON, etc.

Good working knowledge of nuclear and/or general numerical methods (e.g. PDEs, dynamics, deterministic and stochastic particle transport, isotopic depletion, perturbation theory, nuclear data, kinetics, etc.)

Experience in transient analysis, thermal hydraulics, fuel performance, etc. is desired

Fast reactor experience or experience with Core Operating Limits Report/Tech Specs is desired

Excellent verbal and communication skills

Ability and professionalism to work within published regulatory guidelines for nuclear reactor design

An ability to perform professional-quality software development for nuclear applications is preferred

Strong programming skills in any language is a plus

Experience working in NQA-1 environments desired

Maintain software by working with team to implement clearly defined enhancement requests and bug fixes

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect, and a desire to foster teamwork.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

Nuclear Licensing Engineer

TerraPower is seeking a highly motivated Nuclear Licensing Engineer to be a part of the Natrium® Licensing team. This position reports to a Licensing Manager and provides licensing support to the Natrium advanced reactor project, develops licensing documents, and supports regulatory audits and engagements. Work will be in close consultation with Engineering staff and will ensure Licensing documents are created in accordance with TerraPower licensing strategy and guidance. Support for engagements with the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) includes pre-application meetings, preparing and reviewing permit and license applications, preparing and reviewing topical reports, and responding to NRC audit questions and requests for additional information.

Responsibilities:

Draft, author, and review licensing documents for 10 CFR part 30, 40, and 70 (e.g., Safety Analysis Report and Environmental Report)

Coordinate with project stakeholders to prepare responses to NRC audit questions and requests for additional information

Interface with the Licensing Team to complete licensing tasks

Coordinate with design personnel to ensure designs and design changes meet regulatory requirements

Prepare, review, and maintain licensing documentation in compliance with regulations and strict quality assurance standards

Interface with technical discipline teams at TerraPower, partners, and contractors

Review correspondence to and from regulatory agencies

Develop licensing strategies to support project goals

Key Qualifications and Skills:

Bachelor’s degree in engineering from an accredited, four-year university or equivalent experience

Capability and willingness to adhere to strict quality assurance standards

Demonstrated, applicable experience in nuclear licensing, or comparable field

10 years plus experience with 10 CFR regulations and guidance

10 years plus experience with NRC licensing actions involving 10 CFR 30, 40, and 70 applications

Self-Starter with ability to manage deadlines

Proficient in MS Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Excel) and Adobe Professional

Strong communication skills

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Nuclear Security Systems Resident Engineer

The Nuclear Security Systems Resident Engineer will provide regular in-person oversight and support for the Nuclear Cyber and Nuclear Physical Security Systems supplier based out of Carrollton, Texas. The resident engineer will report to the Nuclear Cyber and Physical Security Systems Design Manger as part of the Natrium Plant Delivery department supporting coordination of cross functional and multi-disciplinary design activities.

The amount of time spent in Carrollton offices and TerraPower’s Bellevue offices and travel in-between will be highly dependent on the successful candidate’s geographic location and availability to travel on a regular basis. The ideal candidate will be in or relocate to Carrollton to allow continuous in-person support and collaboration between the security vendor and TerraPower teams. Candidate would travel periodically to TerraPower’s Bellevue office to meet with Natrium managers and stakeholders.

The Nuclear Security Systems Resident Engineer will coordinate scope, schedule, and technical integration for safety system, non-safety system, and security project coordination. They will also support cross functional collaboration with procurement, licensing, cyber and physical security programs organizations.

The Nuclear Cyber and Physical Security team is responsible for Natrium security systems design and the Nuclear Security Systems Resident Engineer will work with the plant design oversight teams to support communication, address roadblocks, align design and project execution strategies.

The Resident Engineer will work as part of a high performing team of engineers.

Responsibilities

Provides continuous vendor oversight of Nuclear Security design scope for the Natrium plant.

Functions as a direct and immediate conduit of communication to facilitate continued progression of design activities.

Travels to TerraPower’s Bellevue, WA office to meet with TerraPower Natrium managers and key stakeholders.

Supports TerraPower’s plant design oversight team by facilitating good communication between TerraPower and partner stakeholders and managers.

Supports TerraPower’s security vendor project management by resolving/clarifying scope and schedule issues and coordinating with the TerraPower plant design oversight team.

Supports alignment between security vendor and TerraPower on technical issues, Natrium procedures, plant design strategies, implementation of cyber/physical security plan requirements, including application of security control implementation.

Oversight of Natrium Security Systems vendor including budgeting, staffing, planning, and monitoring design development progress

Review Security System design work documents covering design, integration, and implementation of software work products and other system design documents for the Natrium nuclear plant.

Identify critical high-risk areas and initiate proactive measures to prevent potential project delay.

Provide weekly summary reports to senior management about critical task progress, potential delays, challenges, resource needs etc.

Interface and management of subcontractors, vendors and partners providing direction and assimilating their work.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Candidate should have a minimum of 12 years of design/project management experience working on large utility grade projects in the nuclear industry.

A minimum of (5) years of proven leadership experience required.

Physical or Cyber Security design development, I&C security maintenance or integrator experience is mandatory.

Familiarity with Nuclear Cyber and Physical Security related industrial codes, standards and regulations, especially 10CFR73.54, 10CFR73.55, NEI 08-09, NEI 13-10, NEI 03-01, NEI 11-08.

Experience with configuring and managing Security Incident and Event Management appliance (SIEM)

Experience with database structures for installing and integrating security systems

Excellent writing and communication skills

Ability to work independently to solve challenging problems and provide recommendations in a timely manner.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trustworthiness and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

Must have excellent verbal and written communication skills and the ability to clearly convey complex multi-disciplinary engineering and design issues and support resolution.

Experience as a design engineer working in a nuclear quality (10 CFR 50, Appendix B) environment.

Ability to travel frequently to vendor locations outside of Idaho to support in-person collaboration and oversight meetings

Ability to occasionally travel to Bellevue, WA to meet with TerraPower managers and stakeholders.

Familiarity with defensive architecture and nuclear security systems requirements design.

Candidate should have broad based technical and engineering skills with experience designing, installing or maintaining nuclear security systems.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Nuclear Shielding and Criticality Engineer

TerraPower, LLC. is seeking a highly capable and motivated nuclear shielding and criticality engineer. The position is primarily shielding related. The primary software is MCNP 6.2, and strong MCNP skills are required. The class of problems include shielding, energy deposition, neutron fluence, activation, and criticality. Strong written and oral communication skills are essential. Experience working in an NQA-1 environment with rigorous quality standards is desirable.

Responsibilities

Perform radiation shielding design and analysis for advanced nuclear reactors, including calculating dose rates, activation rates, neutron fluence rates, and energy deposition rates.

Develop radiation sources based on material activation analysis for non-fuel in-vessel components.

Perform criticality calculations and develop upper subcritical limits (USLs)

Establish and maintain formal documentation of analysis results.

Work at TerraPower is dynamic and evolutionary. As such, new responsibilities often arise, and you should be capable of and excited about learning and applying specialized skills.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Experience:

B.S. degree in Nuclear Engineering with 5-20 years’ experience

M.S. degree in Nuclear Engineering with 3-15 years’ experience

Ph.D degree in Nuclear Engineering with 2-10 years’ experience

Competitive candidates will typically have the above experience, but less experienced candidates with highly desired experience in specialized areas will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Strong MCNP skills and experience with performing radiation shielding calculations for both gammas and neutrons are required. Experience in criticality safety is desirable.

Candidate should be familiar with variance reduction using MCNP, and features such as surface source read/write, mesh tallies, and weight windows. Familiarity with ADVANTG for weight windows generation is a plus.

Excellent written and verbal and communication skills required.

Ability and professionalism to work within published regulatory guidelines required.

Experience working under NQA-1 quality program desired.

Strong computational expertise desired.

Experience with Attila /Attila4MC and computed aided drafting (e.g., SolidWorks, SpaceClaim) is a plus.

Fast reactor experience a plus.

Strong programming skills in any language is a plus, and Python is commonly used in this position.

Knowledgeable or highly motivated to quickly learn basic nuclear reactor design concepts to meaningfully support engineering team.

Acts independently to determine methods on new or special assignments. Works without specific direction from manager.

Acts as a technical subject matter expert in the area of shielding or criticality safety.

Mentors or provides technical guidance to less experienced engineers.

Creates formal networks involving coordination among groups. Exerts cross-functional influence.

Actual position starting level and title will be determined based on assessment of qualifications.

Repetitive work: Prolonged

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Operations Training Instructor

The Operations Training Instructor reports directly to the Operations Training Manager and is focused on the development of the operations training programs and training materials for the Natrium Demonstration Project (NDP). In this role, you will develop the programs, processes, and procedures necessary for establishing and running licensed operator and non-licensed operator training. The successful candidate will integrate and coordinate with other departments, project partners, regulators, and outside organizations working on operator training.

Responsibilities

Develops operator training processes, programs, and procedures for a newly developed training department, ensuring compliance with company standards, NRC regulations, INPO accreditation criteria, and other codes, standards, and regulatory requirements.

Develops and executes a strategy for training program initial accreditation.

Uses the Systematic-Approach-to-Training to develop new high-quality training curriculum for licensed and non-licensed operators.

Revises training programs to maintain instructional and technical accuracy based on plant design changes, modifications to plant procedures, and changes in plant processes.

Participates actively in the Training Curriculum Review Committee.

Coordinates with operations management, the emergency response organization, and other organizations in the develop and improvement of training programs.

Prepares trainee examination material and trainee evaluations for classroom, laboratory, OJT, and simulator environments, as appropriate.

Prepares processes for training performance evaluations, trainee counseling sessions, and individualized training programs.

Incorporates information gained from INPO Significant Operating Experience Reports, NRC Circulars, Bulletins, and Information Notices, plant design changes, plant procedure changes and revisions, new regulations, and training effectiveness reports into the training program and various training material.

Participates in NRC, INPO and management audits, and prepares responses to audit findings.

Develops and maintains knowledge of plant design, plant operations, disciplinary skills and techniques, commensurate with the responsibility to develop effective, high quality training.

Supervises the collection and maintenance of auditable training records and reports required to document training program activities.

Assists the Operations Training Manager in establishing department goals, and cost estimates that result in budgeted activities for the assigned area including specific project or subject matter areas as assigned.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Associates degree in science/engineering

4 years of commercial nuclear power plant operator training experience

2 years of experience as a simulator instructor

Previous SRO/RO license holder or 4+ Years of experience with an SRO-certification

Mastery of skills and extensive subject matter knowledge in carrying out assignments associated with the analysis, design, development, implementation, evaluation, and proposed modification of licensed operator training programs

Working knowledge of plant simulation software

Working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint

Excellent technical writing, communication, and presentation skills 

Attention to detail and aspiration for training excellence

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, able to communicate openly, and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork

Understands how individual actions and decisions can positively and negatively affect the ability of the total organization to meet its requirements to regulatory agencies and to the public

Adapts well to emergent changes due to changes in plant design, plant procedures, schedule changes, or other challenges associated with the design, construction, and commissioning of a new nuclear power plant

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

Job Functions:

Job Functions are physical actions and/or working conditions associated with the position. These functions may also constitute essential functions for the job which the employee must be able to fulfill, with or without accommodation. Information provided below is to help describe the job so that the applicant has a reasonable understanding of the job duties/expectations. An applicant's ability to perform and/or tolerate these actions and conditions will be discussed and workplace accommodations may be made on a case-by-case basis following an individualized assessment of the applicant and other considerations, including but not limited to any governing safety standards.

Motor Abilities: Sitting for extended periods, bending/stooping, grasping/gripping, fine motor control (hands)

Physical exertion and/or requirements: Minimal, with ability to safely lift up to 25 pounds.

Repetitive work: Prolonged

Special Senses: Visual and audio focused work

Work Conditions: Stairs, typing/keyboard, standard and/or sitting working environment of >8 hours/day

Travel required 0-10%

TerraPower’s technology is controlled for export by various agencies of the U.S. Government. TerraPower must evaluate applicants who are foreign nationals (other than asylees, refugees, or lawful permanent residents) in accordance with U.S. Government export control requirements. To facilitate TerraPower’s export control reviews, you will be asked as part of the application process to identify whether you are a U.S. Citizen or national, asylee, refugee, or lawful permanent resident of the United States. Government export authorization approval times vary. Based on the business needs for a particular position, TerraPower may not consider a foreign national from a country if it is impracticable to obtain timely Government export approval.

structured_description.about: TerraPower seeks an Operations Training Instructor to develop and manage operator training programs for the Natrium Demonstration Project. You'll design training curricula, procedures, and materials for licensed and non-licensed operators while ensuring compliance with NRC regulations and INPO accreditation standards.
structured_description.responsibilities:
• Develop operator training processes, programs, and procedures ensuring compliance with NRC regulations, INPO accreditation criteria, and applicable codes and standards
• Execute strategy for training program initial accreditation and maintain instructional accuracy based on plant design changes and procedure modifications
• Use Systematic-Approach-to-Training (SAT) methodology to develop high-quality training curriculum for licensed and non-licensed operators
• Participate actively in the Training Curriculum Review Committee and coordinate with operations management, emergency response organization, and external organizations
• Prepare trainee examination materials and evaluations for classroom, laboratory, OJT, and simulator environments
• Develop processes for training performance evaluations, trainee counseling sessions, and individualized training programs
• Incorporate information from INPO Significant Operating Experience Reports, NRC Circulars and Bulletins, plant design changes, and training effectiveness reports into program materials
• Participate in NRC, INPO, and management audits; prepare responses to audit findings
• Develop and maintain knowledge of plant design, operations, disciplinary skills and techniques
• Supervise collection and maintenance of auditable training records and reports documenting program activities
• Assist Operations Training Manager in establishing department goals, cost estimates, and budgeted activities
structured_description.qualifications:
• Associate's degree in science or engineering
• 4 years of commercial nuclear power plant operator training experience
• 2 years of experience as a simulator instructor
• Current SRO/RO license or 4+ years of SRO-certification experience
• Mastery of skills and extensive subject matter knowledge in analysis, design, development, implementation, and evaluation of licensed operator training programs
• Working knowledge of plant simulation software
• Working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
• Excellent technical writing, communication, and presentation skills
• Attention to detail and commitment to training excellence
• High degree of trust, integrity, and ability to communicate openly and foster teamwork
• Ability to adapt to emergent changes in plant design, procedures, schedule, and construction/commissioning challenges

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Payroll Specialist

 

Responsibilities

Support payroll administrations across multiple business segments compliance for remote workforce across multiple states outside of Washington State

Process international payroll /benefits in UK

Timely and accurately process multi-state payroll withholding, remittance of payroll taxes including federal, state, local taxes, any amendments as needed.

Understand proper taxation for employer paid benefits for multi-state payrolls

Track multi-state payroll/benefits compliance year-round (i.e. PTO, overtime), provide employee communications, help with completing year-end compliance requirements.

Review and incorporate any multi-state payroll/benefits required disclosures in the annual open enrollment materials, benefits summary, certification and contracts or accuracy, and distribute required documents for compliance

Prepare bi-weekly multi-state payroll and benefits reconciliation, quarterly W-2 review, conduct internal benefits audit to ensure data integrity

Calculate tax gross up related to relocation Calculate workers compensation and quarterly Washington State L&I owed

Assists with internal and external audits. i.e. workers compensation, insurance audit, L&I, DOL, 401K, GAAP financial and other compliance audits

Support benefits and leave administration experience including health, disability, life, and 401(k) as well as ancillary benefits

Assist annual open enrollment, new hire enrollments, qualifying life events/benefit changes

Partnering with benefits broker to support insurance claims and general troubleshooting

Support leave eligibility and calculations, partnering with our vendors, insurance carriers and benefit broker to resolve issues such as billing and claims

Perform payroll/benefits reconciliation per internal procedure and identify and resolve payroll issues timely

Assist On-boarding new employees including leading new hire orientation, explanation of benefits, policies and systems, prepare new hire packets and post on-boarding orientation follow up

Assist Off board terminating employees in ADP, complete HR termination template request, submit the insurance carrier template and notify the COBRA vendor if needed

Adhere to compliance calendar deadlines

Submit Internal Communications and updating benefit notifications/materials to TP Web

Support HRIS integration with ADP. Routine cross check of HR information in HRIS with ADP employee of records. 

Assist with employee questions/changes for direct deposit, tax withholding allowances, qualifying life events, benefit changes, etc.

Identify opportunities for process and systems improvements. Assist any ad hoc projects as needed

 

 

Key Qualifications and Skills

At a minimum, four year BA/BS degree in Accounting, Finance, or General Business

A minimum of seven years of payroll administration experience including health, disability, life, and 401(k) as well as ancillary benefits

Experienced with processing multi-state payroll

Customer service oriented in high growth, high touch organization

Accuracy, time management, and timely follow-up

Proficient with Microsoft Office suite tools and able to quickly adapt to other Finance and Accounting systems (Oracle Fusion, ADP)

Working knowledge of payroll systems, experience with ADP preferred

Highly organized and detail-oriented; high degree of accuracy

Experience completing payroll journal entries, month end payroll reconciliation and an understanding of accounting principles

Ability to multi-task. Handle payroll processing, benefits projects and ad hoc requests timely and accurately

Capable of quickly comprehending new information and technologies

Capable of working in a fast-paced environment

Strong communication skills, comfortable working with operations team/non-accounting professionals

Ability to keep payroll and Human Resource information confidential

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect with a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

 

Performance Improvement Manager

 

The Performance Improvement (PI) Manager is a full-time exempt position that reports to the Senior Manager, Organizational Effectiveness and is responsible for development, implementation, and oversight of TerraPower performance improvement programs, which includes the Corrective Action Program (CAP), Self-Assessments, Benchmarking, Operating Experience, Performance Analysis and Trending, Root Cause Analysis, key performance indicators and any other duties as assigned by the Senior Manager, Organizational Effectiveness. Continuously improve performance improvement program execution and lead the company in becoming a self-identifying and self-correcting organization. Ensure effective identification, screening, analysis, resolution, implementation and measurement of performance issues. Prepare performance data and adverse trends using observations, CAP, self-assessment, benchmarking, operating experience, observations, and performance indicator data. 

Responsibilities

Program and process owner for PI and CAP.

Oversight development, maintenance, and implementation PI and CAP standard operating documents (SOD).

Oversight development and implementation of the observation program.

Represent TerraPower on various PI and CAP industry working groups.

Oversight development and deployment of PI and CAP programs to support construction of Natrium plants.

Work with cross-functional management members to identify performance gaps and implement effective corrective actions.

Oversight development and use of key performance indicators and report out to management.

Oversight root cause investigations and effectiveness reviews.

Oversight assessments performed on all functional areas.

Oversight implementation of the operating experience program and industry reporting.

Oversight implementation of the benchmarking program.

Oversight development and implementation of PI and CAP training.

Lead Corrective Action Review Committee meetings.

 

Key Qualifications and Skills

BS, BA in applicable field (e.g., math, science, engineering, or business administration) with 12 years’ experience in a commercial nuclear work environment. AA degree with 15 years’ experience in an Engineering work environment; or equivalent combination of education and experience.

5+ years’ experience working under NQA-1 or similar requirement

Previously licensed SRO preferred.

Experience working with nuclear corrective action programs.

Experience working with nuclear performance improvement programs.

Experience working in a project environment is a plus.

Experience working with government organizations such as Department of Energy and/or the Nuclear Regulatory Commission is a plus.

Prior managerial experience preferred.

Strong customer service and training skills and a desire to achieve results.

Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.

Experience working with Confluence, Jira, PowerBI, and Devonway.

Strong oral and written communication skills.

Strong organizational and time management skills.

Ability to work independently and in a cross-functional team environment.

Excellent analytical, interpretive, and problem-solving skills.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Plant Design Integration Products Lead Engineer

Responsibilities

Collaborates with a geographically diverse team and partner organizations to develop integrated nuclear island and plant-level design deliverables with elevated focus on plant architecture, technical leadership, and cross-discipline alignment.

Leads development, explanation, and communication of plant architecture including system interactions, functional allocations, interfaces, anddesign/analysis assumptions.

Leads plant level efforts to resolve broad, highly complex technical issues that affect multiple aspects of the Natrium design.

Serves as subject matter expert in one or more areas of plant level design or architecture

Interprets, explains, and maintains plant-level design documents including design basis documents, functional requirements, and plant design specifications.

Ensures alignment of plant architecture and design documentation with the Licensing Modernization Process (LMP) including risk-informed categorization and defense-in-depth evaluations.

Develops plant-level integration deliverables such as plans, strategies, design basis documents, and plant design specifications in collaboration with engineering, licensing, safety analysis, and PRA teams.

Provides leadership and mentorship to the Plant Design Products team and supports development of integration engineering capabilities.

Facilitates technical integration reviews, configuration discussions, and architecture strategy sessions across multiple disciplines.

Key Qualifications and Skills 

Minimum 12 years of experience in nuclear facility design or operations in engineering fields such as plant or systems engineering, design engineering, operations engineering, reactor engineering, or field engineering.

BS degree in nuclear, mechanical, chemical, electrical engineering.

Demonstrated leadership in cross-functional plant design integration.

Working understanding of NRC or DOE Nuclear regulations; prior NRC interaction desirable.

Experience with Licensing Modernization Process (LMP) or similar risk-informed licensing approaches is desirable

Ability to clearly communicate complex plant architecture and design concepts.

Strong verbal and written communication skills.

Experience with quality standards such as ASME NQA-1 or ISO 9001.

Ability to work in a FOAK conceptual design environment requiring strong engineering judgment.

 This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. jobs.title: Principal Core Thermal Hydraulics Engineer
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Principal Core Thermal Hydraulics Engineer 

TerraPower is seeking a highly motivated Principal Core Thermal Hydraulic Engineer to support the sodium-cooled Natrium Reactor program. Reporting to the Manager of Core Thermal Hydraulic Methods & Analysis, you will be leading the development and validation of thermal-hydraulic methodologies, software, and analyses, providing technical guidance to ensure successful project completion and interfacing cross-disciplinary engineering teams in the company. As a principal engineer, you will address the technical challenges in refining reactor and core assembly designs and evaluation of their thermal-hydraulic behaviors under various conditions in advanced nuclear reactor systems.

Responsibilities: 

Lead the technical effort to develop and validate thermal hydraulic methodologies, software, and analysis for sodium-cooled fast reactor design

Develop, qualify, and maintain the computer code to analyze the steady-state nuclear reactor core thermal-hydraulics

Have an ownership of the qualified software for the reactor core thermal-hydraulic software

Support the technical decision to address the critical challenges in reactor core design

Support the project planning and define the work scopes for the critical path of the project

Perform thermal hydraulic analysis and engineering calculation/assessment of nuclear reactor core components and structures in exploration of reactor architecture options including component selections, configurations, and sizing in support of the design of Natrium demonstration reactor system using in-house thermal-hydraulic software and/or hand calculations

Work with cross-disciplinary teams to analyze the thermal and mechanical design considerations for the safety of the reactor and its components

Provide technical presentations on analyses performed to the broader team.

Prepare informal and formal reports to document findings and make reasoned recommendations, and report to core thermal hydraulic team manager/lead

Support licensing activities related to the Natrium demonstration project by assisting in the preparation of licensing submittals

Support design reviews as plant system and subsystem designs are established and refined

Provide Support and oversight during test execution, evaluation of test results, and reporting of test results

Establish and maintain system requirements and design basis documents for reactor core design and core thermal hydraulics

Key Qualifications and Skills 

B.S., M.S., or Ph.D in Mechanical, Nuclear, or other relevant technical Engineering area from an accredited university

A minimum of 15 years of related experience with a Bachelor’s degree; or 8 years and a Master’s degree; or a PhD with 5 years of experience with demonstrated ability to perform the thermal-hydraulic analysis of nuclear reactor core components for simulation-based design

5+ years of technical leadership and/or project management experience

Strong background in nuclear thermal-hydraulics analysis, with demonstrated expertise in evaluating and performing engineering calculations related to heat and mass transfer in nuclear reactor cores, and hydraulic characterization of reactor core components, as proven through university research, publications, and/or work experience

Proven problem-solving skills and a willingness to apply those skills to a variety of engineering problems.

Ability to handle projects that include many competing objectives and design constraints, with ability to resolve conflicts between objectives

Demonstrated expertise and experience with python programming and software development, qualification, and verification & validation. Proficiency in C++ programming is highly preferred

Experience working with high-performance computing (HPC) environments preferred

Experience in Software Quality Assurance (SQA) program and Nuclear Quality Assurance (NQA-1) standards highly preferred

Strong knowledge and experience in liquid metal thermal-hydraulics or experience with nuclear reactor analysis codes (subchannel analysis code or system thermal-hydraulic codes)

Must have strong verbal and written communication skills to meet NQA-1 requirements, including the formal documentation of calculations.

Actual position starting level and title will be determined based on assessment of qualifications

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.title: Principal Safety Methods Engineer
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Principal Safety Methods Engineer

This role supports the development, enhancement, and deployment of plant‑scale thermal‑hydraulic analysis methods and models for the Natrium reactor. Reporting to the Manager, Plant Thermal Hydraulics Methods, the principal engineer supports the creation, improvement and qualification of advanced system-level modeling approaches that enable accurate, robust prediction of plant behavior across operational and transient scenarios.

The position requires expertise in system‑level thermal‑hydraulics, thermal‑fluids behavior, computational methods, and scaling analysis supporting benchmarking computational models against experimental data.

Responsibilities

Support verification, validation, and uncertainty quantification activities for plant scale thermal-hydraulic analysis tools.

Develop and execute code assessment plans, including validation matrices, test selection, and quantitative comparison methodologies.

Perform scaling analysis to evaluate the applicability of test data to reference system conditions, including assessment of similarity criteria and scaling distortion.

Interpret and apply experimental data from separate effects and integral test facilities to support model validation and closure of technical gaps.

Define validation needs, develop test requirements, and collaborate closely with test engineering teams to ensure alignment between model gaps and experimental programs.

Document V&V methodologies, model assumptions, validation results, and uncertainty analyses for internal design processes and regulatory submittals.

Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to support plant design, safety analysis, and licensing workflows.

Apply and improve TerraPower’s SQA and NQA1 modeling quality practices and ensure traceability and configuration control of analysis inputs, models, and results.

Mentor junior engineers in system modeling, validation methodologies, and best practices in code assessment.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Degree in Mechanical, Nuclear, Chemical Engineering, or similar field, with 8 plus years in the field of system‑level thermal‑hydraulic analysis and code assessment for nuclear or complex energy systems.

Demonstrated experience in code assessment, including model-to-test comparison, validation metric development, and uncertainty quantification.

Hands‑on experience working with experimental thermal‑hydraulic data, including data analysis, uncertainty evaluation, and comparison against system‑level predictions.

Proficiency with system analysis tools (e.g., RELAP5‑3D, TRACE, SAS, or equivalent) applied to plant‑scale transient and steady-state evaluations.

Strong foundation in thermal-fluid and reactor system behavior considerations relevant to advanced reactor designs.

Experience performing scaling analysis to ensure appropriate translation test-scale phenomena, including application of similarity criteria and detailed scaling distortion impact qualifications.

Experience developing validation strategies, defining data needs, and coordinating with experimental programs.

Experience with SQA and NQA‑1 quality assurance practices for modeling workflows is highly preferred.

Strong analytical thinking, clear technical communication, and the ability to manage multiple validation tasks in parallel.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

jobs.title: Project Controls Engineer (NI/EI Civil Schedule)
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Principal Project Controls Engineer

The Principal Project Controls Engineer will have lead NI/EI civil schedule responsibility for delivering project performance tracking and analysis, schedule, data validation and project change control for the Natrium project. This individual will work closely with various project stakeholders including TerraPower management, engineering, and other functional group management as well as with subcontracted/subrecipient teams and/or individuals. Additionally, will support the planning and schedule functions of a project. This project controls position will lead the implementation of systems, applications and procedures for key project control processes thereby ensuring management’s ability to monitor and control successfully project outcomes and performance.

Responsibilities

Responsible for monitoring, reporting and review trending of all NI and EI Civil related construction schedule issues.

Utilize EVMS principles and other project management best practices to support the project team in the execution of defined scope and deliverables.

Support month-end Earned Value reporting process including assessing of BCWS, BCWP, ACWP, SPI, CPI, EAC, BAC, and VAC for assigned tasks with variance reports to be written for any values that exceed established thresholds.

Gather, analyze, verify schedule performance data, and monitor project work in place costs including procurement, subcontractor spend and accruals and provide such information to TerraPower Finance.

Lead change control process through development of Project Change Notices (PCNs) for changes affecting baseline budget, scope or schedule impacts which exceed established thresholds and process these changes into the P6 baseline and live schedule monthly.

Support internal and external stakeholders by the creation of ad-hoc reports, analysis, and forecasting as required.

Evaluate project schedule progress and performance and identify developing problem areas and communicate these issues through Power BI dashboards and other collaborative modeling tools.

Support the facilitation of project review meetings, and project planning as assigned and actively interact with project team members as the primary project controls point of contact.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or equivalent technical field preferred.

PMP or AACE certificate is highly preferred.

jobs.title: Project Manager, Equipment Delivery Management
jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Project Manager, Equipment Delivery Management

The Project Manager, Equipment Delivery Management (EDM) serves as the primary interface between the project organization and Supply Chain for assigned critical equipment packages. Reporting to the Senior Manager of Equipment Delivery Management, this role is responsible for monitoring and holding Supply Chain accountable to project milestones, managing cost account performance through EVMS, driving change control and risk identification, and ensuring that all equipment delivery activities for assigned packages are progressing in alignment with project requirements. The Project Manager is expected to identify problems early, drive resolution, and escalate when needed to protect project outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Supply Chain Interface & Oversight

Serve as the primary day-to-day liaison between the project organization and Supply Chain for assigned equipment packages; monitor Supply Chain’s development and execution of delivery plans against project milestones.

Hold Supply Chain accountable to agreed schedules, milestone dates, and reporting cadences; formally request recovery plans when deficiencies are identified and monitor execution until performance is restored.

Ensure Supply Chain is developing and maintaining detailed schedules consistent with project-level milestones in the Integrated Master Control Schedule (IMCS).

Facilitate cross-functional alignment meetings with EPC, Operations, and Engineering on behalf of EDM; flag and drive engagement when alignment gaps exist.

Coordinate planning of project integration activities related to Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Equipment Qualification (EQ), commissioning, and system turnover.

Escalate unresolved Supply Chain performance issues to leadership with supporting documentation.

Cost Account Management & EVMS

Hold full cost account management (CAM) responsibility for assigned equipment packages, including budget ownership, cost control, and EV baseline integrity in compliance with EVMS requirements.

Establish, maintain, and defend the Performance Measurement Baseline (PMB) for assigned cost accounts; authorize and control allocation of budget to Supply Chain.

Review supplier monthly reports and validate earned value inputs provided by Supply Chain; challenge unsupported progress claims and ensure EV is grounded in physical completion.

Calculate and analyze EV metrics on the defined reporting cycle; identify cost and schedule performance trends and distinguish transient anomalies from systemic variances requiring corrective action.

Develop and maintain the Estimate at Completion (EAC) for each assigned cost account using EV performance data, risk-adjusted projections, and remaining work assessments; update on the defined reporting cycle with written justification for any revision.

Change Control & SCR Management

Serve as the primary initiator of change control for assigned cost accounts; identify, document, and route all required scope, cost, and schedule changes through the approved PCN process.

Lead project impact assessments for project impacting Supplier Coordination Requests (SCRs) identifying deviation, non-conformance, or cost impact; review forecast variances submitted by Supply Chain and prepare recommendations for leadership approval.

Ensure all approved changes are reflected in the IMCS in a timely manner; coordinate with PMO controls to maintain baseline integrity.

Risk Management

Identify, document, and track project risks arising from equipment delivery activities, cost performance, and schedule status for assigned packages.

Escalate risks that increase overall project risk exposure following project risk management procedures.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, project management, supply chain, or a related technical discipline.

10+ years of experience in project management, supply chain oversight, or equipment delivery on major capital programs, with demonstrated cost account management or EVMS experience.

Experience on nuclear, energy, defense, or heavy industrial capital projects strongly preferred.

Working knowledge of earned value management systems (EVMS), EV metric calculation, EAC development, and variance analysis.

Solid understanding of supply chain processes, contract administration, SCR review, and procurement change control.

Familiarity with integrated master scheduling and milestone governance across complex, multi-year programs.

Strong analytical skills; able to identify performance trends, assess project impact, and prepare clear, defensible documentation for leadership review.

Effective communicator and collaborator; able to manage relationships with Supply Chain, Engineering, EPC, Operations, and PMO counterparts and drive alignment across organizational boundaries.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect, and demonstrate a genuine commitment to team accountability and project success.

This position is subject to qualification requirements in accordance with ANSI/ANS 3.1. Candidates must meet the minimum education and experience criteria applicable to the role, including relevant nuclear industry experience where required. Verification of data will be part of the hiring process.

Project Manager

TerraPower is seeking a Project Manager responsible for managing activities related to TerraPower’s scope of the Natrium Demonstration project including planning, execution and closure. This position will report directly to the Sr. Project Manager and will coordinate with all areas of the Natrium Demonstration project, as well as other TerraPower internal organizations. The Project Manager will manage project conflicts to ensure on-time and on-budget delivery of products and services and is the first point of contact for any project issues or discrepancies.

Responsibilities

• Manage project cost, schedule, and scope execution using project management best practices and principles, ensuring adherence to plan and addressing deviations.
• Drive and hold project teams accountable for achieving project deliverables and staying within budget while still meeting scope and technical performance requirements.
• Ensure the technical adequacy and quality of work products to validate scope completion.
• Lead/drive the planning process for the work scope within the control accounts. Work with the relevant engineering leads to obtain the necessary planning inputs. Serve as the primary point of contact for interfacing with project controls in the implementation of plans in the integrated master control schedule (IMCS).
• Develop and maintain staffing/resource plans to accomplish the work and adjust the schedule to be resource leveled. 
• Monitor and analyze cost and schedule variances using the earned value (EV) data recorded compared to the time-phased budget. Determine root causes and develop corrective actions / recovery plans / variance management plans as necessary to avoid large variances.
• Work closely with the Contract Administrator to ensure that the work performed is in compliance with all contractual requirements.
• Ensure that records on the project are stored in accordance with corporate procedures. 
• Maintain the project risk register and ensure that actions are taken to reduce project risk. 
• Work closely with Project Management Office (PMO) support, to ensure that work performed follows the PMO requirements.

• Minimum of 10 years of experience directly related to Project Management or Project Delivery, with demonstrated experience on large projects.
• Proven experience leading multi-million-dollar projects, Nuclear industry preferred.

• Experience with test engineering and/or mechanical design is preferred

• Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills required.

jobs.description:
TerraPower is a nuclear technology company based in Bellevue, Washington. At its core, the company is working to raise living standards globally through a more affordable, secure, and environmentally friendly form of nuclear energy along with innovations in medical isotopes to improve human health. In 2006, TerraPower originated with Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries who evaluated the fundamental challenges to raising living standards around the world. They recognized energy access was crucial to the health and economic well-being of communities and decided that the private sector needed to take action and create energy sources that would advance global energy deployment. TerraPower’s mission is to be a world leader in new nuclear technologies, while developing innovators and future leaders in the nuclear field. As a result, the company’s activities in the fields of nuclear energy and related sciences are yielding significant innovations in the safety and economics of nuclear power, hybrid energy and medical applications – all for significant human health benefits.”

TerraPower is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced nuclear reactor research and development and influencing change within the nuclear power landscape and bringing forward the critical production of medical isotopes. TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. 

Quality Assurance Engineer

This role is remote or onsite in Everett, WA with travel up to 50% within the continental U.S.

The QA Engineer reports directly to the Quality Assurance & Continuous Improvement Manager. This position is responsible for performing Quality Assurance activities for TerraPower’s advanced reactor programs. The ideal candidate will have QA experience in NRC 10 CFR 50, Appendix B, 10 CFR Part 21, ASME NQA-1-2015, and Regulatory Guide (RG) 1.28, Revision 5. The ideal candidate should be an NQA-1 Certified Lead Auditor (ATL) and have extensive experience with evaluation and qualification of NQA-1 commercial supply chain suppliers. The ideal candidate will also have extensive experience applying a graded approach to quality in environments ranging from conceptual design using collaborative environments to safety related design and construction supporting PSAR requirements and construction permits. Experience with industry codes, standards and guides such as ASME, AWS, AISC, ASTM, EPRI, NEI, ISO, NEC and IEEE are strongly desired.

 

Responsibilities 

Review customer and regulatory contracts and develop baseline requirements matrices/documents.

Interface with customer and supplier QA representatives to assist with resolution of quality, technical, and complex compliance issues.

Review internal and external engineering documents including design drawings, specifications, test plans/procedures, etc., to ensure compliance with governing procedures and appropriate flow down of applicable QA requirements.

Plan and perform (as a Certified Audit Team Leader (ATL)) supplier qualification audits and surveillances of suppliers and partnering companies.

Perform annual evaluations and triennial audits of approved suppliers on the TerraPower Evaluated Suppliers List (ESL).

Coordinate and host Customer and Regulator audits, surveillances, and visits.

Develop, revise, and/or review QA processes and procedures.

Plan and perform (as a certified ATL) internal Audits.

Participate in Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA), Continuous Improvement, Lessons Learned/Operational Experience, and Risk Management processes.

Review quality affecting procedures (engineering, procurement, document control, records, testing, etc.) to ensure flow-down of applicable QA requirements.

Review procurement specifications, purchase requisitions, purchase orders, contracts, task orders, statement of work, and quality program requirements documents to ensure flow-down of applicable quality assurance requirements.

Perform Internal audits of TerraPower QA policy, implementing procedures, and documentation.

Perform mentoring and training on various NRC quality assurance issues and licensing.

Key Qualifications and Skills

Undergraduate degree or equivalent experience in a technical discipline, business, management, or quality assurance field.

8 - 10 years’ experience in a nuclear (NRC, DOE) Quality Assurance function regulated environment by the USNRC.

Current certification as NQA-1 Lead Auditor or an NRC vendor inspector other industry recognized certification or the ability to be certified within one year of hire.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly and display respect and a desire to foster teamwork.

jobs.description:
The TerraPower Isotopes® (TPI™) division has been established to pursue medical isotopes development--advancing nuclear science for significant human health benefits. We seek to support revolutionary radiotherapies for treatments of various cancers through the supply of radioisotopes. TerraPower currently has unique access to a supply of thorium for the long-term production of actinium-225 in the growing alpha therapy market and intends to expand into other nuclear related medical treatments.

TPI is seeking to hire highly motivated and forward-thinking professionals who are interested in focusing on advanced radiopharmaceutical applications and influencing change within nuclear medicine. Quality Assurance Specialist - TPI 

TerraPower Isotopes is seeking an experienced Quality Assurance Specialist to support Ac-225 production at the Everett, WA manufacturing facility. 

The successful candidate will have experience in a quality assurance role with focus on ensuring compliance with Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), regulatory guidelines (FDA Title 21 CFR 210/211 and ICH Guidelines), radiation safety, and company policies. The Quality Assurance Specialist will support the quality assurance activities including batch and material disposition, deviations, CAPA, change controls, authoring procedures and policies, supplier qualification and performance monitoring, audits, and system implementation. The position requires excellent judgement and discretion in balancing compliance and business resolutions. The Quality Specialist will report to the Manager, Quality Assurance and work closely with a multidisciplinary team including Operations, Quality Control, Engineering, R&D, Procurement, Project Management, and Commercial Services teams to achieve company objectives. 

 

Responsibilities: 

Review batch records, Certificates of Analysis, and documentation for accuracy and completion.

Author, revise, and manage approval of site policies and procedures.

Review and approval of quality documentation, including deviations, change controls and CAPA, and complaints.

Support supplier qualification program, including verifying supplier compliance with applicable quality requirements, focusing on critical materials, services, and supplier performance.

Provide Quality support for ongoing production activities at Everett Laboratory.

Support internal and external audits by preparing documentation, analyzing findings, and implementing corrective actions.

Support project workstreams and continuous improvement project driven by Quality.

Support implementation of Quality Management Systems.

Support development processes for validation/qualification of equipment, facility, and/or manufacturing processes. 

Ensure data integrity compliance across quality systems, including adherence to ALCOA+ principles and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.

Balance quality requirements with safety, such as radiation safety, in all quality activities.

Provide comprehensive, Quality-related training to colleagues within TPI and act as a resource for quality related inquiries

Perform other general duties associated with the position as required by supervision.

 

Key Qualifications and Skills 

Bachelor’s degree in life sciences discipline, engineering, or a related field (or equivalent qualification through experience).

6+ years’ industry experience in a regulated production environment (e.g., ISO 9001, cGMP pharmaceutical or radiopharmaceutical industries), including 3+ years of prior experience in a Quality Assurance role.

Certification in a pharmaceutical Quality-related discipline, Lean Six Sigma or project management training/certification is a plus.

Demonstrated experience working within Quality Systems.

Strong working knowledge of quality principles, with proven success in managing complex projects and a wide range of responsibilities.

Experience working within a quality program compliant to FDA/cGMP requirements Title 21 CFR 210/211 and ICH Guidance. Experience in a production radiopharmaceutical facility is preferred.

Strong attention to detail, with the ability to interpret federal regulations, guidance and standards. Ability to incorporate relevant requirements into TPI documentation and provide guidance to personnel regarding compliance.

Must demonstrate flexibility in adjusting to changing priorities and schedules in a dynamic environment. Occasional evening or weekend work might be required to support production operations or project deliverables.

Strong interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive accountability.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including technical writing proficiency and executive-level reporting abilities.

The successful candidate will possess a high degree of trust and integrity, communicate openly, and display respect while fostering teamwork.
